violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacked metal containers present on the clean dish rack above the 3-comp sink in the rear of the facility. Ensure that all pans properly air dry prior to stacking together. 

-REPEAT-

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed food debris and residue present in the bottom of the stand up RIC adjacent to the expo area. Observed hot holding equipment with food debris and residue present. Hot holding equipment was not in use at the time of the inspection.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed doors of the dumpster in the dumpster area of the facility open. Ensure that dumpster doors remain closed when not in use.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed the ceiling vent above the 3-comp sink coming off of the ceiling. Ensure that the ceiling vent is repaired so that it is properly affixed to the ceiling.

violations.comments:
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P- Observed shredded lettuce that was prepared at 5:21 AM this morning per the sticker present on the lettuce. EHS took a temperature of the lettuce at 9:26 AM and the lettuce was 43F-44F. Lettuce was cooling in a lowboy in a tightly wrapped container. Ensure that ambient prepared foods cool to 41F within 4 hours. -CDI- lettuce was voluntarily discarded. 





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed ambient prepared lettuce miss the cooling window. The lettuce was cooling in a wrapped plastic container in the lowboy. Ensure that the facility uses proper cooling methods such as venting containers and using larger equipment such as their WIC to cool. -CDI- Lettuce that missed the window was voluntarily discarded. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed several flies present in the facility throughout the inspection. Facility must increase pest control methods to ensure that flies presence in the kitchen are minimized. EHS observed no flies present on food or food contact surfaces. EHS will make a return visit to ensure that pest control measures are increased and pests are no longer present in the kitchen. 
PIC stated that pest control is currently performed once monthly by ECOLAB at the facility.

rows.general_comments:
Overall improvement noted and facility has done a lot of cleaning and organizing.

Facility had labeled shelving in cold and dry storage for la chilanguita and la chonitas (MFUs).

violations.comments: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P- Observed PIC handle bleach to fill up sanitizer bucket and then begin to return to food prep. -CDI- EHS intervened, educated PIC on when to wash and PIC washed hands. -0-

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing -Pf.- Observed food debris in handsink, indicating it was used for dumping. -CDI- EHS educated PIC on proper use of handsink and handsink was properly cleaned. -0-

3-304.11 (A) Food shall only contact surfaces of properly cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils. -P- Observed washed celery and cucumber lying on storage racks of bottom of prep unit. -CDI- EHS educated PIC on proper storage and items rewashed and placed in proper containers. 
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P.- Observed raw chicken outside of original packaging stored over raw whole muscle beef in WIF. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. -1.5-
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P- The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed some items past date mark: 6/23-6/29: repollo, yuca, chicken taco filling, carne molida, lengua, andcanned chipotle peppers; some items missing date mark: cut cabbage, half and half, and pico de gallo; and some items improperly date marked: beans 6/29-7/6 (discard should be 7/5) and salsas in small RIC at FOH that were filled from larger containers in WIC date marked for today instead of original date mark. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. OVERALL IMPROVEMENT NOTED WITH DATE MARKING. CONTINUE TO MONITOR TO ENSURE ITEMS ARE PROPERLY DATE MARKED AND DISCARDED PAST 7 DAYS. **REPEAT** -3-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. -Pf- Observed raw beef thawing under running water at 85F and not submerged. -CDI- EHS educated PIC on proper thawing methods and that in EHS’ experience, water does not come out of faucets below 70F in Meck county in the warmer months and PIC placed items in WIC. -0-

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used. Observed three stacks of single use plates and to go containers being stored outside of their original packaging right side up on prep shelf in back prep area and above window at cook station. CDI: PIC inverted items to protect against contamination.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. OBSERVED one of the RI coolers in disrepair at the back. Also observed ice machine is in disrepair. Repeat

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed cleaning needed on shelves in the cooking area where single use items stored. Repear

rows.general_comments: - To request a reinspection, please contact the main office at 980-314-1620.

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P - Observed the PIC touch lo mein noodles with their barehands to check the temperature of the product while it was cooling down.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded all contaminated lo mein noodles.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed a large container of raw shrimp to be stored directly on top of containers of ginger on the speed rack in the WIC. REPEAT.
- Observed a container of raw shrimp to be stored on a shelf in the WIC directly above the large bus bin of lo mein noodles that were actively cooling. REPEAT.
- Observed a container of raw shrimp to be stored on the top shelf of the cookline lowboy refrigerator directly above opened packages of fries and dumplings containing precooked pork and chicken.
- Observed a container of raw beef to be stored on the top shelf of the cookline lowboy refrigerator directly above a container of lo mein noodles.

CDI - Storage order rearranged in the WIC, on the WIC speed rack, and in the cookline lowboy so that all RTE items are stored above all raw products.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P - Observed the dishwasher to be testing at 0ppm of chlorine at the time of the inspection. The chemical sanitizer bucket was found to be empty and in need of replacing.

CDI - PIC changed sanitizer bucket. After priming and a couple cycles the dishwasher was observed dispensing a proper 150ppm chlorine solution.

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. - Observed a large bus bin of cooked lo mein noodles to have missed the first cooling window while the EHS was on site. Cooked lo mein noodles were observed with a 6/9 date mark and a time stamp of 11:30am which was confirmed by the PIC and cookline employees to be when they had started to cool down the noodles. At 1:20pm the noodles were still observed at temperatures of 76F in the middle with a 5 degrees change over the course of the previous hour.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded bus bin of lo mein noodles.

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P - Observed a fryer basket full of sesame chicken to be at temperatures of 101-110F at the time of the inspection. Cookline employee stated that they fully cook the chicken and then hold it in the baskets until an order comes in at which point they would drop the chicken a second time.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded basket of chicken.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed insert containers of cooked baby corn and bean sprouts to be at temperatures of 60-65F in the cookline opentop refrigerator. PIC stated both items were placed up into the opentop at around 11am when the facility opened. Cookline employee stated that stated that the lid to the unit was opened for the entirety of their lunch rush, which had just ended as the EHS arrived. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC moved both items to the WIC to rapidly cool down.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed a large container of cooked shrimp in the WIC to not have a date mark of any kind. PIC stated that it was cooked yesterday afternoon.

CDI - PIC date marked item.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. - Observed no written procedures on site for the sushi rice that is held using TPHC. Item was observed properly time stamped and facility has had the document in the past but could not find it today.
- Observed no written procedures on site for the items at the wok line that are held on TPHC. As per the PIC the egg wash on the wok cart, the raw chicken, raw beef, raw shrimp, and cooked shrimp in the cookline opentop are all held using TPHC and are discarded if they last the 4 hours. All items were observed time stamped with an 11:30am time stamp except the egg wash, which was brought out of refrigeration at 11am when the facility opened. REPEAT.

CDI - Written procedures filled out for both the sushi rice and the wok line items at the end of the inspection. Egg wash time stamped for discard at 3pm

TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed a large bus bin of lo mein noodles to be cooling at a rate of .083 degrees/min and to miss the first cooling window with the noodles at 76F at the 2 hour mark. Bin was observed to be deeply filled, densely packed with noodles, and had plastic wrap covering the top of the container which all do not allow for adequate cold air flow around the product to facilitate proper cooling. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded due to it not reaching 70F within the first 2 hours of cooling.

- Observed approx 10-12 containers of smoked crab and a tray of kani to be observed at temperatures of 44-47F in the sushi counter lowboy refrigerator. As per the sushi employee all items were prepared that morning and immediately placed into the sushi lowboys to be used throughout the day. Date marks on the containers supported this statement. Containers of smoked crab were observed with tight fitting lids on and the tray of kani was observed with tight plastic wrap over the top of it, not allowing for adequate cold air flow around the product to facilitate proper cooling. Lowboy refrigerators are also not designed nor approved for rapid cooling and all cooling must be done in the WIC. REPEAT.
- Observed a container of cooked beef to be at a temperature of 94F in the cookline lowboy refrigerator and was observed to be actively cooling down per the cookline employee. Employee stated having cooked the item within the last hour before placing it in the lowboy, which is not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.
- Observed a container of lo mein noodles to be at a temperature of 66F in the cookline lowboy refrigerator and was observed to be actively cooling down per the cookline employee. Lo mein noodles were from the same bulk batch that was observed actively cooling in the WIC. Employees stated they needed noodles up at the cookline so they had taken from the batch still cooling and placed them in the cookline lowboy to use during lunch, which is not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.

CDI - PIC moved all items to the WIC to rapidly cool. Lids and plastic wrap removed from items.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. - Observed all secondary containers used on the wok line rolling cart for spices, seasonings, and sauces to not be labeled as to their contents.
- Observed the bulk bins of sugar, flour, MSG, and rice in the dry storage area to not be labeled as to their contents.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. - Observed the back door to the kitchen to not be fully closed, allowing for the potential entry of vermin into the facility. REPEAT.

CDI - Door closed.

- Observed a gap at the base of the back door when closed, allowing for the potential entry of vermin into the facility. REPEAT.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. - Observed multiple stacks of plastic to go containers and lids at the cookline to be stored with their food contact surfaces face up, allowing for the potential contamination of the food contact surfaces.

4-502.13 Single-use and single-service articles cannot be reused. - Observed plastic single service containers to be in use as bulk bin scoops throughout the facility.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. - Observed a cutting board to have large chunks missing, heavy scarring, and heavy staining which rendered it no longer easily cleanable.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-501.18 Maintain wash, rinse, and sanitize solutions clean. - Observed the wash basin of the 3 compartment sink to be filled and active warewashing to be going on at the time of the inspection and the wash water was observed to be heavily contaminated with food debris/grime. All dishes go through the chemical dishwasher after being hand scrapped/washed.

CDI - PIC changed solution and EHS discussed with staff the proper times to change the wash water.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the racks used in the dishwasher to have heavy accumulations of black grime. REPEAT.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. - Observed a bin containing employee food and ingredients for employee meals such as raw pork to be stored on the middle shelf of the WIC, directly above boxes of raw vegetables to be used for consumers. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC relocated employee bin to lower shelf of the WIC.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-101.11 (A) PIC shall be present during all hours of operation. -PF Observed no PIC until mare then arrive half way threw inspection. PIC was unable to answer questions. 
2-102.11 PIC shall demonstrate knowledge of food borne disease prevention, application of the HACCP principles and the requirements of this code.-Pf. Observed PIC and establishment unable to demonstrate cold holding, cooling, cooling methods, food separation, insects control, maintaining facility clean. EHS will return in 10 days to verify training and cleanness of facility. 





Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed food employee filling up basin with water from hand sink. CDI - PIC to food employee to grab water from prep sink. 
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf Observed no hand soap at hand sink. Soap added.

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed. raw meat, cooked meat, and TCS vegetables stored without covering. CDI - coverings were placed on containers. 


3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw stake stored above RTE items in RIF. PSIC rearrange storage order to prevent contamination.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed raw chorizo, stored above fish. PIC rearrange storage order to prevent prevent contamination. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed heavy food debris and build up on utensils and equipment stored as clean. All items moved to 3 comp for proper washing. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed 4 salsa containers made from night before above 41 cooling in WIC missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed beef stock cooling from before in walk-in cooler above 41 missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P observed all items in prep unit cold holding above 41. PIC stated they will call maintenance to come out and look at unit. Establishment shall not use prep until unit is cold holding 41 and below. Establishment will work out out of reach in cooler until fixed. Ambient temp of unit is 48



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed salsa cooling in large deep plastic containers with tape, fitting lids and stacked. Observed beef stock, cool a large container. Both items missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed several flies throughout preparation area and kitchen. PIC stated pest control comes out 1 times every 3 months. Increase pest control.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed queso stored on floor in walk-in cooler observed tortillas stored on floor in dry storage. CDI - PIC picked up off floor to prevent contamination.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed in use swiping cloth held in bleach sanitizer at 200ppm. CDI - PIC remade bucket at correct concentration.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed avocados in prep unit with stickers still on. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed heavy food, buildup, and debris on cooking equipment prep units, and RIC throughout the facility.
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed hand sink with huge water leak spilling on floor when hand sink is in use. Repair

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.110 Keep waste stored in receptacles so waste is inaccessible to insects and rodents. Observed trash, empty boxes, food, beer, mattress, out side back door of facility. Back of establishment needs to be cleaned and all items need to be put in trash can for avoid access to insects.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed a buildup of residue and debris on the floors and walls throughout the facility. Floors throughout kitchen and under units with heavy accumulation of debris, walls by the cook line and preperation aria by WIC in the back of the facility contained food debris and splatter. Increase cleaning frequency and thoroughness of floors walls and ceilings.

rows.violation_text:
1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-101.11 (A) PIC shall be present during all hours of operation. -PF Observed no PIC until mare then arrive half way threw inspection. PIC was unable to answer questions. 
2-102.11 PIC shall demonstrate knowledge of food borne disease prevention, application of the HACCP principles and the requirements of this code.-Pf. Observed PIC and establishment unable to demonstrate cold holding, cooling, cooling methods, food separation, insects control, maintaining facility clean. EHS will return in 10 days to verify training and cleanness of facility. 





Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed food employee filling up basin with water from hand sink. CDI - PIC to food employee to grab water from prep sink. 
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf Observed no hand soap at hand sink. Soap added.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed. raw meat, cooked meat, and TCS vegetables stored without covering. CDI - coverings were placed on containers. 


3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw stake stored above RTE items in RIF. PSIC rearrange storage order to prevent contamination.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed raw chorizo, stored above fish. PIC rearrange storage order to prevent prevent contamination. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed heavy food debris and build up on utensils and equipment stored as clean. All items moved to 3 comp for proper washing. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

20 - Proper cooling time & temperatures: 3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed 4 salsa containers made from night before above 41 cooling in WIC missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed beef stock cooling from before in walk-in cooler above 41 missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P observed all items in prep unit cold holding above 41. PIC stated they will call maintenance to come out and look at unit. Establishment shall not use prep until unit is cold holding 41 and below. Establishment will work out out of reach in cooler until fixed. Ambient temp of unit is 48



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed salsa cooling in large deep plastic containers with tape, fitting lids and stacked. Observed beef stock, cool a large container. Both items missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

Observed queso stored on floor in walk-in cooler observed tortillas stored on floor in dry storage. CDI - PIC picked up off floor to prevent contamination.

42 - Washing fruits & vegetables: 3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed avocados in prep unit with stickers still on. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed heavy food, buildup, and debris on cooking equipment prep units, and RIC throughout the facility.

Observed trash, empty boxes, food, beer, mattress, out side back door of facility. Back of establishment needs to be cleaned and all items need to be put in trash can for avoid access to insects.

Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed a buildup of residue and debris on the floors and walls throughout the facility. Floors throughout kitchen and under units with heavy accumulation of debris, walls by the cook line and preperation aria by WIC in the back of the facility contained food debris and splatter. rows.general_comments: NOTE: Observed sandwiches in to go case holding above 41F. Unit was in defrost. Sandwiches cooled to 42F within 20 minutes.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed pizzas (5 boxes), wings (4 boxes), 12 burgers/ chicken sandwiches holding below 135F. CDI- Burgers, chicken sandwiches and wings reheated 165F-205F. Pizzas discarded. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed ham, hotdogs and cheddarwurst in walk in cooler without dates. CDI- Items dated with day opened. Observed commercial chicken salad expired per manufacturer's date (6/6/2026 and 6/27/2026). Observed chicken salad sandwiches in to go area dated 7/4/26. Sandwiches were made with expired chicken salad. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

REPEAT

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
Observed owner preparing food while wearing a watch.

violations.comments:
-REPEAT-
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P- The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). 
Observed sliced salami outside of original packing stored in reach-in cooler without date of 5/26. Per PIC, item was opened, portioned and placed in freezer on that date. Salami was removed from the freezer yesterday to thaw for use today. CDI - PIC allowed to date/re-date package of salami, because date product was removed from freezer was recorded on dry-erase placard on prep unit lid. In the future, the PIC may be required to discard food, per 3-501.18 (A), that is not dated or inappropriately dated in accordance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C)
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments:
3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf
Observed various shellstock tags from the previous 90 days out of chronological order during inspection.
CDI - Tags corrected during inspection.
Repeat. 1 pt.

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P 
Observed sanitizer at three compartment sink dispensing below 200ppm quat during inspection. Maintenance contacted. Facility to utilize dish machine until repair.
72 hour VR required.
1.5 pts.
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed various items in WIC holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart.
CDI - All items voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (A) Provide consumer advisory for animal foods served raw or under-cooked. Consumer advisory must include disclosure and reminder.-Pf
Observed specials menu void of consumer advisory during inspection. Specials menu included items such as halibut ceviche, salmon tartare, and filet mignon.
CDI - Employees added asterisks to items to lead customers to consumer advisory in dinner menu. Specials menu is always tucked into dinner menu when given to customers.
0 pts.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
4-301.11 Provide equipment in number and capacity so that cooling, heating, and holding temperatures are achieved. -Pf
Observed WIC over capacity during inspection. Excess storage of foods blocked condenser fan, prohibiting it from being able to circulate cold air properly, leading to improper cold holding temperatures. Ambient air on bottom two shelves against the back wall and under the condenser observed maintaining 43/44F, despite the condenser pushing out air at 40F. Facility has one small WIC, two 2 door RIFs, one 1 door RIC, and three prep units. REHS observed at least 14 items that are cooled from hot in facility/on the menu. PIC stated they receive two deliveries per week. REHS advised PIC to increase deliveries to three times per week, utilize RIFs for cooling, and to rearrange WIC to allow for better air flow and to prioritize TCS foods being held in areas of WIC that maintain proper temperatures.
10 day VR required.
0.5 pts.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor.
Observed various sheet pans of food stacked on top of each other with no barrier between the food and the bottom of the pan atop it. Ensure all food is properly protected from contamination at all times.
0 pts.

EHS observed concentration at 0ppm after multiple runs and primes. PIC contacted repair technician. PIC stated facility will switch to single service items for dining room guests for the time being. EHS stated facility can serve foods on trays with parchment paper. PIC stated repair technician should be onsite today. EHS will conduct 3-day VR to ensure long term compliance. (1.5)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed diced tomatoes actively cooling in WIC in container, tightly covered in plastic wrap. EHS observed only 1 degree change after 45 minutes. EHS advised to remove plastic wrap and spread tomatoes out evenly to quickly cool. CDI - PIC removed plastic wrap and stirred container. EHS verified adequate cooling rate prior to end of inspection. (0.5)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. EHS observed numerous containers of food on make line without proper labels. EHS advised that all working containers of food shall have a label identifying what is in the container. REPEAT. (1)

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. EHS observed a few flies in back of kitchen near dish machine. (0)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. EHS observed interior door and walls of WIC corroding. PIC stated facility is waiting to plan a day where a refrigerated truck can be obtained to conduct repairs without affecting business. REPEAT. (1)

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf 
Observed employees not knowledgeable regarding handwashing process or following sushi prep procedures. CDI by instruction to ensure proper food safety management system is in place and followed. 



A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed no certified PIC on site upon arrival. PIC later arrived but expired in 2025. 



For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Repeat.

2-301.14 (F) Wash hands during food preparation, as often as necessary to remove soil and contamination, and to prevent cross contamination when changing tasks. -P - Observed dishwasher handle soiled equipment then proceed to handle clean equipment at ware washing area. Observed another employee washing gloves off at vegetable prep sink. 
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P - Observed employee turn faucet off with hands after handwashing. CDI, employees rehashed hands. Repeat.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed cut cabbage with prep date of 6/17 and coooked chicken prepared on Saturday according to staff not date marked. CDI, foods voluntarily discarded. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P - Observed employee using sushi rice leftover from day before according to staff. CDI, sushi rice voluntarily discarded. Ensure TPHC procedures for sushi rice are being followed.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed salmon stored in container on ground in WIF.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed a couple wet wiping cloths on counter surface.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed sushi rice utensil stored in water at 88 degrees F.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed some containers wet stacked.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed rusty shelves on racks in walk in cooler. Observed water collecting in container in walk in cooler. Repeat.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed shelving in need of cleaning throughout walk in cooler and gaskets.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed some damage to floor in walk in cooler. Repeat.

violations.comments:
7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. -P
Observed a spray bottle of bathroom cleaner stored above paper towels and toilet paper in the restroom. CDI - Spray bottle was moved to the bottom shelf.

REPEAT
3-602.11 (B) (1)-(4)Food packaged on premises for customer self service shall include a label with the (1) common name of the food; (2) ingredients and sub ingredients in descending order; (3) quantity of contents; (4) name and place of business.
Observed “Overnight Oats” in a refrigerator for self-service with only product name and ingredients labeled. Label is still missing amount and address / location. No allergens are present.

REPEAT
2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils.
Observed employees preparing coffee while wearing watches and no hair restraint

violations.comments:
4-703.11(B) After being cleaned in a mechanical dish machine, equipment food-contact surfaces and utensils shall be sanitized in the dish machine by achieving a utensil surface temperature of 160F as measured by a max/min thermometer.-P. Observed dish machine unable to reach 160F. PIC stated manufacturer instructed establishment to calibrate via area behind front panel below door. Establishment does not use multiuse eating equipment or utensils, but uses dish machine for bar glasses. CDI- Establishment to complete wash and rinse step with dish machine and sanitize with quat solution. EHS will follow up with establishment. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following weblink: Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.comments:
2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. violations.comments:
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Spinach danishes (6-17), garlic and cheese danishes (6-17), and chicken and beef pastries (no date) were either not labeled with prep date or were held past 7 days from preparation. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days of preparation. CDI - PIC discarded danishes and placed prep dates on chicken and beef pastries.


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.17 (A) Wood and wood wicker may not be used as a food contact surface. 
Observed cedar planks being used during the cooking process of the facilities Cedar Plank Salmon. PIC stated that Cedar Planks are single use and discarded. EHS explained to PIC and facility that the use or cedar planks for cooking, as a food contact surface is a process that requires prior approval from the state. Wood can only be used as a food contact surface if it is hard maple or an equivalently hard, close grained wood, and is used as a cutting surface, for confectionery operations as a paddle for scraping kettles, or as shipping containers for whole, uncut, raw fruits and vegetables and nuts in the shell. REPEAT -.5-
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed leak at pre-rinse at 3-comp sink, at drain under 3-comp sink, at hostess station ice bin. Repair. -0-

5-403.12 Liquid waste shall be disposed of in accordance with LAW. Observed condensate from smoker, rainwater near dumpster and drainage from roof through aluminum gutters near parking lot side entrance creating standing water pest harborages in parking lot. Also observed filter soaking barrel full of rainwater. Dispose of rainwater and other liquid waste in accordance perk law to prevent pest harborage. -0-

Observed water damaged ceiling in women’s restroom. Observed flood light broken over women’s restroom stall to the far right. Repair and ensure leak has been repaired.
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed buildup on heat lamps attached to ceiling. Clean often enough to prevent buildup. -0-

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
10 DAY VR.
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf 
Observed overall need for PIC to train staff on ware-washing processes. Staff not aware of which test strips should be used with which sanitizer, how to operate chemical dish machine, set up 3 comp sink properly, or able to identify sanitizer apart from soap. PIC must urgently retrain staff in proper ware-washing. A return visit will be made in 10 days to reassess staff and PIC on duty understanding of proper ware-washing. (-1)

REPEAT. 10 DAY VR.
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed many dishes on rack above 3 comp sink stored as clean with visible grease residue/food debris, and black residue inside ice machine.
All dishes brought to ware-wash for re-w/r/s. A return visit will be made within 10p days to reassess ware-wash processes and ice machine. (-1.5)

4-702.11 Utensils and food contact surfaces of equipment shall be sanitized before use, after cleaning.-P 
Observed no sanitizer at 3 comp sink dispenser, or sanitizer buckets set up for use with wiping cloths on prep surfaces throughout kitchen. Staff identify dawn dish soap as sanitizer when asked what is used for the sanitize step. 
CDI- EHS provided education on sanitizers and 3 comp sink ware-washing process. Quat sanitizer available at mop sink dispenser observed at proper concentration.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed two large containers of salsa in small lowboy at kitchen entrance which failed to reach 41F or below during overnight cooling.
CDI- Salsa’s voluntarily disposed of. (-1.5)

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed ranch dressing and whipped cream in lowboy at 50F.
CDI- PIC voluntarily disposed. (-0)

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed sliced lettuce, diced tomatoes, and seafood medley in cook line fliptop without date marks, which was prepped yesterday. Also observed multiple date marking procedures in use; some TCS items dated with prep date, other with expiration date. EHS advises the PIC to ensure all staff are using the same date marking procedure, with ready to eat foods prepped and kept onsite longer than 24 hours all dated for no more than 7 days, with prep date as day 1. 
CDI- EHS verified prep date with chef and allowed dating of items. (-1.5)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed tall containers of salsa cooling in lowboy, and stacked containers of portioned salsa cooling in fliptop above. Large containers failed to cool within 6 hours. EHS advises staff to cool foods in less dense, uncovered containers, which are not stacked; this allows entry of cold air. 
CDI- Salsa’s which failed to cool voluntarily disposed, portioned salsa relocated to WIC for rapid cooling, cooling methods discussed with staff. (-0.5)

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observed a live beetle in rinse compartment of 3 comp sink and several house flies and fruit flies in back of main kitchen. EHS verified regualr pest control services are provided. EHS advises increasing visit frequency and monitoring carefully. No contamination to food observed. (-0)

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-303.12(B)(C)(D) Unpackaged food may not be stored in direct contact with undrained ice except: whole raw fruits or vegetables; cut raw vegetables such as celery, carrots, or potatoes; tofu; and raw poultry and raw fish received immersed in ice.
Observed containers of shredded lettuce in undrained water at several locations. EHS discusses with PIC and other staff need to store TCS produce without undrained ice or water. (-0)

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed many wiping cloths in use throughout establishment and no sani-buckets made up for their storage.
CDI- PIC sets up sani-buckets with quat. (-0.5)

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue.
Observed utensils in 115F water at cook line. 
CDI- Water voluntarily poured out, utensils moved to ware-wash. EHS explains that utensils in warm water must be held hot at 135F or above. (-0.5)

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used.
Observed many stacks of opened single service trays throughout kitchen removed from protective sleeve. EHS advises staff to keep single service articles in packaging or otherwise protected while awaiting use. (-0.5)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance.
Observed all racks inside WIC severely rusted, no longer cleanable. Replace corroded racks. (-0.5)

4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed lowboy upon entrance to kitchen with ambient temp in 50’s, not able to hold TCS foods. 
CDI- All cooling items relocated, PIC advised to call for work order and not store TCS foods inside until repaired and able to hold food at 41F and below.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-501.18 Maintain wash, rinse, and sanitize solutions clean.
Observed rinse basin at 3 comp sink soiled and with inspect in water.
CDI- Sink drained, basin sanitized. (-0)

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Facility has employee health policy in place but no documentation that employees have been trained on policy. CDI- gave PIC updated employee health policy with signature page and informed all employees to read and sign policy. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events
violations.comments:
2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf 
Facility did not have written procedures available. PIC stated that policy had been posted but is no longer on wall. CDI- gave additional copy of vomit and diarrhea clean up plan and encourage PIC to keep it in the office. 


For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw burgers stored over pre-cooked crab cakes in reach freezer. CDI- items rearranged. Points not escalated due to improvement from previous inspection. 

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed multiple food items in reach in coolers and walk in cooler not in cooling process uncovered. CDI - products covered.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed corned beef in walk in cooler 45-47F that was cooked last night. All other products in walk in cooler were observed at 37F indicating that product did not fully cool. CDI - corned beef discarded. 



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed cooked turkey, hot dogs, roast beef, ham, cooked chicken and cooked pork in prep units without dates. CDI- PIC was able to accurately identify when products were prepared and allowed to date. Observed employee portioning wings and labeling with today’s date but wings were cooked yesterday. CDI- wings dated with yesterday’s date. Observed Caesar dressing and salsa with dates exceeding 7 days. CDI- both items discarded. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf. Observed spray bottle of bleach unlabeled. CDI- PIC labeled bottle 
7-204.11 Provide sanitizer at correct concentrations, diluting as required.-P. Observed sanitizer in red bucket exceeding 200pm. CDI- sanitizer diluted to proper concentration.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed corned beef in walk in cooler in large pieces and stacked together not allowing product to cool and meet cooling parameters. CDI- corned beef voluntarily discarded. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.115 Live animals may not be allowed on the premises of a food establishment unless otherwise allowed in the NC Food Code per 6-501.115 (B) and (C ). -Pf. Observed front of the house employee behind bar upon arrival with a dog. CDI- informed employee that only service animals are allowed in the building and also not allowed behind bar. Employee took dog outside.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-303.12(B)(C)(D) Unpackaged food may not be stored in direct contact with undrained ice except: whole raw fruits or vegetables; cut raw vegetables such as celery, carrots, or potatoes; tofu; and raw poultry and raw fish received immersed in ice. Observed hard boiled eggs stored in water in prep units on line.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed severely dented colander and several pans.
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed many cutting boards heavily scored and stained.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed general cleaning needed of surfaces throughout including interior of refrigerators on line, bottom shelf of reach in freezer, shelving units under cook line, sides and exterior of equipment.

5-202.14 Prevent backflow or back siphonage prevention devices installed shall meet American Society of Sanitary Engineering standards. -P. Observed back flow preventer not rated for continuous pressure with hose and spray arm attached and water turned on. CDI- water turned off and nozzle removed.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed general cleaning needed of walls behind cooking equipment and around dish machine. Floors in need of cleaning under equipment and along walls.

violations.comments:
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Salsa measured 49F in walk-in cooler. PIC stated it was cooked and cooled last night. CDI - PIC discarded salsa. Use ice bath with frequenty stirring, or uncovered, shallowed pans to cool large batches of TCS foods after preparation. 0 pts.

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Green salsa (110F) and queso (95F) were below 135F in hot holding. TCS foods in hot holding shall measure at least 135F. CDI - PIC reheated items to above 165F. 0 pts.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Pupusas in walk-in cooler measured 43-45F. Raw shrimp and raw tilapia in low boy cooler measured 54F and 50F. Pickled onions, raw shrimp, pico, and raw shrimp measured 45-50F in small sandwich cooler in between grills. PIC stated they got a truck 30 minutes ago from Sysco and the cooler door was propped open for 20 minutes while they put the food away. This caused the pupusas to warm above 41F. PIC also stated they were cleaning the bottoms of the both the other coolers this morning which probably caused the food to warm up. CDI - PIC moved items to walk-in freezer to cool quickly. REPEAT 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Salsa was cooled overnight in large container and it still measured 49F today. Use shallow pans that are uncovered, or ice bath with frequent stirring to cool TCS foods after cooking or preparation. CDI - PIC educated on cooling methods and he discarded the salsa.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Some containers and bus tubs stored as clean were stacked up while they were still wet. Allow utensils to adequately air dry. 0 pts.

21 - Proper hot holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Green salsa (110F) and queso (95F) were below 135F in hot holding. TCS foods in hot holding shall measure at least 135F. CDI - PIC reheated items to above 165F. 0 pts.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Pupusas in walk-in cooler measured 43-45F. Raw shrimp and raw tilapia in low boy cooler measured 54F and 50F. Pickled onions, raw shrimp, pico, and raw shrimp measured 45-50F in small sandwich cooler in between grills. PIC stated they got a truck 30 minutes ago from Sysco and the cooler door was propped open for 20 minutes while they put the food away. This caused the pupusas to warm above 41F. PIC also stated they were cleaning the bottoms of the both the other coolers this morning which probably caused the food to warm up. CDI - PIC moved items to walk-in freezer to cool quickly. REPEAT 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Salsa was cooled overnight in large container and it still measured 49F today. Use shallow pans that are uncovered, or ice bath with frequent stirring to cool TCS foods after cooking or preparation. CDI - PIC educated on cooling methods and he discarded the salsa.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Some containers and bus tubs stored as clean were stacked up while they were still wet. Allow utensils to adequately air dry. 0 pts.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P Observed container of adulterated strawberries in WIC. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded container of adulterated strawberries.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
REPEAT
6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed dead insects throughout kitchen and dry storage area. CDI - PIC removed dead insects
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed live insects on floors near steam table area and dry storage room. CDI - PIC removed insects. PIC was able to show EHS scheduled monthly pest control services. PIC should continue to increase detailed cleaning of kitchen and dry storage area.

3-304.12 (E) In-use utensils, such as ice scoops or utensils for non-TCS food, must be stored in a clean and protected location. Observed one ice scoop stored on rusted metal tray under soda dispensers and second ice scoop stored between ice machine lid and outer housing. CDI-PIC removed items and placed them in 3-comp-sink.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
REPEAT
4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed several wooden pestles, stirring paddles, and wooden muddlers flaking apart to touch along with chip and cracks stored clean under prep table. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded items. 

4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed all metal wired storage racks inside WIC with rust build up. Replace or repair.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments:
REPEAT
5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed green dumpster with both lids missing.
5-501.110 Keep waste stored in receptacles so waste is inaccessible to insects and rodents. Observed litter and trash overflowing around surrounding dumpster area. Increase frequency of cleaning.

REPEAT
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed food debris and build up around cooking and cold holding equipment. Increase frequency of detailed cleaning of kitchen area along with dry storage room. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed floor tiles in front of WIF damaged and missing exposed bare concrete. Repair flooring.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no certified food protection manager on site during inspection. REPEAT*** (1 pt) 





For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf.
Observed rolling cart stored in front of the hand sink closest to the door. -CDI- PIC removed the cart without any correction from the EHS. REPEAT*** 

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed a drainage pipe leading directly into the hand sink. REPEAT*** 

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf
Observed no paper towels at either of the hand sinks in the kitchen. PIC stated that there were no paper towels in the hotel. -CDI- PIC provided napkins to dry hands with. REPEAT*** (2 pts)

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed multiple TCS food items without date marks or have exceeded the 7 day expiration. -CDI- PIC provided correct date marks and voluntarily discarded the necessary items. (1.5 pts)

Observed accumulation of grime on the gaskets on the cold holding units throughout the facility. Increase cleaning facility. (0 pts)

5-403.12 Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids and rainwater shall be drained from point of discharge to disposal
according to law.
Observed a condensate drainage pipe from an air conditioning unit draining directly into the hand sink closest to the entrance
door. Facility if to go through plumbing code enforcement to have the condensate pipe drain in an approved location. REPEAT*** (2 pts)
