violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed facility has a health policy in place as there is a blank one posted on the employee board. Through discussion PIC was able to describe portions of the policy. However, unable to see signed copies/proof of training and attestation during inspection as they were in the GM’s office who was not on site. CDI: Facility employees trained today and confirmed they understand when they should not report to work. In the future ensure verification of training/attestation can be made during inspections. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf Observed every paper towel dispenser in the kitchen not functioning/jammed and no towels available for drying hands at any handsink. CDI: Papertowels provided on top of towel dispensers.

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed blueberries stored over ready to eat cupcakes/cakes in reach in. REPEAT VIOLATION 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed cheese in prep cooler above 41F. Prep cooler air temp was 49F. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days to ensure cooler has been repaired. REPEAT VIOLATION 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed items date marked with discard date of 7/7. PIC indicated that all items were prepared yesterday. 7/7 would be a hold time of 9 days. CDI: Dates corrected to indicate discard of 7/5.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed violations with hand washing, hot holding, cold holding and employees in need of training on food safety concepts. CDI- EHS educated mangers and employees. 

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed cup without a lid stored in the walk in cooler above and with customer foods. Keep in designated area.

2-301.14 Wash hands before engaging in food preparation including working with exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and unwrapped single service and single use articles. -P
2-301.14 (E) Wash hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. -P
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P Observed no employees washing hands throughout the entire inspection unless prompted by EHS. Observed employee handle soiled dishes with gloves, remove gloves, then don more gloves to handle. CDI- Gloves removed and hands washed. Observed employee handle soiled dishes, remove gloves, then go to don more gloves to to handle clean dishes. CDI- hands washed

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed all handwashing sinks in all kitchens blocked or with items stored in them. CDI- Boxes, drink dispenser parts, towels and cleaning supplies removed from all sinks upstairs and downstairs. 
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf Observed no paper towels at hand washing sinks upstairs. CDI- Paper towels stocked at sinks. 
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf Observed no soap at hand sink. CDI- Soap restocked.

3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P Observed employee handle dishes with gloved hands, then go to handle clean dishes with same gloves. (Different time than hand wash incidents. Employee washed dishes several times).

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed over 10 utensils soiled with old food debris and grease. CDI- All items taken to be cleaned. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed potatoes holding below 135F in hot well. EHS RECOMMENDS PLACING ALL BUFFET ITEMS ON TPHC. TPHC REQUIRES TIME STAMP AND PROCEDURES. CDI- Discarded. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed cheese, chia pudding, overnight oats, salsa, salsa verde and hummus holding above 41F on the buffet line. Items are kept in cold bowls. Bowls do not maintain temp throughout the breakfast time frame. Items should be held on TPHC. CDI- All items discarded. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed ham, salami, hotdogs and almond milk without date in upstairs kitchen. CDI- Items discarded. Observed container cut chicken 6/29, container chicken breast 6/29 and salsa bottle 5/18 expired in the downstairs kitchen 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-602.11(C) Bulk food available for consumer self-dispensing shall be prominently labeled in plain view with the manufacturer label provided with the food, or another method of identification that includes the information in 3-602.11 (B)(1), (2), and (6). Observed cereals without proper identification labels for customer view. CDI- Manager brought book out from the back kitchen.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed food stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Keep food off of the floor. 
3-306.11 Protect food on display using shields, packaging, or other effective means. -P Observed apples on display unwashed and unwrapped. Facility does not have sign to wash apples. CDI- Apples taken back to kitchen, washed and wrapped.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee keys and medication stored with customer items in the kitchen area.

rows.violation_text:
1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed violations with hand washing, hot holding, cold holding and employees in need of training on food safety concepts. CDI- EHS educated mangers and employees. 

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

6 - Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed cup without a lid stored in the walk in cooler above and with customer foods. Keep in designated area.

8 - Hands clean & properly washed: 2-301.14 Wash hands before engaging in food preparation including working with exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and unwrapped single service and single use articles. -P
2-301.14 (E) Wash hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. -P
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P Observed no employees washing hands throughout the entire inspection unless prompted by EHS. Observed employee handle soiled dishes with gloves, remove gloves, then don more gloves to handle. CDI- Gloves removed and hands washed. Observed employee handle soiled dishes, remove gloves, then go to don more gloves to to handle clean dishes. CDI- hands washed

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed all handwashing sinks in all kitchens blocked or with items stored in them. CDI- Boxes, drink dispenser parts, towels and cleaning supplies removed from all sinks upstairs and downstairs. 
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf Observed no paper towels at hand washing sinks upstairs. CDI- Paper towels stocked at sinks. 
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf Observed no soap at hand sink. CDI- Soap restocked.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P Observed employee handle dishes with gloved hands, then go to handle clean dishes with same gloves. (Different time than hand wash incidents. Employee washed dishes several times).

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed over 10 utensils soiled with old food debris and grease. CDI- All items taken to be cleaned.

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P EHS observed food employee preparing RTE food into portioning bowl to hand to stir-fry cook. EHS observed food employee grabbing cooked noodles with bare hand. EHS intervened and instructed employee to stop, discard food, and wash hands. CDI - Food employee voluntarily discarded noodles, washed hands, and donned gloves prior to continuing working with RTE food. (2)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P EHS observed dish machine unable to properly dispense sanitizer at correct concentration. PIC ran a few full cycles and even primed unit. PIC contacted repair technician to schedule repairs and has switched to to-go orders only. Only single service items shall be used until unit can be repaired. EHS will conduct 3-day VR to observe compliance. (1.5)

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed cut cabbage, cooked noodles, and raw eggs above 41F during inspection. EHS observed two containers of cut cabbage in WIC 2 (WIC closest to door) above 41F. This is the only TCS item facility stores in unit. EHS observed ambient temperature of unit at 42-43F. This unit never cooled below 41F during inspection. EHS observed cooked noodles and raw eggs in WIC 1 (middle WIC) above 41F. EHS observed ambient temperature at beginning of inspection at 44F, by end of inspection middle WIC was observed at 40F. Majority of TCS foods in middle WIC observed right at 41F during inspection. All other TCS items were observed actively cooling from being prepared at 3:00pm. PIC voluntarily discarded cooked noodles observed above 41F, raw eggs were placed into ice bath to quickly cool. PIC removed items cooling into WIF to finish cooling within time parameters. EHS observed ambient temperature of kitchen at 96F. PIC unable to verify if temperatures were taken today. PIC will contact repair technician to have both WIC assessed to ensure units can hold TCS foods at 41F during service. EHS will return in 3-days to assess units and compliance with cold holding. If temperatures issues are observed, facility could be placed on an immediate suspension or voluntarily close to repair units prior to operating. WIC 2 will only be used to store non-TCS foods until assessed. (1.5)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P EHS observed food employee placing cooked chicken on make line on TPHC. EHS observed this is the same fried chicken that was actively cooling in WIC. EHS asked food employee what temperature the chicken was and food employee verified temperature. EHS observed temperature of chicken at 48F. EHS provided education on TPHC. CDI - PIC placed chicken in WIF to quickly cool and stated chicken will be cooked to order until cooling has been completed. (0)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed fried chicken, noodles, and cooked shrimp in WIC 1 cooling in large container covered with plastic wrap. EHS observed inadequate cooling rates during inspection. PIC stated typically items are cooled in smaller containers in WIF. CDI - PIC removed plastic wrap and stretched containers into smaller containers; placed all items cooling into WIF to quickly cool. EHS observed items below 41F prior to end of inspection. REPEAT. (1)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. EHS observed numerous containers of sauce, seasonings, salts, and sugars in kitchen unlabeled. EHS advised to label all working containers of food so that they can be identified. (0)

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. EHS observed dead pests by front hand sink in kitchen. CDI - PIC removed dead pests. 


6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. EHS observed a few flies in back of kitchen during inspection. (0)

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. EHS observed stacks of pans stored as clean in kitchen still wet with water present on interior surface of container. EHS advised tor stagger pans to allow for adequate air drying prior to stacking for storage. REPEAT. (0.5)

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. EHS observed gaskets on make line and shelving in WIC’s soiled with food residue and debris. REPEAT. (1)

6925 UNIVERSITY CITY BV SUITE 430
 
CHARLOTTE, NC 28262
rows.general_comments: EHS emailed PIC updated employee health policy documents today. Facility was able to verify computer based training but indicated they may use the paper form in addition for ease during inspections.

violations.comments:
4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code, P. Per PIC, cutting boards are wiped down with sanitize every 2 hours and washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the night. Sandwiches directly touch the cutting boards and therefore must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every 4 hours. CDI: PIC will implement every 4 hour W,R,S going forward. 




For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
violations.comments: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed meatballs reheating in steam table. PIC stated they start them in the microwave but that the target is at least 140F but not over 160F. The container of meatballs was being reheated for the second time and therefore must hit 165F. After 40 minutes, the meatballs had risen approximately 30 degrees and still had approximately 30 degrees left to go with 20 minutes left. CDI: Item continued to be reheated in the microwave to over 165F in the microwave. Meatballs going up to commercial reheat were also placed in the microwave due to them tracking to not make it to the required temperature as well. EHS recommended stirring throughout reheating processes. See temp chart.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed 3 fruit flies in area with clean dish storage. REPEAT VIOLATION PIC stated they have a contract with a pest control company that comes monthly. EHS advised increasing pest control frequency due to flying insect presence.
2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed one employee with no hair restraint preparing food.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed non functioning ice machine and water station at consumer self-serve area. Repair, remove, or replace. REPEAT VIOLATION Observed torn gaskets on several reach ins. Observed main prep unit damaged which prevents pans from being placed in unit properly and staff have a towel to prop up pans so they do not fall through. Repair or replace damaged equipment. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving at clean dish drying rack and RIC, prep units, and RIF starting to rust. REPEAT VIOLATION

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed all holding and main use equipment in need of detail cleaning including but not limited to prep units, reach in units, gaskets of units and conveyor toaster. REPEAT VIOLATION Ensure more frequent cleaning.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments:
5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed dumpsters with open lids and doors. 

*NOTE: this is a shared dumpster space*

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed walls in need of cleaning throughout facility including on the cook line, and in the back of house. Many areas have significant food spatter and build up. Observed AC vent covers with dust build up. Observed soup cup and lid holder with significant build up and debris. Observed shelving in cabinet under drink area in need of cleaning. REPEAT VIOLATION 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed shelving in cabinet at drink area in severe disrepair.

40 - Personal cleanliness: 2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed one employee with no hair restraint preparing food.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed non functioning ice machine and water station at consumer self-serve area. Repair, remove, or replace. REPEAT VIOLATION Observed torn gaskets on several reach ins. Observed main prep unit damaged which prevents pans from being placed in unit properly and staff have a towel to prop up pans so they do not fall through. Repair or replace damaged equipment. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving at clean dish drying rack and RIC, prep units, and RIF starting to rust. REPEAT VIOLATION

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed all holding and main use equipment in need of detail cleaning including but not limited to prep units, reach in units, gaskets of units and conveyor toaster. REPEAT VIOLATION Ensure more frequent cleaning.

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed dumpsters with open lids and doors. 

*NOTE: this is a shared dumpster space*

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed walls in need of cleaning throughout facility including on the cook line, and in the back of house. Many areas have significant food spatter and build up. Observed AC vent covers with dust build up. Observed soup cup and lid holder with significant build up and debris. Observed shelving in cabinet under drink area in need of cleaning. REPEAT VIOLATION 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed shelving in cabinet at drink area in severe disrepair.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed portioned bags of lamb and pasta over filled in a prep top in pre portioned bags. PIC stated that they went into the prep unit approximately 45mins prior to the inspection. All other foods in the unit were 41F or below. -CDI- PIC moved the foods into the WIC to cool. Observed a garlic and oil mixture at 74F on the grill top. EHS coached PIC that the garlic and oil mixture must be on temperature control or TPHC. PIC stated that they will determine whether or not they will switch to garlic powder or do TPHC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded. 

EHS will email a copy of the TPHC sheet. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 

Observed Chow Chow and garlic and oil sauce past 7 day date marking window. -CDI- Foods were voluntarily discarded. Ensure all TCS foods requiring date marking are properly discarded when past 7 day date window. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. -Pf- Observed packaged lobster thawing in a container of water that was not running. Water was 84F. Ensure that proper thawing methods are used. -CDI- Lobster was placed into a metal container and put into the WIC to finish thaw process.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed small fruit flies present in the rear of the facility by the mop sink. Ensure that pest control addresses this portion of the facility.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed scoops handles present in the semolina and present in the flour. Ensure that scoops are stored so that the handles are not resting atop food. -CDI- PIC adjusted scoop handles.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed several wet stacked metal containers. Ensure that dishes are properly air dried prior to stacking together.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed a small leak in the air vent on the ceiling above expo. Vent was not dripping on food or food contact surfaces ensure the leak is repaired.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed no CFPM present at time of inspection. REPEAT VIOLATION

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed no signed/confirmation of employee health policy training on site. PIC was able to answer questions regarding information about the employee health policy but indicated they do not have a policy she has signed. CDI: Policy signed during inspection today. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. Observed no written procedures for items held on TPHC. CDI: Procedures filled out during inspection. 



TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-602.11(C) Bulk food available for consumer self-dispensing shall be prominently labeled in plain view with the manufacturer label provided with the food, or another method of identification that includes the information in 3-602.11 (B)(1), (2), and (6). Observed no common name or ingredients information as required for self dispensed cereals. CDI: Common names provided. Facility still needs to meet the rest of the rule. 
3-602.11 (B) (1)-(4)Food packaged on premises for customer self service shall include a label with the (1) common name of the food; (2) ingredients and sub ingredients in descending order; (3) quantity of contents; (4) name and place of business. Observed individually wrapped bagels and english muffins with no labeling. CDI: Bagels and English muffins unwrapped. 
3-602.11 (B) (5) Food packaged on premises for customer self service shall include a label with the major food allergens contained in the food -Pf. Observed individually wrapped bagels and english muffins with no labeling. CDI: Bagels unwrapped.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-306.11 Protect food on display using shields, packaging, or other effective means. -P Observed unprotected food items including grit/oatmeal toppings, cheese and salsa, and grits. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days on or before 7/4 to ensure food is adequately protected. Facility may use physical monitoring, self closing containers or other physical barriers such as sneeze guards.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used. Observed overstacked cups and cups without protection for coffee, milk, and water.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments: 4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf Observed quat sanitizer test strips and expired since 2022 and physically damaged. Test strips were not accurately testing sanitizer. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days on or before 7/10 to confirm test strips are on site.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. violations.comments: 5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed hand sink to right of cook line and hand sink behind sushi prep line with no running water. PIC stated a pipe was leaking from near cook line which required service technician to shut off water temporarily. CDI: Running water to both hand sinks was restored during inspection.

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation Observed a box of unwashed mangos being stored on shelf in reach in cooler in bakery area over ready to eat foods and boxes of unwashed produce being stored on a shelf in walk in cooler above ready to eat foods. CDI: PIC re-arranged items to proper storage order.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ . Observed two containers of mashed potatoes being held below 135F in hot box. CDI: PIC reheated mashed potatoes to over 165F before returning to hot hold.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs . Observed containers of cooked mushrooms, cooked rice and cooked noodles being stored in prep cooler on cook line with date marks ranging from 6/15 to 6/17, several days past their expiration. CDI: PIC discarded expired items. REPEAT.

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. Observed a package of amberjack fish fully thawed in walk in cooler still in its reduced oxygen packaging. Package stated remove from packaging prior to thawing. CDI: PIC cut open package to remove from reduced oxygen environment.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed a scoop being stored handle down in a large container of sugar. CDI: PIC removed scoop from container of sugar.

rows.general_comments:
EHS discussed potentially moving flip top items in deli area to TPHC during warmer months. 
EHS discussed having walk-in unit assessed due to how much fan is working to maintain temps. Future compliance visit. 
EHS provided assistance to PIC in filling out TPHC paper to reflect changes in establishment. 

Per the PIC, staff have been trained to discuss items on menu with asterisk. Explaining to customers that items may be raw or contain undercooked ingredients.

violations.comments:
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink.
Obtain handwashing signage in bar areas.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed pimento cheese and chicken salad in deli flip top, sliced corned beef and sliced turkey in growlers prep unit, raw chicken in 2-door reach-in in back prep area, all temping above 41F. CDI- PIC removed items to rapidly cool due to being within parameters and top layer of items being close to or above fill lines. EHS discussed ensuring units are maintained closed when not in use or are held closed to recalibrate after being held open for extended periods while prepping foods. SEE ITEM #33.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). SEE COMMENT BELOW. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (A) Provide consumer advisory for animal foods served raw or under-cooked. Consumer advisory must include disclosure and reminder.-Pf. 
Observed deli portion of establishment without consumer advisory for new cold smoked salmon offered on menu. VERIFICATION REQUIRED on or before 7/10 to assess establishment consumer advisory. 

3-603.11 (B)(2) Consumer Advisory Disclosures that are asterisked to a footnote shall state, * Items are served raw or undercooked, or contain (or may contain) raw or undercooked ingredients. -Pf. SEE COMMENT ABOVE.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf. Observed TCS foods throughout establishment cooling in active units, covered containers, and in ice baths missing stirring step. CDI- EHS discussed ensuring units are maintained closed when not in use, items are cooled in units not being actively used, items are cooled in containers that are shallow and are maintained without lidding, use of ice bags as necessary. All items were removed to rapidly cool using larger cold holding units and ice bags. *REPEAT*



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving throughout facility. *REPEAT*
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed gaskets of cold holding equipment on Andalou cookline needing repair. Observed boards used to hold breads offered to guests with chipped wood surfaces.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed handles of cold holding equipment, fan in walk-in cooler, all needing to be cleaned.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed food splatter on walls, areas under and behind equipment needing to be cleaned on Andalou side. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed walls needing repair on Andalou side.

violations.comments:
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed cooked chicken wings that were cooked the day prior to the inspection that did not cool within the appropriate cooling parameters. Chicken wings were recorded at 46F. Also observed pizza sauce that was in a flip top low boy that was in the unit from 6/25. Sauce did not cool correctly in the low boy unit. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the chicken wings and sauce. (1.5 pts) 


For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed chicken wings and pizza sauce that were cooled in large deep containers with the lids secured. Items did not cool correctly. Use approved cooling methods when cooling. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the necessary items. (0.5 pts)


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed raw chicken stored above raw lamb. CDI-lamb relocated above chicken.

4-204.112 (B) Provide and position correctly an air thermometer in cold/hot holding equipment. Observed no air thermometer in large prep top cooler. Thermometer needed

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-306.11 Protect food on display using shields, packaging, or other effective means. -P. Observed non-TCS sugar/spice mix in open container at cash register. Food mix needs to be protected from contamination by customers or staff. CDI- mix removed
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed lots of wiping rags stored in baskets. Store in sanitizer between uses.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-901.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils may not be cloth dried. Observed staff towel drying containers.
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed containers of spoons stored with bowl side up. CDI-flipped over so handles up.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed cut watermelon, used for seasonal milkshake flavor, cold holding in flip top cooler at 48F. CDI - Watermelon was cut and placed in flip top at noon. Cut watermelon was placed in ice bath to rapidly cool to 41F or less. Cut watermelon was at 40F by 4PM. Whole watermelons are stored in walk in cooler, cut, placed back in walk in cooler to cool to 41F, then small portions are placed in flip top unit for service. -1.5 points-

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. Observed no written procedures for cheese, sliced tomatoes, coleslaw, diced chicken, hot dogs, quesadillas, and other TCS foods that are held on TPHC. CDI - PIC was able to have all TPHC procedures e-mailed from corporate office during inspection. PIC has been advised to retain procedures in establishment and make available upon request. -0 points-

TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed build up of dust on ceiling tiles and vents in kitchen prep areas. Recommend higher frequency and more thorough cleaning. -0 points-

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments: 5-501.113 The flow pressure of the fresh hot water SANITIZING rinse in a WAREWASHING machine, as measured in the water line immediately downstream or upstream from the fresh hot water SANITIZING rinse control value, shall be within the range specified on the machine manufacturer’s data plate and may not be less than 35 kilopascals (5 pounds per square inch) or more than 200 kilopascals (30 pounds per square inch). Observed pressure gauge reading 38 psi where it should be be 20 + or - 5

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observed small flying insects around can wash and 3-comp sink drain. Per PIC, EcoLab regularly services facility. Recommend more frequent service.

3-304.14(E) Store sanitizer containers used for wet wiping cloths off the floor and to prevent contamination of food, equipment, and utensils.
Observed sanitizer bucket stored on floor under front counter. All other sanitizer buckets properly located. PIC moved bucket off of floor.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris. 4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed build-up of food and splash residue on bottom interior surfaces of refrigeration/freezer units. Observed build-up of food debris on door tracts of front glass display case. PIC to address cleaning frequency.

violations.comments: 5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. EHS observed hand sink by make line full of ice during inspection. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC removed ice from hand sink. REPEAT. (1)

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P EHS observed dish machine unable to dispense chemicals at proper concentration during inspection. PIC stated unit has only been used a few times this morning. PIC instructed food employee to prime unit and run another full cycle prior to checking. CDI - EHS observed proper concentration after unit was primed and a full cycle was ran. (0)

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed all items in WIC above 41F during inspection. During initial assessment, EHS observed WIC at 43F, WIC quickly cooled to 41F within minutes. PIC stated food employee checked temperatures at 10:30am and everything was under 41F. PIC unable to locate today’s temperature logs. PIC stated the first employee that arrives to the kitchen is required to do a temperature check. Facility has installed data loggers in WIC to keep track of ambient temperatures. PIC showed EHS temperature line graph showing the last 24 hours of ambient temperature. EHS observed WIC has been above 41F for the last 24 hours. PIC stated due to temperature issues and information available from the data loggers, all TCS foods not prepared today will be voluntarily discarded. By the end of inspection, EHS observed WIC at 38F. EHS advised to complete temperature logs daily to ensure food temperatures are being documented. EHS did not observe any active cooling in WIC during inspection. EHS observed one item cooling in make unit during inspection. PIC stated items are placed into WIF to cool. All items in WIF at 41F or below. PIC voluntarily discarded all TCS foods in WIC that have been in unit overnight or longer than 4 hours. Repair technician arrived onsite as well to ensure unit is functioning as it should. Repair technician stated everything on unit is working properly. Repair technician stated that evaporator on unit is going bad but is still able to operate; part has already been ordered and will be installed within two days. EHS will conduct 3-day VR to observe long term compliance with cold holding. (1.5)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P Observed employee wash hands and then use bare hands to turn off faucet before returning to food prep. CDI- provided education to employee on proper procedures and employee properly washed hands. 



For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed Alfredo sauce cooling in walk in that did not meet cooling parameters. According to cooling log alfredo was 165F at 11am and placed in walk in cooler. No additional temperatures were taken by staff. Observed alfredo 75F at 3:00pm. CDI- Alfredo discarded. 



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed marinara sauce in steam table at 124F. CDI- reheated to above 165F on stove and placed back in steam table. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed ricotta and pasta in back prep cooler with date exceeding 7 days. CDI- items discarded. Observed cooked sliced chicken in prep unit with today’s date but was cooked yesterday. CDI- relabeled with yesterday’s date. Observed eggplant with date exceeding 7 days. Date was for original day when prepared prior to freezing. Ensure thaw date is added. Points not escalated as facility has a good overall date marking system. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. -P. Observed digital timer system in place to track various pizzas, marinara and mozzarella cheese. Several pizzas had been remade since original timer was set; timer was not reset and no other tracking method used. Employees unsure on how to reset timer or which timers went with which pizzas. Additional training needed. CDI- all pizzas labeled with written labels.

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed Alfredo sauce cooling in deep container. Observed portioned cooked pasta stacked in deep containers in walk in cooler. Observed lettuce that had been washed and cut at 60F in top of prep unit. Cool prior to placing in prep units. CDI- Alfredo discarded, pastas laid out on sheet trays and lettuce laid out on sheet tray and placed in walk in cooler.

5-202.14 Prevent backflow or back siphonage prevention devices installed shall meet American Society of Sanitary Engineering standards. -P. Observed hose with spray arm attached to faucet at can wash with a backflow preventer not rated for continuous use. CDI- spray arm disconnected.

violations.comments:
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. 
Observed chicken cut at 9:30am holding at 46F at 2:30pm. Cooling methods insufficient to support proper cooling. See violation 33 for further details.
CDI - Chicken voluntarily discarded.
1.5 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed marinara holding below 135F during inspection. See temperature chart.
CDI - PIC verified sauce had been reheated within an hour of REHS discovery. Sauce reheated to 165F and placed back into steamwell.
Repeat. 1.5 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed various items throughout kitchen holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart. Cooked chicken in sautee prep overstocked. Dressings in WIC had been held outside of temperature control by front of house staff during lunch rush before being placed back in WIC. REHS recommended utilizing TPHC for these items.
CDI - Chicken moved to RIF to rapidly cool. All other items voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed canned artichokes being cooled in WIC while in covered, stacked containers. Artichoke hearts were holding at 42F with 10 minutes left in cooling window. Observed cut cooked chicken cooling while in a deep, covered container in WIC. Chicken had missed cooling window.
CDI - Artichokes unstacked and uncovered. Chicken voluntarily discarded.
Repeat. 1 pt.
Risk Control Plan to be discussed if no improvement by next inspection.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed various items throughout kitchen holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart. Cooked chicken in sautee prep overstocked. Dressings in WIC had been held outside of temperature control by front of house staff during lunch rush before being placed back in WIC. REHS recommended utilizing TPHC for these items.
CDI - Chicken moved to RIF to rapidly cool. All other items voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed canned artichokes being cooled in WIC while in covered, stacked containers. Artichoke hearts were holding at 42F with 10 minutes left in cooling window. Observed cut cooked chicken cooling while in a deep, covered container in WIC. Chicken had missed cooling window.
CDI - Artichokes unstacked and uncovered. Chicken voluntarily discarded.
Repeat. 1 pt.
Risk Control Plan to be discussed if no improvement by next inspection.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf
Observed various dented cans being stored in conjunction with unadulterated cans during inspection.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.
Repeat. 1 pt.

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. -Observed raw katsu chicken stored above raw shrimp in the reach-in freezer. Raw katzu chicken was previously thawed and prepared at facility (breaded). -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. -No point escalation due to significant improvement in storage order noted in storage order since last inspection. All storage order in prep coolers and WIC was correct. -1/2 points-


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
-Observed prepped cream cheese in low reach-in cooler above 41F. All other foods in cooler were properly cold holding at or below 41F. Date mark showed cream cheese was prepped two days ago, and PIC stated cream cheese has not been removed from cooler since preparation date. -CDI- PIC lowered ambient temperature of cooler and EHS observed temperature of cooler drop to 36F. PIC voluntarily discarded the cream cheese.
-Observed steamed broccoli above 41F in wok prep top, stored in container directly on top of other foods. Broccoli was steamed this morning and needed for lunch service use. All other foods in the cooler were properly cold holding at or below 41F. -CDI- PIC moved steamed broccoli to WIC for rapid cooling. By end of inspection, EHS observed broccoli cooling at sufficient rate to reach 41F within 6 hours of steaming. EHS recommended steaming broccoli a day in advance, so that the broccoli has sufficient time to cool in the WIC and be moved to the wok prep unit for use the next day.


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf -Observed one container of kani held on 4-hour TPHC without a time stamp stating when the kani was removed from temperature control. All other foods held on 4-hour TPHC had a correct time stamp (rice, other containers of kani, eel). EHS also observed proper TPHC procedures for all foods held on 4-hour TPHC. -Significant improvement in TPHC procedures and implementation noted since last inspection. No point escalation- 1/2 points-

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. -Observed ROP tuna thawed in prep cooler, still within the ROP bag environment. -CDI- PIC cut hole in bag to remove it from the ROP environment.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.14 Unpackaged food shall be protected from contamination during preparation. -Observed bottom of food container directly touching the top of crab meat in prep unit. Crab meat container did not have lid to protect the food from contamination. -CDI- PIC placed lid on crab meat container.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container.
Observed tongs used to dispense cooked noodles stored with the handle in contact with the food. -CDI - PIC removed the handle from the food. 
-REPEAT-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. -Observed wet stacking among multiple dishes, stacked and stored as clean. -CDI- PIC separated the containers for proper air drying.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance.
-Observed cardboard with visible food residue stored on top of seasoning bowls. PIC stated cardboard is used to protect the seasoning bowls from contamination. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the cardboard. Going forward, PIC stated employees will use plastic wrap to cover and protect the seasonings from contamination.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed a large plastic bin of lettuce to be at temperatures of 44-46F in the 2 door lowboy at the sushi area. Both sushi employees stated not having it out at all today and the last time it was used was yesterday. Item was out of temperature for an unknown amount of time.

CDI - Voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. -P - Observed a bottle of floor cleaner to be stored on a sry storage shelf amongst opened packages of spices and seasonings.

CDI - Chemical bottle moved to chemical storage area in dishwashing room.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed approx 30-40 packages of various grab & go sushi rolls to be at temperatures of 43-47F in the sushi display case, sushi 2 door lowboy, and sushi sliding door lowboy. All rolls were prepared today at approx 8am. Sushi employee stated that there is less room in the shared WICs on Mondays so they use the other refrigerators to cool the prepped sushi rolls down. Lowboys, grab & go cases, and sliding door lowboys are all not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.

CDI - All actively cooling sushi rolls moved to the WIC, where they were then observed at 40F by 10:15am.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. - Observed a small plastic scoop used for mocha powder behind the coffee bar to be stored with its handle in direct contact with the mocha powder.

CDI - Scoop removed so handle is not in contact with the mocha powder.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the interior/exterior gasket area of the cheese lowboy and the sliding door tracks of the deli display case to have accumulations of food debris/grime. REPEAT.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed sushi with raw fish stored over California roll in display case that did not contain raw fish. CDI, sushi relocated for proper storage.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed cooked mushrooms cooling at ambient room temperature on sheet tray. Cooked mushrooms had a temperature of 74F and had been removed from oven 1 hour prior. CDI - Cooked mushrooms were moved to walk in cooler to rapidly cool to 41F or less. -0 points-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed frozen packages of beef thawing at ambient room temperature on prep table. CDI - Packages were taken to walk in cooler to continue thawing under refrigeration.
3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. Observed thawed ROP Salmon in refrigerated drawer that had not been removed from oxygen free environment. PIC has been advised to follow thawing instructions printed on each individual package. -0.5 points-

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed packages of chicken breading mixture stored on platform less than 6 inches above the floor. Raise as needed.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed grease and flour residue build-up on the sides of the fryers; observed sticker residue on the outside of cleaned food containers that were stored above the 3 comp sink; observed the WIC floor and shelves with residue build-up and needing cleaned. Increase cleaning frequency to prevent build-up. REPEAT. 

Note: no escalation in point deduction due to some improvement; drink machines, WIC surfaces, and prep areas were observed as clean at time of inspection.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed top lids missing on recycle dumpster unit. Replace lids.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed the floor area under and around the fryers has residue and debris build-up and needs to be cleaned. Ensure frequent cleaning to prevent build-up. REPEAT.

Note: No escalation in point deduction due to some improvement; previously cited ceiling tiles and WIC flooring have improved based on previous observations and entries.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-501.14 Change the filters and clean the intake and exhaust air ducts so they are not a source of contamination by dust, dirt or other materials. Observed excess dust and grease build up at vents above fryer units. Clean as needed.

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris and sticker residue on dishes stored as clean. -CDI- PIC moved items to dish pit to be W/R/S. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed items in top of prep unit above 41F. Observed ambient air at 37F. Refer to temperature chart. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items. EHS recommended facility keep lid closed more often or use TPHC. -1.5-
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Food https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed flying pests. Recommended increase pest control frequency, especially during summer months. -0-

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacking with dishes stored as clean. -CDI- PIC moved items to dish pit to be W/R/S. -0-

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor./ 4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used. Observed drink lids out of the original protective package with drink splash on them. -0-

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed interior and exterior surfaces of some cold drawers, door of prep unit and NFCS and counter where drink machine is in need of cleaning. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. -0-

56 - Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed box of employee popsicles stored over retail items. -CDI- PIC relocated items. -0-

CDI-all TCS foods from the line was discarded. TCS foods prepped today (date marked today) were sent to WIC to cool, TCS foods in prep cooler overnight were discarded.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed scoop handle down in sugar, tea area cabinet.

violations.comments:
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Several TCS RTE foods in upright cooler had today's date labeled on items, but PIC stated they were actually cooked yesterday. CDI - PIC corrected date labels.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Floor tiles loose underneath prep sink. Repair floor tiles. 0 pts.
