violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed proper employee health policy however no documentation of training of current staff. CDI- PIC had staff read and sign employee health policy during inspection. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf. Observed no soap at hand sink next to ice machine. Repeat violation. CDI- soap provided.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P. Observed condensation from air vent in walk in cooler dripping into container of uncovered meatballs stored below vent. CDI- product discarded.

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed multiple 5 gallon buckets of sauces and soups in walk in cooler uncovered that were prepared on previous dates.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed ricotta, sliced tomatoes, sausage and green peppers above 41F stored in left side of 2 door low boy cooler in pizza area. Right side of unit holding below 41F. CDI- items relocated. Observed main prep unit on line holding at 48-51F. Observed all foods in unit above 41F. See temperature chart. PIC did not know how long unit had not been holding temperature. CDI- all product in unit discarded except product prepared today was allowed to be placed in walk in to rapidly cool. Repair person arrived on site before end of inspection and cooler was holding at 38F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed chicken wings and eggplant in drawers near stoves without dates. These items are cooked and frozen on same day then pulled from the freezer as needed. Observed chicken wings in walk in cooler with cook date of 6/15/26 but were pulled from the freezer yesterday. Facility should include a pull/thaw date for frozen items to ensure dates are accurate. CDI- items were allowed to be dated.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed cooling occurring throughout the facility in reach in refrigerators. Observed items cooling in walk in covered in deep containers. CDI- all items uncovered and/or moved to walk in cooler to facilitate cooling. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-304.13 Linens and napkins may not be used in contact with food unless used to line a container for the service of foods and are replaced each time the container is refilled for a new customer.
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor.
Observed portioned salads stacked together in salad prep with bottom of bowl in direct contact with salad being stored underneath. All stacked salads observed covered with table napkin.
CDI - Napkin removed from salads and all salads unstacked.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed bucket of quat sanitizer on cook line with less than 100ppm. CDI- bucket refilled with proper concentrated sanitizer. 
3-304.14(D) Maintain dry wiping cloths free of visible debris and soil. Maintain sanitizer containers for in-use cloths free of visible debris and soil. Observed employee using dry wiping cloth for handling hot items to also wipe of tongs in between uses.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P EHS observed pulled pork, baked beans, spicy BBQ sauce, and chicken wings below 135F (See temp chart). PIC stated all items were re-heated to 165F on either stove top or tilt skillet prior to being placed into hot holding units. PIC stated all items were re-heated 45 min prior to inspection. PIC stated items have not been removed from unit. EHS observed all other TCS items inside unit at 135F or above. CDI - PIC removed all items and quickly re-heated items back to 165F prior to placing back into hot box. PIC turned temperature of hot box up. REPEAT. (3)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed lime sour cream and salsa Roja stored in make unit above 41F. PIC stated bottles were prepared this morning about 2 hours prior to inspection. EHS advised to hold TCS sauce bottles in reach in compartment of make unit to allow for more air flow which will help with long term compliance. CDI - PIC instructed food employee to removed bottles and ice down in WIC prior to moving back to make unit. PIC stated bottles will be held in reach in compartment moving forward. (0)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. EHS observed numerous flies near back of kitchen area. PIC stated pest control is out monthly and recently conducted a visit a few days prior. PIC stated facility is obtaining food-safe pesticide approved for commercial use. EHS advised to procure an SOP for this process to ensure food is protected when used in facility. (0)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. EHS observed reach in freezer on make line in disrepair. PIC stated work order has already been placed. EHS observed unit with out of order signage posted and no TCS foods stored inside. 

4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. EHS observed interior walls and door of WIC beginning to corrode. PIC stated facility plans on obtaining refrigerated truck within the coming week to move all food inside truck while WIC is being repaired. EHS will return when truck arrives onsite to verify holding temperatures and any changes to process. EHS observed bottom of prep table in kitchen area accumulating corrosion. REPEAT. 

4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. EHS observed two cutting boards with deep cuts on cutting surface creating a surface that can no longer be effectively cleaned. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded cutting boards. (0.5)

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed walls by mop sink and dish machine soiled with black residue and debris. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. (0)

EHS advised to procure an SOP for this process to ensure food is protected when used in facility. (0)

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. EHS observed reach in freezer on make line in disrepair. PIC stated work order has already been placed. EHS observed unit with out of order signage posted and no TCS foods stored inside. 

4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. EHS observed interior walls and door of WIC beginning to corrode. PIC stated facility plans on obtaining refrigerated truck within the coming week to move all food inside truck while WIC is being repaired. EHS will return when truck arrives onsite to verify holding temperatures and any changes to process. EHS observed bottom of prep table in kitchen area accumulating corrosion. REPEAT. 

4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. EHS observed two cutting boards with deep cuts on cutting surface creating a surface that can no longer be effectively cleaned. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded cutting boards. (0.5)

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed walls by mop sink and dish machine soiled with black residue and debris. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. (0)

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed facility unable to demonstrate what training and how they know employees have been trained regarding the health policy. Manager is knowledgeable and there are fully translated posters throughout the facility regarding foodborne illnesses. Per PIC, this is covered in onboarding and they cannot work without the onboarding. CDI: EHS provided paper copy of updated 3 page employee health policy and PIC reviewed and had staff sign policy during inspection. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. -P- Observed food employee handle raw steak to place it on the grill and then handle clean utensils to place cooked eggs in and then handle ready to eat items without washing hands. -CDI- EHS educated food employee on when to wash and food employee washed hands. REPEAT VIOLATION

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed open case of raw steak stored over bags of fries in drop in freezer. CDI: Fries removed. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed top tray of storage rack for utensils with many utensils/small equipment with grease and debris build up. CDI: Equipment removed for washing and some equipment removed to be taken out of use as PIC stated they do not need it anymore. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed 2 containers of egg patties in hot box below 135F. CDI: Product voluntarily discarded. All other hot items held at 135F and above throughout facility. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed all items in stacked reach in cooler holding above 41F. Air temp of unit was 45F. Items were relocated to walk in cooler to properly cool to 41F. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days on or before 7/2/26 to ensure cooler is repaired. REPEAT VIOLATION 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments: 6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. EHS observed hand sink by pizza station missing proper hand washing sign. EHS provided PIC with additional hand washing signed. CDI - PIC posted signage. (0)

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed numerous pans with sticker residue present on exterior surface of pans. EHS observed these pans stacked on each other when put away for storage. EHS observed immersion blender with food residue present on blade surface. CDI - PIC removed pans and blender to dish machine where it will be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed cooked rice, romaine lettuce, raw cod, raw chicken, and raw shrimp stored in make unit above 41F (see temp chart). Food employee stated romaine was needed for service and had just been cut and placed into unit prior to beginning of inspection. PIC stated cod, shrimp, rice, and chicken had been in unit since opening at 11am. PIC stated all items are taken from WIC at open to stock unit. EHS observed temperature of interior thermometer at 44F. EHS observed temperature of unit after 30 minutes at 41F. EHS advised to check temperatures at a more frequent rate to observe if unit can hold up with capacity of facility when busy. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded cod, shrimp, and rice. Lettuce and chicken was removed from make unit to be cooled in WIC. REPEAT. ***Third full point deduction for cold holding. EHS has observed some improvement since last inspection. Risk control plan is not needed at this time. IF facility is out of compliance during next inspection. Risk control plan could be implemented. (3)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed cooked chicken wings actively cooling in WIC from being portioned into smaller containers. PIC stated wings had been cooked at 11am, placed into WIC to cool, and then portioned into smaller metal containers for storage. EHS based cooling rate on final cook time of 11am. EHS observed wings cooling in covered metal containers in WIC. EHS observed inadequate cooling rate after thirty minutes. CDI - PIC removed wings from WIC, placed onto sheet pans, and placed into WIF to quickly cool prior to storage. EHS observed temperature of wings at 41F prior to end of inspection. (0.5)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. EHS observed a few pans stored as clean being stacked on top of each other with water present on interior of containers. EHS observed pans stagger stacked and air drying during inspection. (0)

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. EHS observed ceiling of WIC leaking water. EHS observed only minor dripping occurring during inspection. Water is coming from sprinkler access in WIC from repairs conducted prior which caused to an opening in the ceiling. PIC is aware of issue and has scheduled repairs for ceiling. (0)

rows.violation_text:
10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. EHS observed hand sink by pizza station missing proper hand washing sign. EHS provided PIC with additional hand washing signed. CDI - PIC posted signage. (0)

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed numerous pans with sticker residue present on exterior surface of pans. EHS observed these pans stacked on each other when put away for storage. EHS observed immersion blender with food residue present on blade surface. CDI - PIC removed pans and blender to dish machine where it will be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed cooked rice, romaine lettuce, raw cod, raw chicken, and raw shrimp stored in make unit above 41F (see temp chart). Food employee stated romaine was needed for service and had just been cut and placed into unit prior to beginning of inspection. PIC stated cod, shrimp, rice, and chicken had been in unit since opening at 11am. PIC stated all items are taken from WIC at open to stock unit. EHS observed temperature of interior thermometer at 44F. EHS observed temperature of unit after 30 minutes at 41F. EHS advised to check temperatures at a more frequent rate to observe if unit can hold up with capacity of facility when busy. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded cod, shrimp, and rice. Lettuce and chicken was removed from make unit to be cooled in WIC. REPEAT. ***Third full point deduction for cold holding. EHS has observed some improvement since last inspection. Risk control plan is not needed at this time. IF facility is out of compliance during next inspection. Risk control plan could be implemented. (3)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed cooked chicken wings actively cooling in WIC from being portioned into smaller containers. PIC stated wings had been cooked at 11am, placed into WIC to cool, and then portioned into smaller metal containers for storage. EHS based cooling rate on final cook time of 11am. EHS observed wings cooling in covered metal containers in WIC. EHS observed inadequate cooling rate after thirty minutes. CDI - PIC removed wings from WIC, placed onto sheet pans, and placed into WIF to quickly cool prior to storage. EHS observed temperature of wings at 41F prior to end of inspection. (0.5)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. EHS observed a few pans stored as clean being stacked on top of each other with water present on interior of containers. EHS observed pans stagger stacked and air drying during inspection. (0)

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. EHS observed ceiling of WIC leaking water. EHS observed only minor dripping occurring during inspection. Water is coming from sprinkler access in WIC from repairs conducted prior which caused to an opening in the ceiling. PIC is aware of issue and has scheduled repairs for ceiling. (0)

violations.comments: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P Observed employee wash hands and then use clean hands to turn off faucet before attempting to return to handle food. CDI: Employee rewashed hands and used paper towel as a barrier to turn off faucet.

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY Observed spray bottle of quat sanitizer being stored at pizza prep area in which quat sanitizer measured 0ppm. CDI: Spray bottle was refilled with quat sanitizer which measured 200ppm.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs . Observed chubs of chicken, ham, London broil and roast beef opened from their original packaging being stored in display case with no date marks. CDI: PIC date marked chubs of ham and chicken as they were opened this morning and voluntarily discarded London broil and roast beef as they were unsure of date they were opened.

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P - Observed leafy greens held on TPHC but discard label not provided. Observed diced potatoes removed from WIC and held on TPHC but no 4 hour label provided. CDI, leafy greens voluntarily discarded and label provided for potatoes.

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf - observed Barbaquoa thawing under running water at 81 degrees F. CDI, item transferred to WIC to thaw under refrigeration.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed some spice containers, sugar and flour not labeled by common name of food.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed some wiping cloth held on waist of food employees, some soiled cloths on ground in prep line and some wiping cloths held in disinfectant and not sanitizer solution.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure.Observed some food residues behind equipment on floors in hard to reach areas. Maintain these areas clean.

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments: 3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf. Observed only one shellfish tag for oysters in the last 90 days. PIC indicated that other tags were accidentally discarded. CDI- by educating PIC on proper shellfish tag retention requirements.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed carrots, potatoes, zucchini, plantains and cabbage cooling on counter upon arrival. All items were cooked at approximately 10:15-10:30am. PIC placed items over ice on counter. Ice bath was insufficient to properly cool foods in required time parameters. CDI- items placed in shallow pans in walk in cooler to ensure parameters were met. Repeat violation. If violations occur at next inspection a risk control plan will be required to be implemented. 



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed all foods in steam table below 135F. Steam table was not on due to breaker tripped. CDI- steam table turned back on and all foods reheated to above 165F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed dates on several items not being carried through to working containers on line. Pico, queso and cooked onion carrot mix were labeled with today’s date in prep units but products were prepared yesterday. CDI- items allowed to be redated with yesterday’s date. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

rows.general_comments:
EHS completed inspection with trainee Ericka Jolley 
EHS is allowing facility to use emergency TPHC for the unit that is not working. Facility has a second cooler that was not working at the start of the inspection. Facility was not keeping any food in this unit. EHS and PIC discussed the food that is typically stored in the prep unit and only lettuce was deemed to be TCS.
Facility has one additional prep unit in the main kitchen area that can be used for storage as well. 

EHS is allowing facility to utilize emergency TPHC for the units. All food must start at 41F and MUST be discarded after 4 hours. Items must be labeled.

violations.comments:
2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P 
Observed food handler wash hands and turn off faucet with bare hands. CDI - PIC re-educated food handler on hand washing practices and food handler re-washed.

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed food in handsink of prep area with food debris in basin. Observed food in handsink on cook line being used to dump water from prepped tuna.
CDI - EHS re-educated food handler on limitations of handsink usage.

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. 
Observed unwashed produce directly above RTE foods in WIC. CDI - PIC restored proper storage order.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed multiple dishes and pans with visible food debris stacked as clean. CDI - Moved affected pans to be re-w/r/s. 
**REPEAT**

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
EHS observed many TCS items in prep unit holding above 41F. After 30 minutes the ambient cooler temperature was 43F on the top portion of the unit. EHS thermometer and there was no temperature change on the TCS items. See temperature chart. PIC placed TCS items on emergency TPHC until the prep unit is fixed by technician. PIC voluntarily discarded TCS items. 3 DAY VR.
-3-

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf
Observed krab sticks being thawed under running water at 83F. CDI - PIC added ice to water to maintain a 70F water temperature to thaw.

3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use.
Observed avocados in prep unit with stickers still attached. CDI - Food handler removed stickers and re-washed avocados.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf. Observed four (4) large containers of cooling salad lettuce in WIC cooling at a rate below required rate. CDI - PIC and EHS discussed cooling rate and options for improving cooling rate (I.E. rotating salad bins on speedrack to covered storage to allow for open cooling of bins, etc.) EHS provided ambient cooling chart to PIC. REPEAT. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed cleaned metal food containers stacked together while still wet on the back prep table. Ensure cleaned equipment is properly air dried before stacked together.

rows.general_comments: EHS observed the establishment preparing a bread starter. PIC stated the starter is made from flour, water, baking soda and salt and then covered and stored at room temperature to ferment. PIC further stated the starter is used to prepare fried dough covered by melted sugar for jalebi (dessert) and no TCS ingredients are added.

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed knives stored as clean with visible food residue and build up in knife holder in prep aria. CDI - PIC moved all knives to dish put for cleaning. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed pongal cooling from yesterday above 41F. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded item. 



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). EHS Observed several items made yesterday with today’s date marking. EHS observed items frozen from a previous day with today’s date marking. Items: Hpma, tomatoes, garlic chutney. CDI - PIC changed all date marking to reflect original prep date. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed chopped cabbage being stored without protective rap on mettle shelving. CDI - PIC washes and wrapped.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers

violations.comments: 3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. EHS observed speed rack full of blanched fries and TCS foods. PIC stated speed rack is mainly used to cool fries, due to amount facility will need in a day it is usually left uncovered. EHS advised to cover items on speed rack if cooling has been completed. (0)

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P EHS observed dish machine unable to dispense sanitizer properly during beginning of inspection. PIC stated it has been used but only a few times since this morning. CDI - PIC was able to prime unit and run a few full cycles. EHS observed proper concentration of sanitizer prior to end of inspection. 
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed numerous pans, stored clean, with sticker residue present on exterior of pan. EHS observed a few pans with food debris and residue present on interior portion of pans. CDI - PIC removed pans to three compartment sink to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (1.5)
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed sliced cheese, blue cheese, and ranch in make unit above 41F. PIC stated sliced cheese was placed into unit at 11am, ranch and blue cheese have been in unit over night. EHS observed dressings stored in portion cups stacked high in top of make unit; cheese placed on parchment paper in original packaging. EHS advised facility to consider storing cheese and sauces in reach in compartment of unit if cold holding cannot be achieved in top of make unit. CDI - PIC removed cheese to WIC to allow for quick cooling, sauces were voluntarily discarded. (0)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. EHS observed shelving used to store food items in WIC soiled with food residue and debris. EHS advised to increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. (0)
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. EHS observed hand sink faucet broken in men’s restroom. PIC stated sink will be scheduled for repairs. (0)

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. EHS observed concrete flooring in WIC in need of sealant. EHS observed sealant has diminished over time. EHS observed floor beginning to become sticky and hard to clean due to being absorbent. 

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed walls and flooring around three compartment sink and cooking equipment soiled with food residue and debris. EHS advised to increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. REPEAT (1)

violations.comments:
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed tomatoes in walk in cooler prepared at 4:30am still above 41F. CDI: Tomatoes voluntarily discarded. EHS provided suggestions to PIC regarding cooling including but not limited to adding ice to the water after using veggie wash so the tomatoes remain 41F and below during preparation. Then, be sure to slice and place tomatoes in the walk in immediately after prep. EHS also discussed different placement of the tomatoes as they were right next to do the door. The facility received a large delivery this morning and they cleaned the walk in floors meaning the door was left open for some time with the tomatoes right at the door. Consider also uncovering tomatoes until fully cooled to 41F. 





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed gaskets in disrepair on prep unit of cookline. Repair or replace. *REPEAT*
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed heavily rusted prep shelf on cookline. EHS discussed seeking corrosion resistant paint in lieu of replacing.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed rust buildup on ceiling vents due to water condensation. EHS discussed speaking with management and cleaning vents.

violations.comments:
REPEAT.
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed opened package of ground beef beside cabbage on WIC rack. PIC states all produce is employee food only and EHS advises need to label both racks for employee food clearly, and ensure food for the operation is clearly separated. 
CDI- Ground beef moved to rack below produce. No contamination observed. (-1.5)

REPEAT.
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed stack of dishes and ice scoop stored as clean on rack with stickiness and food debris. 
CDI- Dishes re-w/r/s. Risk control plan or permit action may result if repeat violation is not in compliance at next inspection. (-3)

REPEAT.
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed top layer of snow crab legs in cook line fliptop 2 at 48F and packaged butter portions above 70F in expo beverage cooler. 
CDI- Butter voluntarily disposed, top layer of crab legs relocated to WIC. (-3)

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed portioned cups of ranch and tartar sauce in beverage cooler prepped this morning labeled 06/22. 
CDI- Prep date confirmed and items correctly dated, then relocated to WIC for rapid cooling. (-0)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
REPEAT.
REPEAT.
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed portioned TCS dressings cooling in broken beverage cooler above 70F. Also observed sliced sausage cooling in WIC with cellophane covering preventing entry of cold air.
CDI- EHS confirms time dressings began cooling and allows relocation to WIC to complete cooling. TCS foods shall not be stored in the beverage cooler until it’s in good repair, and pre-packaged foods only, per manufacturer. Cellophane removed from cooling sausage. (-1)

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf
Observed crawfish thawing in container beside prep sink in undrained water at 65F.
CDI- Water drained, crawfish begin cooling. Proper thawing methods discussed with PIC. (-0.5)

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observed several dead roaches on floor in dry storage. PIC advised to remove dead pests promptly. Orkin verified as pest abatement service, and no contamination to food/equipment, live pests, of droppings observed. (-0)

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed numerous used wiping cloths on prep tops throughout establishment and no sani-buckets set up for their storage. EHS directs PIC to set up sani-buckets for expo, cook line, dish area, and prep area. (-0.5)

3-304.12 (C ) Store in-use utensils on a clean portion of the food preparation table or cooking equipment. The utensils and storage surface must be cleaned and sanitized every 4 hours.
Observed knives in bar area with blades between prep counters. EHS advises alternate storage. 
CDI- Knives removed. (-0)

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor.
Observed unwrapped single service plastic containers on prep cart which was covered in food debris and soiled cardboard.
CDI- Cardboard removed, prep cart cleaned and sanitized, soiled single service containers exposed to food debris voluntarily discarded. EHS advises staff leave articles in their sleeves until use. (-0.5)

4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent.
Observed beverage cooler graded for pre-packaged foods only being used for portioned sauces. Replace with equipment graded for this purpose.

Observed beverage cooler with ambient temp of 75F, not able to hold TCS foods. Do not store TCS foods inside until cold holding can be maintained. 

4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair.
Observed wooden handled spoons at clean dish racks with large cracks and deterioration, no longer cleanable and smooth. Replace with items which can be washed and are in good condition.

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observed several dead roaches on floor in dry storage. PIC advised to remove dead pests promptly. Orkin verified as pest abatement service, and no contamination to food/equipment, live pests, of droppings observed. (-0)

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed numerous used wiping cloths on prep tops throughout establishment and no sani-buckets set up for their storage. EHS directs PIC to set up sani-buckets for expo, cook line, dish area, and prep area. (-0.5)

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (C ) Store in-use utensils on a clean portion of the food preparation table or cooking equipment. The utensils and storage surface must be cleaned and sanitized every 4 hours.
Observed knives in bar area with blades between prep counters. EHS advises alternate storage. 
CDI- Knives removed. (-0)

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor.
Observed unwrapped single service plastic containers on prep cart which was covered in food debris and soiled cardboard.
CDI- Cardboard removed, prep cart cleaned and sanitized, soiled single service containers exposed to food debris voluntarily discarded. EHS advises staff leave articles in their sleeves until use. (-0.5)

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: REPEAT.
4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage.
Observed 4 of 4 fryer baskets with deteriorated metal wire coming apart from frame, no longer safe to use. PIC states they have replacements onsite and will remove old baskets from service. (-1)

4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent.
Observed beverage cooler graded for pre-packaged foods only being used for portioned sauces. Replace with equipment graded for this purpose. 

4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed beverage cooler with ambient temp of 75F, not able to hold TCS foods. Do not store TCS foods inside until cold holding can be maintained. 

4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair.
Observed wooden handled spoons at clean dish racks with large cracks and deterioration, no longer cleanable and smooth. Replace with items which can be washed and are in good condition. 

4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized.
Observed green cutting board in prep room with deep scoring, no longer cleanable. Replace.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: REPEAT.
4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed need for cleaning to outside of equipment and racks throughout establishment to remove stickiness, debris, oil residue, etc. increase cleaning frequency. (-1)

51 - Plumbing installed; proper backflow devices: 5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair.
Observed hot water faucet of hand sink upon entrance to kitchen not working. Repair at earliest opportunity. Multiple other hand sinks available and hot water verified as available and meeting demands. (-0)

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.114 Discard items and litter stored on the premises as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance.
Observed large ice machine near back door not operational and stated to be unused, and no intention of use in future. Remove equipment which is no longer needed. 

REPEAT.
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure.
Observed need for cleaning at hand sinks and their attachments; soap and paper towel dispensers, walls, etc. Observed need for cleaning of floors throughout; food and dirt accumulating in corners, behind and beneath equipment. (-1)

violations.comments:
5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. - Observed the handwash sink in the cardinal cafe back room to be obstructed from use by a wiping cloth in its basin.

CDI - Wiping cloth removed.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:
3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. Pf - Observed a can of pumpkin with heavy dents along its seams and was stored on the can rack in the dry storage room of the banquet kitchen amongst wholesome cans.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed an insert container of raw shelled eggs to be stored on the speed rack in the club lounge prep kitchen RIC directly above insert containers of RTE cut leafy greens. REPEAT.
- Observed a package of cooked lobster meat to be stored on the same tray with insert containers of raw salmon filets in a way that would allow for the salmon filets to potentially contaminate the lobster package in WIC 3 in the banquet kitchen. REPEAT.

CDI - Storage order rearranged in the club lounge prep kitchen RIC and the banquet kitchen WIC 3.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed trays/containers of cut melon, onion cream cheese, plain cream cheese, yogurt, and cheddar cheese to be at temperatures of 45-51F on top of and adjacent to the cold plate at the club lounge buffet. EHS advised the PICs to place the items onto TPHC during both previous inspections but there has been a new executive chef during all 3 inspections resulting in this solution not being communicated to the staff of the club lounge. Breakfast buffet is only operational from 6:30am-10:30am. REPEAT.

CDI - Items time stamped for discard at 10:30am. EHS filled out TPHC document with the 2 sous chefs at the time of the inspection and explained the TPHC procedure to the staff in the club lounge. EHS reviewed the entire policy and how to properly document the time stamps with the staff.

- Observed a tray of cooked potato wedges to be at a temperature of 68F on a speed rack in the club lounge prep kitchen. Speed rack was outside of temperature control for an unknown amount of time and no employees were actively working with the items. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed a chub of ham to be in WIC 3 in the banquet kitchen and was not provided with a date mark of any kind. Prep staff stated opening the package yesterday (6/25).
- Observed a container of hard boiled eggs to be in the RIC adjacent the large dough mixer in the banquet kitchen and was not provided with a date mark of any kind. Prep staff stated that they had opened the package of hard boiled eggs the day before to make salads for the cardinal cafe grab & go.

CDI - PIC date marked items.

- Observed a container of sausage gravy mix to be in the banquet kitchen WIC 3 and was provided with a date mark of 6/13, making today day 14. Staff stated he had not been using it and had made fresh sausage gravy mix but had forgot to the throw away the old batch. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed approx 10 containers of arugula Greek salad to be cooling at a rate of 0.0 degrees/min in WIC 1 in the banquet kitchen. Items had their cooling process interrupted by the prep staff adding final ingredients, placing the lids on the containers, and moving them to the main floor kitchen 4 door RIC that is in the shared Dogwood kitchen space. All of these variables resulted in the salads to not cool down at all.

CDI - PIC had staff member bring all arugula Greek salads back down to the banquet kitchen, had the lids removed and placed back into WIC 1. EHS observed the items at 41F by 10am.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf - Observed no chlorine test strips in the club lounge prep kitchen to measure the sanitizer levels of the 2 mechanical dishwashers.

CDI - Chlorine test strips were found in the executive chefs office in the banquet kitchen and brought up to the club lounge.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the nonfood contact surfaces of multiple plastic and metal insert containers on the clean dish storage racks at the banquet kitchen cookline to have tape/sticker residue. All contaminated insert containers were found on the top of their stack or not stacked at all, allowing for the food contact surfaces of the containers to remain clean.

CDI - All contaminated containers brought to the dishwasher area to be W/R/S.

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P
Observed employee handling raw chicken directly with black gloves, proceeded to discard gloves after handling raw chicken and donned on new gloves to handle ready to eat food directly for customer order. EHS intervened and PIC voluntarily discarded salad and instructed employee to remove gloves and wash hands. EHS/PIC provided education on when to wash hands.

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed in external WIC prepared corn in large white plastic container stored directly below unwashed produce and container of prepared lemons stored directly under unwashed produce. CDI - PIC corrected storage order for prepared corn and voluntarily discarded lemons. 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed three containers of shredded cabbage in low boy cooler above 41F from 6/25/26. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded all three containers of shredded cabbage.

rows.facility_name: TSUKI JAPANESE STEAK HOUSE & SUSHI BAR

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P EHS observed raw tuna stored over RTE sauces in sushi reach in unit. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC rearranged storage order. (0)

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P EHS observed dish machine unable to dispense sanitizer during inspection. Facility has used dish machine since opening. PIC assessed unit and stated tubing installed on unit to deliver sanitizer has holes present and prevents the liquid from reaching unit. CDI - PIC was able to repair unit during inspection. EHS observed unit dispensing chemical at correct concentration prior to end of inspection. (0)

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed numerous TCS items in WIC and 2-door sushi LB above 41F(see temp chart). After thirty minutes EHS observed no change in temperature. PIC stated on Wednesday ice build up was observed on back of unit in WIC. PIC stated units thermostat was adjusted to help with the freezing and must have been turned up too much. PIC stated yesterday WIC was observed at 42-43F, in response PIC turned unit back down. PIC was unaware the 2-door LB was in disrepair. PIC voluntarily discarded all TCS foods in WIC and 2-door LB and contacted repair technician. Due to additional refrigeration space and limited number of TCS items needed for service, facility will be allowed to temporarily operate while unit is being repaired. Facility will not be allowed to cook/cool any items until unit is repaired and must only store non-TCS foods inside WIC during this time, all other TCS foods shall be stored in extra units in kitchen area. All TCS items in sushi 2-door LB and WIC were voluntarily discarded. Sushi station has additional 2-door LB that is mainly used and all items can be sufficiently stored inside. EHS will return on Monday to ensure repairs have been done. If WIC has not been repaired, and IS will be issued. (1.5) *** Prior to EHS leaving facility EHS observed WIC at 40F, EHS advised facility to continue with what was previously discussed. Facility was advised to monitor internal temperature throughout the evening to ensure unit can consistently maintain temperature below 41F prior to beginning to utilize unit again. PIC stated repair tech will arrive at 6:30 to assess 2-door LB sushi unit and WIC. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) EHS observed call items in sushi display case without proper dates. PIC stated that items were thawed/cut this morning and verified with items in reach in unit. EHS stated during last inspection, all items in sushi display shall be dated marked. PIC discussed issue with sushi chef who verified dates with prep list from this morning. CDI - PIC was allowed to date all sushi items. PIC stated moving forward all items will be dated. This is the same date marking issue discussed during last inspection. REPEAT. (3)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. EHS observed container of dry rice and sweet tea without labels. These items are stored in dry storage. EHS provided education. (0)

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. EHS observed numerous metal bowls and containers stored as clean above three compartment sink with water present in interior of pans and equipment. EHS advised to stagger all dishes to allow for adequate air drying prior to stacking for storage. REPEAT. (0.5)

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. EHS observed inside surface of reach in compartment door and outside surface of toaster over soiled with food residue and debris. REPEAT. (1)

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed wall and floor around dish machine area soiled with food residue and debris. (0)

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:
3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P
Observed moldy jalapeño and shishito peppers in WIC during inspection.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.

3-401.14 (F) Establishment must obtain prior approval from the Health Department before using a non-continuous cooking process. Pf Non-continuous cooking application and procedures can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety
Observed partially cooked poached eggs being held cold in WIC during inspection. PIC stated eggs are cooked to around 135F before being cooled down in WIC, and reheated for service. Facility does not have approval for non-continuous cooking.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded par-cooked eggs. REHS and PIC discussed alternatives to this process, such as utilizing pasteurized shell eggs or opting to hold poached eggs on TPHC.
1.5 pts.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. 
Observed chicken cooked on and cooling since 6/25/26 holding at 44F during inspection. Observed cooked chicken wings portioned on and cooling from ambient since 6/25/26 holding at 45F. Cooling methods insufficient to support proper cooling. See violation 33 for further details.
CDI - Both items voluntarily discarded.
1.5 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed various items throughout kitchen holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart.
CDI - Items voluntarily discarded.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed cooked chicken cooling since 6/25/26 while in a deep pan near WIC door during inspection. Observed portioned chicken wings cooling from ambient since 6/25/26 while wrapped in plastic bags and stored in a deep container near WIC door. Both items missed proper cooling window.
CDI - Items voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed facility cooling pastas, salads and prepared foods in blast chiller where one container is open to track temperatures and others were closed and stacked while cooling. Open container showed it was at 41F and all items pulled and placed in front case where other items that were covered were observed above 41F. CDI items from front display case pulled to be cooled back down in blast chiller and items covered in blast chiller currently opened to allow proper air flow. Observed fruit platters for catering that were stacked while cooling in walk in unit. CDI items uncovered and spread out on racks to allow proper airflow for cooling.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed pan of baked chicken under heat lamp at 123-130F. CDI - chicken was for staff meal and reheated to above 165F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed Cole slaw, sour cream and salsa in one door prep unit at server station area holding above 41F. See temperature chart. Observed air temp of unit at 53F. CDI- all items discarded. Maintenance arrived on site to repair cooler and observed holding at 41F before leaving. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed one container of cooked pork belly dated with today’s date when sliced but was cooked 2 days ago. Ensure original cook/prepared date follows through with product until used or discarded. CDI- product allowed to be redated with correct date. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:

rows.violation_text:
13 - Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated: 3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. -Pf-

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments: 2-101.11 (A) PIC shall be present during all hours of operation. -PF. Observed PIC not present. Had staff contact to come in. PIC arrived half way through inspection.

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed no certified food protection manager onsite at beginning of inspection.

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P Observed staff member relocate mop and mop bucket remove gloves get new gloves and enter WIC CDI-not PIC present, EHS interceded. Staff member #2 took trash outside, staff #1 told him to wash hands (since I’d just told him to wash his hands) went to hand sink wet hands, turned off water, soaped up, turned water back on rinse and turn water off with hands. PIC not present, EHS interceded and demonstrated a correct hand washing procedure. CDI- Staff #2 rewashed hands

For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed hand sink clogged/slow drain from food CDI-sink cleaned. 
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. Repeat-Observed no handwashing signs at any handwashing sinks. CDI-handwashing signs provided by last EHS located in folder and put up at hand sinks

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed a couple TCS foods > 41. Staff said some items were out for a big order. Temps quickly came back down. Two items that didn’t were discarded. See chart. CDI-voluntarily discarded. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed several TCS items in WIC with labels dated 6/19, these items were still frozen so dates were incorrect. 
Facility is closed on Mondays and does majority of prep on Tuesdays.
CDI - Food properly labeled. Since food was still frozen, the food is not a hazard but date marking procedures are not being followed. No points taken at this time as risk was low, but points will be taken for future inspections.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf
3-501.19(B)(4)(5)Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. The food shall be discarded after the food has completed its 4 hour hold time, if the food is in unmarked containers, or marked with a time that exceeds the 4 hour time limit. -P
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. 

Observed correctly written TPHC form for rice noodles, pork belly, beef, these items did not have times for removal from heat or cold. CDI-voluntarily discarded
Observed shrimp appetizer in individual portioned to-go boxes on counter at 80F. Asked staff if they were on TPHC, asked PIC when he arrived, that menu item is not on the TPHC form. Needs to be added to the form or held cold (<41F). CDI-voluntarily discarded



TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed dish including undercooked eggs not baring asterisk to lead to menu’s consumer advisory.
CDI - PIC added asterisks to all menus.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed tightly wrapped pork cooling >2 hours and still >70F. CDI-voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .
3-304.14(D) Maintain dry wiping cloths free of visible debris and soil. Maintain sanitizer containers for in-use cloths free of visible debris and soil. Observed soiled cloths on prep tables.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed scoop without handle. CDI-cup discarded
3-304.12 (E) In-use utensils, such as ice scoops or utensils for non-TCS food, must be stored in a clean and protected location. Observed ice bucket stored on floor.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed unwrapped to-go container face down on a greasy cardboard box. Protect clean items from contamination

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place. Observed no drain plug in dumpster. Needs replacing.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-403.11 (A)Areas designated for employees to eat and drink, shall be located so that food, equipment, linens, single service/use items are protected from contamination. Observed staff eating meal at prep table.
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed staff supplements/food not separated from facility items.
