Observed handsink in second food preparation area with no handwash sign. EHS provided signage for PIC to post. 
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed items hanging on handsink located in second food preparation area. CDI - PIC removed items from land sink.

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. 
Observed unwashed produce above ready to eat foods in prep unit. CDI - PIC restored storage order.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed raw whole eggs stored in RIC above RTE foods. CDI - Eggs were removed from the establishment.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed ice machine flap with black build up. Increase cleaning frequency. CDI - PIC cleaned flap.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed items: cooked rice, half&half, and hot dogs in prep unit cold holding above 41F. PIC voluntarily discarded items. CDI - Technician arrived during inspection to apply freon to the cooler and ambient temperature dropped to below 41 degrees. Observed one of three pans of gumbo in reach in cooler at 43F. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded affected pan.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C)
Observed items tomato and coleslaw in prep unit with prep dates of 6/17, exceeding the 7 day holding parameter. 
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded affected items. 

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed portioned chili cups in RIF with no date marking to indicate prep dates. CDI - PIC labeled chili with date mark.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed lasagna and mashed potatoes hot holding in hot box at 115F and 125F. Both items were placed in hot box prior to lunch service. Internal temperature of hot box was measured at 190F. CDI - Both items were placed in oven to reheat to 165F or greater. Lasagna was reheated to 194F and mashed potatoes to 188F. PIC has been advised to monitor reheating processes making sure all TCS foods are reheated to 165F or greater prior to placing in hot holding. -1.5 points-

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed pasta, sliced cheese and sour cream in prep top unit at cook line above 41F, air temp was 39F. PIC stated items were sitting out. Observed cream cheese and half/half in lowboy at coffee bar above 41F, air temperature was 58F. Observed breakfast sandwiches, melon cups, yogurt cups and spinach danishes above 41F in display case at coffee bar, air temp was 42F. Observed whipping cream above 41F in glass reach in cooler in back kitchen area of coffee bar, air temp is 46F. violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf. For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. EHS observed portioned roast beef in WIC above 41F. PIC stated roast beef was cut and portioned this morning but unsure of exact time. EHS observed 6:09 am on date marking sticker. EHS observed roast beef above 41F at 10:30am. PIC stated roast beef is cut, portioned, and cooled in WIC. EHS observed no documentation of cooling process. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded roast beef due to missing cooling window. (1.5)

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed liquid eggs, whipped eggs, and sliced tomatoes above 41F in make units. EHS observed containers of liquid eggs and whipped eggs in secondary pan stacked on in another pan. PIC stated eggs are stored in this manner due to make units freezing product. EHS advised that this creates a barrier for cold holding. PIC stated eggs were placed into unit at 7:30am-8:00am. PIC unaware of what time tomatoes were placed onto make units. CDI - Sliced tomatoes voluntarily discarded. Liquid and whipped eggs moved to WIC to quickly cool. Secondary container was removed, and new eggs were placed into unit. REPEAT. (3)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) EHS observed container of feta cheese and sausage patties held beyond seven days. EHS observed another second container of sausage patties and Italian sausage with no date. PIC stated sausage patties were made this morning and verified item with prep list. PIC unaware of when Italian sausage was opened/thawed. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded feta cheese, sausage patties, and Italian sausage. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(4)(5)Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. The food shall be discarded after the food has completed its 4 hour hold time, if the food is in unmarked containers, or marked with a time that exceeds the 4 hour time limit. -P EHS observed toss salad mix being stored at expo window on time. EHS observed TPHC sticker past 4 hour hold. EHS notified PIC. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded salad mix. (0)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed cooked mushrooms, onions, tomatoes cooling from being prepared 30-45 minutes prior to inspection according to PIC. EHS observed items cooling at room temperature. EHS observed temperatures above 70F. PIC stated food is left out at room temperature until food has begun cooling and then placed into WIC. EHS advised that item only has two hours from time cooling begins to be under 70F. PIC removed items from speed rack and placed into WIC with tight fitting lids. EHS observed inadequate cooling rates after thirty minutes. At this time, these items had a remaining 20 minutes to achieve proper cooling. EHS intervened and advised to stretch items on sheet pans and placed into WIC to quickly drop temperature to meet parameters. CDI - PIC stretched onions, mushrooms, and tomatoes onto sheet pans and placed into WIC. EHS verified prior to two hour window and observed temperatures below 70F. (1) REPEAT. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. EHS observed container of in use wiping cloths at expo window at 0ppm. CDI - PIC removed container from service until a new bucket can be re-filled at correct concentration. (0.5)

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. EHS observed numerous pans and containers stored clean with water present on interior surface of containers. EHS advised to stagger dishes prior to stacking to allow for adequate air drying prior to storage. (0)

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. EHS observed personal items being stored co-mingled with single service items and dry foods. EHS advised to dedicate area for orderly storage of personal items. (0)

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. 
Observed unwashed produce stored directly above ready-to-eat foods. CDI- EHS discussed storage order with PIC, PIC began adjusting order. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). SEE COMMENT BELOW. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed lasagna in walk-in, portioned salsa in glass reach-in, both stored without date labeling and were originally removed from packaging earlier in the week per the PIC. CDI- PIC had date labels marked on containers. *REPEAT*



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

Observed unclean cloths stored on prep counters. EHS discussed removed to sanitizer or replacing.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed walk-in freezer with heavy ice build up on piping. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed unclean aluminum foil being used on cooking equipment. PIC stated items are replaced weekly. Ensure that only smooth and easily cleanable equipment is used and single use articles are removed and replaced at a frequency that prevents buildup.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed top of dish machine with heavy food residue and debris accumulation. Containers stored as clean with sticker residue intact. Increase cleaning frequency. *REPEAT*

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed cracks in floor around drains, Floors in need of repair around cracks. *REPEAT*

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed cardboard on floors in WIC. PIC stated items are change out daily. *REPEAT*

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed no available PIC with food protection manager certification. PIC demonstrated knowledge by answering questions during and after inspection, as well as by having no priority violations. REPEAT.






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf Observed handwashing station in restroom without paper towels in dispenser. CDI - PIC provided paper towels from available supply prior to end of inspection.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed presence of approximately seven (7) fruit flies throughout kitchen area, namely at mopsink. Facility is utilizing cups of vinegar as means of insect control, in conjunction with scheduled pest control (ECOLAB) Observed no contamination of exposed food or clean equipment via insect at time of inspection.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed scoop handles resting on non-tcs frozen food items in front cold drawer freezer.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed one (1) unopened box of single service articles (cups) stored at floor next to dry shelving. Elevate as needed.

rows.general_comments: EHS and PIC discussed adding data loggers/continuous monitoring devices to walk in cooler and walk in freezers. Facility has extremely large coolers/freezers with lots of food items present and this would strengthen their food safety management system as well as reduce the burden on the manager for close monitoring of cooler air temperatures. EHS advised there are a variety of different options but through discussion PIC indicated that one able to be remotely checked may work best for her use case.

violations.comments:
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed lomein noodles from yesterday cooling in walk in cooler in deep, covered bus tubs still above 41F. CDI: Items voluntarily discarded. 
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed large bus tub of raw pork prepared yesterday still cooling above 41F. CDI: Pork voluntarily discarded. 





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed cut cabbage, imitation crab meat, and cooked pork in prep unit with no date mark. REPEAT VIOLATION. Significant improvement from previous inspection as these 3 pans were the only items without dates. CDI: Dates added for day of prep. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed lomein noodles cooling in deep, covered plastic bus tubs from yesterday. Observed raw pork cooling in a deep covered bus tub from yesterday. CDI: Product voluntarily discarded. EHS and PIC discussed proper cooling methods for large amounts of food such as noodles and prepared meats/seafood. EHS advised to use smaller portions, uncovered, and mixing periodically. Additionally ice can be added in many cases. Ensure that products cool within 4 hours to 41F. REPEAT VIOLATION 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed no label on squeeze bottle at hibachi station that PIC stated was water. PIC will be making a chart to identify the bottles by color.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-901.11(A) Air dry equipment and utensils after cleaning and sanitizing./4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed clean pans wet stacked on the cooking line. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. REPEAT VIOLATION

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed some large plastic lids in need of replacement due to no longer being smooth and easily cleanable. REPEAT VIOLATION 
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed split gasket on walk in cooler door and on sushi low boy cooler doors. Observed walk in freezer fan blade covers on outside fish unit broken and damaged. Observed torn door flaps on walk in cooler and on first walk in freezer door.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed build up in hard to reach areas of reach in cooler doors throughout. Observed cleaning needed to interiors of walk in freezers and of reach in freezer.

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P 
Observed waitstaff handling onion rings and bread that was going to a customer. CDI items immediately discarded and replaced with fresh product. Review proper food handling with waitstaff 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. 
Observed unwashed watermelons and pineapples on shelving above RTE cut onion. CDI - PIC re-stored proper storage order.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed slicer with food buildup and debris on and around the blade. PIC verified that it was cleaned after the last use (2 days ago). CDI - PIC instructed and educated food handler to wash/rinse/sanitize slicer. 
4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure
Observed cavity of one microwave with food buildup and debris. Increase cleaning frequency.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed cold holding above 41F of items: leaf lettuce, portioned raw steak, ranch and one large tub of chopped romaine. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded affected items.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. -P
Observed sanitizer bucket on bottom shelf directly next to container of soda water that was being used to make batter. CDI - PIC re-located soda water.

violations.description: Compliance with variance, specialized process, reduced oxygen packaging criteria or HACCP plan
violations.comments:
8-103.12 Comply with the procedures in the HACCP plan as submitted and approved as part of a variance or local approval. Maintain approval and records as required.-P/Pf
Observed logs for reheating that was used the same temperatures or temperatures only one degree off for all products for multiple days (6/2, 6/3, 6/5, 6/8, 6/10, 6/11, 6/21, and 6/24) and missing visual cold hold checks for the day for multiple days (6/21, 6/3, and 6/9). Unable to review continuous monitoring system during the inspection due to system issue. Will follow up to check items. Verification required.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
Observed several working containers and shakers of seasonings, and a bin of panko bearing no identifying labels. CDI - PIC produced a sheet of labels that were being placed onto unlabeled containers and shakers at time of inspection.

violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed 2 employee drinks stored above food on prep line. CDI-drinks relocated

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments: 3-402.11 and 3-402.12 Provide records from supplier to guarantee fish served raw or undercooked is free of live parasites. -Pf. Observed asterisk on menu for fish of the day, for the consumer advisory. Fish of the day rotates between red snapper and trout. Parasite destruction letter provided for red snapper but not trout. CDI-discussion with PIC that trout cannot be served undercooked

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed staff prepping raw fish on same prep area as storing of uncovered dessert toppings, cooling collards and cooling red beans. Raw meats/seafoods should be prepared in separate area as RTE foods to prevent cross-contamination. CDI-fish prep moved to prep sink where fish was already thawing.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed several containers with sticker residue stored as clean. CDI-returned to 3-comp. sink for scrubbing

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed improper cooling methods used. Cooling rates not met. Active cooling must take place to meet cooling parameters. Cooling in ambient room temperature, deep covered containers, ice wands without stirring are not active methods. CDI-all items voluntarily discarded

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed cooked collard greens, cooked red beans, chicken and jambalaya on counter or speed rack during inspection. Cooked crawfish, cole slaw, tomatoes, cooling in WIC while in deep, covered plastic containers. An ice wand had been used with red beans soon after I started the inspection. All items had missed the cooling window. CDI-all items voluntarily discarded

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed scoops without handles in sugar and sauce. CDI-utensils discarded

rows.general_comments:
- PIC stated that egg roll fillings are Cooked, Cooled, Wrapped, Frozen, and then Reheated to order. Permit states that COOLING IS PROHIBITED due to lack of cooling equipment. EHS discussed immediately stopping cooking and cooling proteins. Eggroll’s must be wrapped with raw fillings. If this is found again on another inspection, an intent to suspend your permit will be issued. Please reach out to EHS if you plan to install a blast chiller to continue cooling food items. 

- EHS discovered large blue container holding large batch of house-sauce. Container is unable to be submerged in 3 compartment sink. When asking PIC how they clean it, they stated it is sprayed down and brushed out. Discontinue use of large container and replace with smaller containers that may be submerged in 3 compartment sink - Bus tubs work great here. 

- TPHC procedures provided during inspection.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed no employee health policy on site. EHS provided health policy. - 0 - 

 


For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events
violations.comments:
2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf Observed no vomit diarrhea plan on site. CDI - EHS provided. - 0 - 



For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation.

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf Observed no paper towels at one of the two hand sinks in the kitchen. PIC stated they do not have key to open and replace paper towels. CDI - Paper towels were placed next to sink. - 1 -

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw beef and eggs stored above RTE egg rolls in RIC. CDI - Storage order rearranged. 

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over RTE items in RIC. 

- 1.5 - 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed date marks missing from egg rolls with cooked filling in PT. Items are pulled from bulk batch in RIF. Ensure date is transferred from bulk batch. CDI - Dates transferred. 

EHS observed 2 containers of previously canned mushrooms dated 6/17 and 6/18. CDI - Items voluntarily discarded. - 1.5 -



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed empanadas in the RIC that were made 15 min ago and sliced tomatoes in the prep top that were prepped this morning , both above 41F. CDI PIC put items in the WIC and cooled down to 41F .

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

Observed black colored buildup on the door gaskets of the refrigeration units in the facility.

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed metal pan with build up of mold in cold holding drawer that was turned off and not being used. CDI - Metal pan was removed and taken to three compartment sink to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. PIC has been advised to empty and clean equipment that is not being used or scheduled for repair. -0 points-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed package of sugar stored on floor in dry storage area. CDI - Sugar was moved to nearby shelving. -0 points-

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed handles of two plastic scoops making contact with flour in two large storage bins. Plastic scoops were repositioned as to not make contact with food. -0.5 points-

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed heavy food debris, food splash, flour, and residues on shelving, on non-food contact surfaces of cooking, cold holding, and hot holding equipment, and on interior surfaces of reach in coolers. Recommend higher frequency and more thorough cleaning. -REPEAT- -1 point

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed build up of food debris and food splash on floors and walls in food prep areas. Recommend higher frequency and more thorough cleaning. -0 points-

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments:
3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf
Observed shellstock tags out of chronological order during inspection.
CDI - PIC corrected tag storage order during inspection.
1 pt.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed cut cabbage in salad prep void of label identifying date which it was prepared. Observed french onion soup in WIC baring prep date of 6/24/26, despite the veal stock it is made from being prepped on 6/20/26. 
CDI - Cabbage voluntarily discarded. REHS educated PIC on proper date marking of items prepared with ingredients made or opened on days prior.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

rows.general_comments:
Repairs will be occurring to main dish room in the coming weeks. EHS has discussed plan with PIC if/when the large dish machine will need to be shut off due to construction repairs. PIC will send EHS written procedures for if this occurs. Facility will be able to utilize numerous high temp dish units located in facility to accommodate for missing dish unit. EHS will review plan and discuss further with PIC. PIC stated no banquets are scheduled for the next week. 

Facility has 4 single door high temp dish machines on same floor as gallery and banquet kitchen, as well as single door high temp units on floor 2 and ground floor levels. PIC stated all are in good repair, and temps have been checked daily to ensure temperatures are met. Facility also has surplus of plates, silverware, and glassware available if needed to ensure adequate clean dishes are available at all times. 

A lot of TCS items have been reconsolidated into WIC storage. PIC stated three reach in coolers will be removed and want to replace unit with three compartment sink. EHS stated PIC should contact permitting senior to discuss plans.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed diced tomatoes and boiled eggs cooling at inadequate rates in WIC #4. EHS observed both items cooling in cambros with tight fitting lids. CDI - PIC removed lids and instructed food employee to place items in ice bath and stir to quickly cool. REPEAT. (1)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. EHS observed make unit in gallery kitchen in disrepair. PIC stated unit is in the process of being repaired. EHS observed signage posted on equipment and no food present inside unit. EHS observed high temp single door dish machine in disrepair during inspection, EHS observed signage posted. PIC stated engineers are onsite to repair unit but have to wait on exterior construction to finish. Facility has larger dish unit downstairs that is mainly used. (0)

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. EHS observed gaskets on equipment in gallery kitchen soiled with food residue and debris. REPEAT. (0.5)

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. EHS observed wall by hand wash station becoming brittle and breaking apart near the bottom of the wall; this has also created a space between the floor tiling and the wall. EHS observed the tile floor trimming beginning to expand away from the wall creating a large gap between the trimming and walling. Facility has tried to fill with insulation but it is not a sufficient barrier or seal. Repair. REPEAT. 

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. EHS observed ceiling tiles near the back area of dish pit that are porous. EHS advised to replace tiles with smooth and durable ceiling tiles that can be easily clean.REPEAT. (0.5)

***EHS will de-escalate points due to improvement. Facility has replaced/repaired numerous items from previous inspection: tiling by tilt skillet and entering banquet kitchen, ceiling tiles throughout banquet hallway, and partial walling in dish room area. By next inspection, remaining items should be repaired according to PIC. Dish room is scheduled for repairs to commence in the coming weeks.

rows.general_comments:
Due to CDP glitch error unable to mark the following items out on inspection form above: 
#25 - 3-603.11 - Ensure consumer advisory is provided for all under cooked food items. Facility recently printed new menus. Consumer advisory for smoked salmon Benedict does not clearly indicate that smoked salmon is also an undercooked item in addition to the poached eggs. Recommend to change the disclosure on the menu from “cooked to order” to “items may be served raw or undercooked” to clarify that smoked salmon is a raw product that is not further cooked prior to serving. 
 
#49- 4-602.13 - Non -food contact surfaces shall be clean. Observed some general cleaning needed for drawers on cook line and back splash of mixer. 
 
#55- 6-201.11 - Floors, walls and ceilings shall be easily cleanable. Observed some grout missing between floor tiles near dish machine. 
 
Facility cooks and cools more than 4 items (chicken for chicken salad, ground sausage, spicy sausage and various sauces.). Risk 
category will be changed from risk 3 to risk 4.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed uncovered beverage stored on top of microwave and another beverage stored on counter where food prep occurs. CDI- beverages discarded or relocated.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P. Observed box of moldy lemons in reach in cooler. PIC was aware and was waiting to return to vendor for credit. Recommend to keep separate from product in use. CDI- sign placed on lemons to not use/for return.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw shell eggs stored in grill drawers above pasteurized liquid eggs. CDI- items relocated. Observed raw shrimp stored over package 
of deli turkey and pre-cooked sausages in walk in cooler. CDI- items relocated. 
 
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed box of unwashed asparagus stored over container of salsa in walk in cooler. CDI- relocated. 
 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation 
 
3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P. Observed employee wearing gloves crack raw shell eggs and then move to handle bread for toast without changing gloves. CDI- discussed proper glove changing procedure with employee who then also discarded gloves, washed hands and donned clean gloves.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed tomato slicer with food debris. CDI- placed at dish area to be rewashed. 
 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , 
and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed cooked breakfast potatoes under heat lamp at100F. CDI- potatoes reheated to above 165F. Recommend to place in steam table to maintain 135F and above or use TPHC. 
 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS 
Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed green chilis, sliced ham, goat cheese and several other items in units on line without dates. Observed date marking procedures not being followed for freezing and thawing of foods. Verification required to review overall date marking procedures. 
 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed andouille sausage, ground sausage, gumbo, corn, ham, lobster, crawfish and other foods in units on line with date labels exceeding 7 days. CDI- all products discarded. 
 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at 
the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely 
https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

Observed working container of powdered sugar and large container of flour unlabeled.

Observed green chilis, sliced ham, goat cheese and several other items in units on line without dates. Observed date marking procedures not being followed for freezing and thawing of foods. Verification required to review overall date marking procedures. 
 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed andouille sausage, ground sausage, gumbo, corn, ham, lobster, crawfish and other foods in units on line with date labels exceeding 7 days. CDI- all products discarded. 
 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at 
the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely 
https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

24 - Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P. Observed liquid pasteurized eggs and egg whites next to grill that were being held on time without a time label. CDI- time label added.

37 - Food properly labeled: original container: 3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. 
Observed working container of powdered sugar and large container of flour unlabeled.

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-502.13 Single-use and single-service articles cannot be reused. Observed milk containers in server coolers being reused to store ice tea.

51 - Plumbing installed; proper backflow devices: 5-202.14 Prevent backflow or back siphonage prevention devices installed shall meet American Society of Sanitary Engineering standards. -P. Observed backflow preventer at canwash not rated for continuous use with hose and spray nozzle attached. CDI- spray 
nozzle disconnected.

56 - Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used: 6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity. Observed broken light fixture in walk in cooler resulting in lighting less than 10ft candles.

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings.
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. 
Observed various items all throughout kitchen void of covering or wrapping to protect the foods from potential contamination. Ensure all foods are properly protected from potential contamination. Observed raw repackaged salmon being stored above french fries, and raw repackaged ground beef being stored above raw repackaged steak in RIF during inspection.
CDI - PIC corrected storage order.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed chicken stock with prep date of 6/17 and salsa with prep date of 6/16 in WIC during inspection. Observed portioned salsas in RIC baring prep date of 6/25 despite salsa being on an earlier date.
CDI - Chicken stock and salsa voluntarily discarded. REHS educated PIC on proper date marking of portioned items.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (B)(2) Consumer Advisory Disclosures that are asterisked to a footnote shall state, * Items are served raw or undercooked, or contain (or may contain) raw or undercooked ingredients. -Pf
Observed dish served with fried egg void of asterisk leading customer to consumer advisory during inspection. Addition necessary.
10 day VR required.
0.5 pts.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed heavy food debris build up on shelving units in WIC during inspection. More thorough or frequent cleaning necessary to preclude pest harborage and/or cross contamination.
Repeat. 0.5 pts.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed various cracked floor tiles throughout kitchen area during inspection. Repair or replacement necessary.
0 pts.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF PIC could not provide employee health policy or proof that employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms and illnesses. CDI - PIC educated about employee health and given sample health policy to go over with employees. 0 pts.

For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Gravy (131F) and chicken (125F) measured below 135F in hot holding. TCS foods in hot holding shall measure at least 135F. CDI - PIC reheated items to above 165F. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Cassava leave in upright cooler (45F), rice (44F) and green rice (51F). TCS foods in cold holding shall measure 41F or less. CDI - PIC stated that these items are pulled out frequently to prepare orders which caused the food to warm up. The kitchen is extremely hot because the AC isn't working so ensure food is not left out and minimize opening doors to heat of kitchen doesn't warm food above 41F.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. Establishment is using TPHC for fufu. No written procedures are available. CDI - PIC e-mailed TPHC form and he filled it out during inspection. 0 pts.

TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.comments: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ . Observed a large container of chicken salad being stored in glass door RI in sandwich prep area and a bowl of French toast batter being stored in prep reach in cooler at cook line both above 41F. See temp chart. CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded French toast batter and placed chicken salad in walk in cooler to rapidly cool. REPEAT. Points not escalated due to improvement since previous inspection.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU . Observed plastic covered containers of chicken salad sandwiches cooling in glass door reach in cooler in sandwich prep area in which chicken salad was cooling at a rate insufficient to reach cooling parameters. See temp chart. CDI: PIC moved chicken salad sandwiches to walk in cooler to rapidly cool.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed a large spoon being stored in a container of cooked potatoes in flip top cooler at cook line in which handle of spoon was resting in potatoes. CDI: PIC removed spoon and replaced with a spoon with handle positioned above food. REPEAT.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed a large scoop being stored in a container of flour in which handle of scoop was buried in flour. CDI: PIC removed scoop from flour.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used

violations.comments: 2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P EHS observed food employee handling raw chicken removing gloves and donning new gloves to begin working with RTE foods with no hand wash. EHS intervened. CDI - Food employee washed hands. (2)

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P EHS observed food employee preparing soup with one gloved hand and barehand. EHS observed bare hand being used to top soup with RTE ingredients. EHS intervened. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded both soups and re-made entrees. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. EHS observed large containers blocking access to hand sink. This is the only available hand sink in main kitchen area. CDI - PIC removed containers to allow access. (1)

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P EHS observed raw fish stored above RTE sauces in make unit reach in. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC removed plastic-arranged storage. No contamination observed. (0)

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) EHS observed today’s date on all items in both make units. PIC stated they mark items for the day it was placed into unit, not the day it was made. PIC stated they can verify the date it was made to ensure it is not held beyond 7 days. PIC stated in previous inspections, facility was told this was within code. EHS provided education. Facility will date mark all ingredients in make units with the day the product was made moving forward. EHS allowed PIC to verify all items in reach coolers and provide accurate dates for all items in make unit. EHS observed small trays prepared to accompany soup entrees. These small trays contain various TCS items to be used with the soup. EHS observed the same date marking process with the trays as seen with the make units. PIC stated trays are dated for the day they were prepared. EHS provided education stating that unless each item is dated individually, or items are discarded daily, the oldest date shall be reflected and used as the correct date mark for the trays to ensure compliance with date marking. PIC adjusted date to reflect this. EHS will return in 3 days to observe compliance. If incorrect dates are observed on items during VR, items will be required to be discarded. (1.5)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed raw chicken cooling in 2-door RI at inadequate rate(see temp chart). EHS observed chicken cooling in reach in cooler tightly wrapped with plastic wrap. CDI - PIC removed plastic wrap and stated will stir frequently to allow item to cool. EHS verified adequate cooling rate prior to end of inspection. EHS observed one item cooling adequately during inspection and observed some improvement with awareness of the cooling process. EHS advised to continue to coach employees on proper cooling methods. If facility does not continue to improve, a risk control plan could be placed. REPEAT. (1)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. EHS observed food employee preparing food without proper hair restraint. REPEAT. (0.5)

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. EHS observed scoop for rice stored in water at room temperature. CDI - PIC removed spoon from water and placed in empty container. (0)

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed hand washing near FOH with food debris and build up inside hand washing sink. CDI - PIC cleaned hand washing sink.

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-602.11 (A) (1) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, before each use with a different type of raw animal food. P Observed five serving spoons stored clean near 3-comp-sink with sticky like texture and purple serving spoon with food debris and build up. Observed container stored cleaned with food build up on the inside of container. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded container and moved serving spoons back to 3-comp-sink to be rewash. PIC stated the serving spoons will no longer be used after cleaning items they were removed from use 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. - Observed no CFPM on site at the time of the inspection. PIC stated that no one who works, there including the owners, possess a ServSafe certificate or other food managers certificate. REPEAT.

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. - Observed a scrubbing brush to be stored in the back kitchen area handwash sink basin.

CDI - Scrubbing brush removed from sink basin.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed a container of sliced tomatoes to be at temperatures of 44-46F in the opentop refrigerator. PIC stated that they do not store anything in the opentop overnight and it gets filled around 8:30am each morning. REPEAT.
- Observed a tray of meatballs to be sitting on a rolling cart in front of the bread ovens, outside of any temperature control and at temperatures of 44-51F. PIC stated that they took it out at the same time the opentop was stocked and were waiting for the water to heat up in the steamtable before they put the meatballs into the microwave to reheat. EHS advised staff that they cannot keep the meatballs out while waiting for the steamtable to heat up and they must keep them in the WIC during that process.

CDI - PIC moved all out of temperature items to the WIC to rapidly cool.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. - Observed a gap at the base of the back door to the facility, allowing for the potential entry of vermin into the facility.

6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. - Observed one live roach on the floor between the 3 compartment sink and the WIC.

6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. - Observed a dead roach in a small plastic container adjacent the back door of the facility.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. - Observed cases of water and cases of bagged chips to be stacked on the floor in the dry storage area.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. - Observed the handwash sink in the back kitchen area to be falling off the wall and to be in need of re-caulking. REPEAT.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the gaskets to the front counter lowboy refrigerator to have accumulations of food debris/black grime. REPEAT.
- Observed the fan cover in the WIC to have accumulations of of dust/debris.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. - Observed the floors beneath the dry storage racks to have accumulations of food debris/packaging/grime. REPEAT.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed PIC not certified as a food protection manager. No staff member on site with certification during inspection.






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed employee fill up container with water for steam well in handsink. CDI: EHS educated employee on handwashing sink limitations and employee relocated to prep sink to continue getting water for steam well.

4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code, P. Observed cutting board surface at pizza line soiled with food debris. PIC stated that this surface is cleaned once a day, sometimes every other day. PIC stated that there have been no pizzas made today at this station. Observed pizza paddle with heavy accumulation of build up and not yet used today. Observed dicer with food debris accumulation present from the day prior. Employee stated that this item was not used today. CDI: PIC took cutting surface, dicer and pizza paddle to 3-comp sink for W/R/S. EHS educated PIC and staff on cleaning frequently of food contact surfaces. 





For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed queso and marinara in hot well with temperatures below 135F. Employee stated that they were reheated in the steam oven before being placed in hot well 2 hours prior. Hot well had little water in it. CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded queso and reheated a new bag and reheated marinara. Both Items reached over 165F before being placed in steam well and more water added to steam well.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed container of jalapeño poppers in RIC without a date label. Employees could not verify when item was prepared. Observed ground beef in pizza fliptop with date 6/13. PIC stated that it was cooked on that day then frozen and pulled from the freezer two days ago. Observed marinara sauce put into a secondary container from bulk container in the walk in. New container was dated for today and bulk batch was prepared yesterday. CDI: Jalapeño poppers voluntarily discarded. Ground beef label added with date it was pulled from freezer. Marinara date was corrected to reflect the original prep date. EHS provided education on proper date marking procedures.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed food employee working with food without a hair restraint.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed inside of multiple prep units with debris and residue accumulation. Increase cleaning frequency. Observed multiple plastic containers with two different date stickers on it in fliptop unit. PIC stated that the old date stickers were not removed during the washing process. CDI: PIC changed out containers and took them to get stickers removed and W/R/S. REPEAT

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf. Observed food items in the WI cooler, cooling in tightly covered containers. CDI. PIC removed the covers. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Toilet facilities: properly constructed, supplied & cleaned
violations.comments:
6-202.14 A toilet room located on the premises shall be completely enclosed and provided with a tight-fitting and self-closing door.
Observed both bathrooms without a self closing door. PIC to replace closing mechanism for doors.

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
EHS observed a few pans with visible food debris present. 
CDI- Items taken to dish area to be w/r/s. 
**REPEAT** -1.5-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
EHS observed 1 pan of diced tomatoes with no date mark. EHS observed 1 container of cooked brisket with a prep date of 06/16. 
CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded of the items. 
-0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-602.11 (B) (1)-(4)Food packaged on premises for customer self service shall include a label with the (1) common name of the food; (2) ingredients and sub ingredients in descending order; (3) quantity of contents; (4) name and place of business.
 EHS observed labels for customer self service foods in RIF do not have the facility address and net quantity of contents. Ingredients must be labeled on the packaging so ‘seasoning’ must be completely listed. 
**REPEAT** -1-

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf
EHS observed several dented cans stored with nondented cans in dry storage. PIC stated that these cans are returned for credit. 
CDI- EHS provided education and cans were segregated for return. 
-0-

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. 
EHS observed deep pits in the flooring throughout the kitchen. Repair. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure.
EHS observed heavy food build up on the exterior of ac unit in kitchen. This is directly above a food prep area. EHS observed food debris on ceiling tiles in the kitchen. More frequent cleaning is recommended.

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P
2-301.14 (F) Wash hands during food preparation, as often as necessary to remove soil and contamination, and to prevent cross contamination when changing tasks. -P - Observed dishwasher handling soiled equipment then proceeding to handle clean equipment on clean drainboard of dish machine without washing hands in between. CDI, employee washed hands.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed block cheese overstocked at chill line at 46 degrees F. Observed all TCS foods in top prep unit on line which had been turned off at night per PIC and loaded in morning foods cold held between 49-=52 degrees F. CDI, all foods voluntarily discarded as foods in unit at 6am per operator . Repeat. Due to repeat violation on risk factor, if facility is not in compliance with cold holding on next regulatory inspection a Risk Control Plan will be requested. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed cut melons and tomato’s with discard date of 6/22. CDI, foods voluntarily discarded .

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P - Observed dairy creamers and milk held on TPHC time exceeded the 4 hour discard time of 10:00am. CDI, foods voluntarily discarded and containers changed out.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed 1 container of sink and surface sanitizer testing less than required concentration of lactic acid.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments:
5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place. Observed drain plug missing from outdoor dumpster.

5-501.110 Keep waste stored in receptacles so waste is inaccessible to insects and rodents. Observed dumpster door open .

rows.violation_text:
8 - Hands clean & properly washed: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P
2-301.14 (F) Wash hands during food preparation, as often as necessary to remove soil and contamination, and to prevent cross contamination when changing tasks. -P - Observed dishwasher handling soiled equipment then proceeding to handle clean equipment on clean drainboard of dish machine without washing hands in between. CDI, employee washed hands.

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed block cheese overstocked at chill line at 46 degrees F. Observed all TCS foods in top prep unit on line which had been turned off at night per PIC and loaded in morning foods cold held between 49-=52 degrees F. CDI, all foods voluntarily discarded as foods in unit at 6am per operator . Repeat. Due to repeat violation on risk factor, if facility is not in compliance with cold holding on next regulatory inspection a Risk Control Plan will be requested. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed cut melons and tomato’s with discard date of 6/22. CDI, foods voluntarily discarded .

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

24 - Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P - Observed dairy creamers and milk held on TPHC time exceeded the 4 hour discard time of 10:00am. CDI, foods voluntarily discarded and containers changed out.

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed 1 container of sink and surface sanitizer testing less than required concentration of lactic acid.

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place. Observed drain plug missing from outdoor dumpster.

5-501.110 Keep waste stored in receptacles so waste is inaccessible to insects and rodents. Observed dumpster door open .

CDI - over temp items were placed in WIC to rapidly cool.
