rows.general_comments:
USING TPHC:
1. Ensure food starts at 41F or below.
2. Label foods for no more than 6 hours (if the food will not exceed 70F- 4 hours if it will)
3. Dispose all remaining at the end of the time window. Do NOT cool and reheat.
4. Full out and retain these procedures onsite: https://mecknc.widen.net/s/pvtc855zds/tphc-template-english

violations.comments:
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink.
Observed no posted hand sink signs.
CDI- EHS provided county signs at both hand sinks. (-0)

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed one container of white rice in WIC cooked yesterday at 45F, which failed to cool. EHS provided education to both present staff members about cooling methods. 
CDI- Rice which failed to cool on time voluntarily disposed. (-1.5)

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed cooked potatoes at 103F in steamwell.
CDI- Potatoes reheated above 165F and returned to steamwell with lid. All other steamwell items in compliance. (-0)

REPEAT.
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed sliced spinach, lettuce, couscous, chorizo, feta cheese, diced tomato, and mayo in cold well/lowboy above 41F. See temp chart. 
CDI- Items rapidly cooled in WIC. EHS advises that lowboy was overfilled past its capacity, and cold air cannot circulate if fans are blocked. Ambient air in unit observed at 40F. Work order should be placed and TPHC should be used while awaiting repair. Open lid make top should be maintained with cabinet air between 35F-38F to maintain TCS foods at 41F or below. 

*Risk Control Plan or permit action may be taken if cold holding is out of compliance at the next inspection, due to repeat violations.

REPEAT.
3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed several undated pans of rice cooked today or yesterday in WIC. Observed multiple containers of shredded lettuce cooling, sliced tomato, and TCS bottled dressings prepped yesterday or today without date marks.
CDI- EHS confirmed prep date with chef who prepared. Items with unknown prep date voluntarily disposed. Rice and sliced lettuce dated. (-1.5)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
REPEAT.
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed numerous containers of rice cooling in WIC with parchment placed on top of rice, followed by lids, and contains stacked 3-4 high. Lower containers are not able to cool in these conditions. Provide shallow, uncovered, well spaced containers for cooling TCS foods. 
CDI- Cooling methods discussed with PIC. Failed cool rice voluntarily disposed.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
Observed unlabeled oil mixture (non-TCS confirmed) in squirt bottle at make top, and small containers of decanted herbs on dry storage rack without labels. Label all spices and herbs with working name. (-1)

violations.comments:

violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf.

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. No CFPM manager on duty during inspection. REPEAT

6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink.
Observed sink used for handwashing without sign. CDI- EHS provided handwashing sign.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Observed pre packaged deli meats, sausage patties, and egg patties being maintained without date labeling. PIC also stated items without dates were opened, frozen, and thawed to be used on future date without providing dates to reflect 7-day parameter. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded deli meats frozen and thawed past parameters, provided date marking for items known to be within parameters. *REPEAT*

-No live shellstock onsite; oysters, clams, mussels arrive frozen and fully cooked. 
-Tablet must be provided to all tables while ordering; paper menu does not include complete consumer advisory. PIC states that all ordering is done through the iPads.

violations.comments:
REPEAT.
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed ice inside hand sink basin closest to burners where soups are cooked/heated. 
CDI- EHS discusses not using hand sink as dump sink with PIC. Basin sanitized. (-2)

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed unlabeled spray bottle of de-greaser. 
CDI- Bottle labeled. (-0)

REPEAT.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed multiple wiping cloths throughout kitchen in use without sani-buckets set up for their storage. EHS directs PIC to set up buckets and speak to staff about need to do this each morning prior to using cloths. (-0.5)

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted.
Observed round metal containers nesting while wet on clean dish rack.
CDI- Dishes spaced to dry. (-0)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed quat dispenser at main kitchen 3 comp sink not operational. EHS notes there is another 3 comp sink, and high temp dish machine, that can be used in meantime. Quat may also be manually added. PIC states they will call Ecolab to place work order. (-0)

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed no certified food protection manager onsite during inspection. PIC and employees demonstrated knowledge by answering questions during inspection and having no priority violations or citations during inspection. REPEAT.






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed one (1) package of unopened RTE chicken held within bin at room temperature. Chicken was observed as frozen. Per conversation with PIC, frozen chicken can not be thawed via running water due semipermeable commercial ROP bag. CDI - PIC rotated chicken to WIC to thaw under refrigeration.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed approximately three (3) fruit flies at non food contact area adjacent to proofing cabinets. Observed no contact with food or clean equipment and utensils by insects. Continue work with licensed pest control to prevent pest entry.

violations.description: Toilet facilities: properly constructed, supplied & cleaned
violations.comments: 5-501.17 Provide a covered waste bin in female restrooms. Observed covered waste bin in female restroom with broken latch, unable to properly close. Replace with properly covered waste bin as needed.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place. Observed cardboard dumpster with no drain plug in place. Contact landlord and/or waste management company to replace missing drain plug.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed minor rust on walls of WIC. Resurface and/or cover such that no rust or corrosion is present within WIC.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-403.11 (A)Areas designated for employees to eat and drink, shall be located so that food, equipment, linens, single service/use items are protected from contamination. Observed one (1) bin of employee food and beverages (unopened energy drinks, sandwich jelly, etc.) stored above one (1) box of unwashed produce (uncut, unwashed onions) in WIC. Store employee food and beverages such that they are not above food held for resale.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed opened raw fish stored above waffles in tall reach in freezer. CDI- Waffles removed and placed above. Observed opened bagged raw fish stored with cooked turkey legs in tall reach in freezer. Turkey legs were unpackaged and stored in a container with bagged fish. CDI- Fish removed from turkey legs and placed below. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed sliced tomatoes, oxtails and milk in tall reach in cooler without a date. CDI- Items dated. Once prepared or opened keep items dated.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed shelving throughout in need of cleaning. Observed bins housing seasoning and flour in need of cleaning throughout.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed damaged tiles under prep table near fryers. Observed cove base missing from wall under prep sink. Observed corner guard missing from wall across from hand sink. Paneling is pulling away from the wall where the corner guard is missing.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:
3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P
Observed containers of strawberries, a container of raspberries, and herbs that were adulterated with white fuzzy build up. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the necessary items. (1 pt)

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed the guide of the deli slicer soiled with residue/debris. Increase cleaning frequency/clean every 4 hours. Due to severity, points not taken. (0 pts) 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

Observed a container off salsa that had been date marked 5/25/2026. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the salsa. Due to severity, points not taken. (0 pts)

rows.general_comments: EHS conducted walk through of bar area with PIC. PIC stated bar prepares non-alcoholic beverages and provide sodas to guests at the bar as well. EHS discussed any observations observed to be corrected for next inspection.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P EHS observed raw salmon over RTE foods in kitchen low boy. EHS observed raw tuna stored over kani salad in sushi low boy. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC rearranged storage order. No contamination was observed. (1.5)

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-602.11 (A) (4) Food contact surface of a thermometer shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, before using or storing. P. EHS observed thermometer stored on ice machine at sushi bar soiled with food debris and residue. CDI - PIC removed thermometer from service to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (0)

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed shredded fandito cheese, soft shell crab, and meatballs cooling at inadequate rates (see temp chart). EHS observed items cooling in reach in units of make tables, covered with tight fitting plastic wrap. PIC stated items were prepared at 4pm and placed into units. PIC stated this is not the correct procedure. Items should be thinned out on sheet trays in WIC to cool. CDI - PIC provided education to staff, food employees moved items onto sheet pans and into freezer to quickly cool. (0.5)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. EHS observed foods in dry storage stored in boxes on the floor. EHS advised to elevate boxes six inches off ground. (0)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed cheese ravioli and chicken egg rolls (6/14) held longer than 7 days.
CDI - Items voluntarily discarded.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

35 - Approved thawing methods used: 3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water.
Observed salmon thawing in ROP.
CDI - Salmon was removed from ROP.

40 - Personal cleanliness: 2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils.
Observed all food employees working in the kitchen with no hair restraints.
CDI - Food employees put on hair restraints.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container.
Observed cup, used as scoop submerged in marinara sauce.
CDI - Cup was removed.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted.
Observed metal pans stacked wet.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf
Observed multiple cracked spatulas.
CDI - PIC discarded.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
EHS observed multiple items in lowboy drawers (under hot holding well) were above 41F. Ambient air temperature of the unit was 49F on EHS thermometer and 51F on facility thermometer that is kept in the unit. After 30 minutes there was no change in temperature. PIC voluntarily discarded all TCS food. Facility is not going to use the unit until it has been repaired. Facility is going to use other cold holding unit until it is repaired. PIC stated Vendor will be onsite tomorrow morning.
-1.5- **3 Day VR**

rows.general_comments: EHS provided PIC with updated employee health policy in both English and Spanish.

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments:
3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf
Observed shellstock tags not in chronological order for past 90 days.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pan of raw shrimp stored directly behind a pan of bacon jam, stored inside the drawers of make table 2. 
CDI: PIC corrected storage order to prevent contamination.

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. 
Observed pan of raw chicken milanesa stored directly above a pan full of raw marinated lamb, inside the lowboy portion of make table 3.
CDI: PIC corrected storage order to prevent contamination.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed pan of sliced pepperoni and pan of sliced prosciutto both missing date labels and stored inside the lowboy portion of make table 1. Food employee stated both TCS items were prepared yesterday. 
CDI: Date labels were created.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed pan of cooked mushrooms date-marked with a prep date of 6/10, stored inside make table 1. PIC stated this was the correct date of prep.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded cooked mushrooms.
Observed pan of diced tomatoes date-marked with a prep date of 6/16, stored inside make table 1. PIC stated that was not correct and actual date of prep was 3 days prior (6/22).
CDI: PIC removed date label and created new date label with correct prep date.
Observed pan of cooked potatoes date-marked with a prep date of 6/18, stored inside make table 2. PIC stated this was the correct date of prep.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded potatoes.
Observed pan of crab cakes date-marked with prep date of 6/18, stored inside make table 2. PIC stated this is a food item they freeze then thaw, and stated they thawed this item on Tuesday (6/23).
CDI: PIC created second date label to depict the "clock start" date.
Observed pan of tomato cream date-marked with prep date of 6/16, stored inside the WIC. PIC stated this was not correct and was actually prepared on Friday (6/19). 
CDI: PIC removed date label and created new date label with correct prep date.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P

3-DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf
Observed missing time stamps for Hollandaise. Observed “7-12” (5-hour) time stickers for pancake batters. CDI - New time stickers were written for the time items were removed from temperature control.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-202.12(A) Use poisonous or toxic materials according to manufacturer's label. Use pesticides approved for use in commercial food service only.-P
Observed a can of RAID in the back kitchen that is not approved for commercial kitchen use. CDI - Items was removed from the kitchen.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
REPEAT
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry.
Observed a large light gap at the bottom of the back restaurant door. No points taken due to an unsuccessful sweep being installed on the bottom of the door.

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
Observed an employee preparing food while wearing two bracelets.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed a cup and a scoop with handle submerged inside a bin of flour.

violations.comments:
REPEAT
4-901.11(A) Air dry equipment and utensils after cleaning and sanitizing.

4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized.
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.

Observed missing tiles and low grout in front of the 3-compartment sink and dish machine.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed one metal container of sausages on HH container of sausages were on HH for approximately 1 and a half hours per food employee. Sausage at the top of the container was 119F sausage at the bottom of the container was 144F. Container was over filled. -CDI- PIC reheated sausage to 165F prior to placing back on HH. Ensure that all HH food is maintained at 135F and above. 
All other HH food was properly holding. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed several foods in the facilities grill drawers above 41F. The grill drawers were holding an ambient temperature of approximately 43F. Foods that were above 41F in the grill drawers and that were not placed into the unit this morning were discarded. 
Facility is going to use emergency TPHC on the grill drawers until repaired. Once food leaves the WIC they will start the 4 hour TPHC window. 
-CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded; using emergency TPHC until repaired. 
EHS WILL MAKE RETURN VISIT. 
3-DAY VR



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed ambient prepped ham and sausage in lidded deli cups cooling at 43F in the WIC, cooling food was still at 43F 15 minutes later. Ensure that food is un-lidded during the cooling process. -CDI-Food was un-lidded and moved to the WIF to properly cool. Food cooled by the end of the inspection. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments: 6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf- Observed dented can with dent present on the cans seam intermingled with other cans. Ensure that if cans are dented they are stored separate and apart from other food and food contact surfaces when being held for credit. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the can.

violations.comments:

Observed ice being dumped inside of the front counter hand sink. CDI - Employees were educated and ice was removed.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:

3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. Pf

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P. Observed raw beef being stored above cooked beef inside lowboy freezer in 12th floor kitchen. Raw beef was removed from hermetically sealed packaging. CDI: Storage order was rearranged. 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-703.11(B) After being cleaned in a mechanical dish machine, equipment food-contact surfaces and utensils shall be sanitized in the dish machine by achieving a utensil surface temperature of 160F as measured by a max/min thermometer.-P. Observed dish machine reaching a maximum temperature of 149F after running multiple cycles. Facility will be using the 3 comp sink to wash, rinse, sanitize. VERIFICATION REQUIRED. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following weblink: Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed raw hamburger patties stored above RTE cooked chicken wings in cordial RIC during inspection.
CDI - PIC corrected order.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed various foods in WIC holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart. Ambient in unit 40F.
CDI - All out of temperature foods voluntarily discarded. REHS recommended reorganizing the WIC to place non-TCS food items under the condenser/in front of the door, and pushing all TCS foods deeper into the cooler where cold air flows better. REHS additionally recommended adding a plastic curtain over the door to ensure cold air remains in the unit while it is open.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed cut lettuce in cordial RIC void of date label, farro in cordial drawers with a discard date of 6/23/26, and cooked chicken in cordial RIC with discard date of 6/22/26 during inspection.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded all items.
Repeat. 1.5 pts.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

rows.general_comments: Establishment does not serve sushi or use live shellstock. Establishment now serves Ramen but does not serve soft boiled eggs. Eggs are fully cooked.

violations.comments:
2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P Observed employee wash hands and then turn off faucet without using a barrier. CDI - Employee was instructed to rewash hands. Proper handwashing procedure was reviewed with PIC. -0 points-

For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw steak stored above large bucket of chicken flavor soup base in walk in cooler. CDI - Storage order was corrected. -0 points-

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. Observed heavy black build up on inner surfaces of ice machine. Recommend higher frequency and more thorough cleaning. -0 points-

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed large covered bus tub of white rice (date marked 6/23) in walk in cooler with an internal temperature of 50F. PIC stated that white rice was placed in walk in cooler day prior to cool and has not been removed. Observed two stacked and covered large bus tubs of lo mein noodles cooling in walk in cooler. Initial temperature was 83F. A second temperature of 80F was recorded after 45 minutes of cooling. CDI - Container of white rice was discarded. Lo mein noodles were uncovered and unstacked to allow cold air to circulate in and around food. -REPEAT- -3 points-

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed open package of commercially cooked pork dumplings and tray of raw beef in flip top cooler holding at 50F and 51F. External thermometer of flip top cooler read 54F while an internal ambient temperature was measured at 50F. CDI - TCS foods were discarded and flip top cooler was taken out of service. PIC has been advised to conduct frequent temperature checks of TCS foods and equipment. -0 points- 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed a large bus tub of white rice that failed to meet cooling parameters from day prior and was discarded. Observed stacked and covered bus tubs of lo mein noodles that failed to meet cooling parameters during inspection. CDI - Rice was discarded and lo mein was unstacked and uncovered to facilitate rapid cooling. Cooling methods were discussed with PIC. -REPEAT- -1 point-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed bus tubs used to store TCS foods in walk in cooler with broken chips and cracks. Discard equipment that is chipped, cracked, or broken and replace. Observed flip top cooler unable to maintain TCS foods at 41F or less.

4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving throughout facility including in food prep areas, walk in cooler, and in dry storage areas. Resurface or replace shelving so that it is smooth and easily cleanable. - REPEAT- -1 point
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed pipe under mechanical dish machine leaking. Establishment is using bucket to catch leaking water. Repair plumbing. -0 points-

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed food splash, debris, and residues on flooring and walls under and near cooking equipment in kitchen. Recommend higher frequency and more thorough cleaning. 

6-501.114 Discard items and litter stored on the premises as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance. Observed equipment in various states of repair that look no longer used. Observed piles of equipment stored in dry storage areas and near can wash. Observed tools stored on shelving near walk in cooler. Observed several large soiled cooling units stored in kitchen and in hallway. Remove equipment that is no longer necessary to operation or maintenance of establishment. -REPEAT- -1 point-

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed foods in lowboy freezer holding above 41F during inspection. Unit had been unplugged. All items voluntarily discarded and unit plugged back in. Observed dill cream sauce over stacked in fryer prep and holding above 41F. Sauce voluntarily discarded. Observed most TCS foods in RIC and WIC holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart. Both units unable to maintain ambient temp at or below 41F while in use, but were able to achieve below 41F after remaining closed for ~20 minutes. PIC stated gasket issues are the suspected cause for cold holding issues in RIC. Condenser in WIC is visibly frozen over and audibly struggling. Various TCS foods in RIC voluntarily discarded due to date marking violations. Foods in WIC dropped in temperature and were allowed to be kept. Maintenance contacted during inspection. Facility to maintain temperature log for WIC until REHS return.
72 hour VR required.
1.5 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf - Observed the TPHC log for the items held on the counter top (including fish roe and condiment bottles) to not be filled out at the time of the inspection. Employee stated that they open at 7am and all the items are placed on the counter at around 6:30am every morning and it gets discarded by 10:30am. EHS reminded front counter employee that it is important to maintain the logs as the items are brought out of refrigeration.

CDI - Front counter employee filled out TPHC log for today, with the discard times being labeled for 10:30am.

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. - Observed a large plastic container of ROP tuna that were thawed in the 2 door lowboy at the front counter. Packages thawing instructions stated “remove from packaging” and all packages were observed fully intact. REPEAT.

CDI - Front counter employee put holes into all ROP packages of tuna, removing them from their ROP environment.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the wire storage racks in the basement prep room to have accumulations of dust/grime/debris.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. - Observed the walls behind in the basement prep room to have splatter debris/food debris.

Front counter employee stated not having the container out of the opentop and that they swapped the containers about an hour prior and was unsure why it was out of temperature. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC relocated to lowboy refrigerator to rapidly cool.

Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf - Observed the TPHC log for the items held on the counter top (including fish roe and condiment bottles) to not be filled out at the time of the inspection. Employee stated that they open at 7am and all the items are placed on the counter at around 6:30am every morning and it gets discarded by 10:30am. 35 - Approved thawing methods used: 3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. - Observed a large plastic container of ROP tuna that were thawed in the 2 door lowboy at the front counter. violations.comments:
4-703.11(B) After being cleaned in a mechanical dish machine, equipment food-contact surfaces and utensils shall be sanitized in the dish machine by achieving a utensil surface temperature of 160F as measured by a max/min thermometer.-P. 
After checking dish machine final rinse gauge and running department disk thermometer through the machine, it was discovered that disk thermometer showed 148F on final rinse, and in-place thermometer on machine showed final rinse of 139-140F. Person running dish machine needs to know what to look for when processing utensils. Facility also needs to purchase a disk thermometer in order to monitor maximum temperature of final rinse to be at least 160F. Facility has called company so that they can repair the dish machine. Utensils must be processed through 3-compartment sink to properly wash, rinse, and sanitize. VR visit must be made within 3 days to see that dish machine is properly running and properly sanitizing. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following weblink: Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed par-cooked chicken wings at 46F in refrigerator; conversation with kitchen manager as well as observation of other food items in this refrigerator shows the appearance that these wings were likely left out on a counter before being placed in refrigerator. Chicken wings come from reach-in freezer where they are at 32F, and all other items in the refrigerator where the chicken wings were at 46 were at 39F. Assure that items are removed from bag and that bag is then placed quickly back into refrigerator. CDI by items being placed on sheet tray into walk-in freezer, where they were later measured at 31F.


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments: 4-901.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils may not be cloth dried. Observed staff person using a towel to dry pans. These must be placed onto rack for thorough draining and air-drying. CDI by running items back through dish machine.

violations.description: Hot & cold water available; adequate pressure
violations.comments: 5-103.11(B) Provide sufficient hot water to meet the peak hot water demands.-Pf. Hot water at hand sinks only has one combined inlet for water rather than an adjustable hot and cold faucet. Water at most hand sinks is 115-124F, too hot at upper temps for proper and safe hand washing. Have adjusted down to between 100-109F. VR visit needed to re-check for proper temperatures.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed numerous TCS items above 41F in 2-door RI (see temp chart). EHS observed ambient temperature of 55F. EHS observed no change in temperature of unit after 30 minutes. PIC stated temperature is checked twice a day. EHS observed temperature logged this morning at 42F. EHS observed internal thermometer at 40F with no change over time. PIC voluntarily discarded all TCS items in unit and discussed issue with maintenance team on site. 3-day VR required.(1.5)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P
Observed one food employee wash his hands multiple times throughout the time of inspection, and not using a protective barrier to turn faucets off after every handwash. All other food prep employees were observed following proper handwashing procedures.
CDI: PIC went over handwashing procedures with food employee and he re-washed his hands to prevent contamination.

6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink.
Observed no handwashing signs posted at any of the handsinks inside the kitchen.
CDI: EHS provided PIC with handwashing signs and PIC posted at each handsink.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pan of raw shrimp stored directly behind a pan of prepared deviled eggs, inside the cooler drawers.
CDI: PIC corrected storage order to prevent contamination.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed pan of beyond meat, pan of pizza sauce, pan of mozzarella cheese, pan of pre-cooked sausage, and pan of roasted garlic all cold holding above 41F inside the flip top portion of make table 1. PIC stated they did not have the lid open prior to start of inspection. Ambient air temp of unit was at 41F. PIC stated the fan inside of the unit will sometimes get blocked by the pans inside, affecting food temps. 
CDI: PIC moved all above mentioned TCS food items into the WIF to rapidly cool down. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (A) Provide consumer advisory for animal foods served raw or under-cooked. Consumer advisory must include disclosure and reminder.-Pf
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed no consumer advisory present on facility's online menu. Facility's physical menu has consumer advisory.
Observed no asterisks on any menu items for facility's physical menu correlating with the footnote. PIC stated caesar dressing is made in-house and includes raw eggs. PIC also stated steak is cooked to temp per order. The following menu items will need asterisks included to be in compliance for facility's consumer advisory: Surf & Turf, Caesar Salad, Side Caesar Salad, Chicken Caesar Wrap, Huevos Rancheros (on brunch column) any option to have caesar dressing as the dressing or sauce for menu item, any option to sub chicken for steak on bowls, and any option to add steak as a protein for salads. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed one unlabeled spray bottle stored at the dish area. PIC stated that the liquid inside the spray bottle was degreaser.
CDI: PIC labeled spray bottle as degreaser for easy identification.

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
Observed food employee actively engaging in food prep with bracelets on.
Ensure food employees remove all jewelry from hands when engaging in food prep to prevent contamination.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used.
Observed stacks of single-serve boats and single-serve bowls stored out of it's original protective packaging, right-side up, along the make line.
Keep all single-serve articles stored inverted when out of it's original protective packaging to prevent contamination.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf
Observed no chlorine test strips to measure the sanitizer concentrations for the chlorine dish machine.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed damaged FRP wall on the left corner of can wash, exposing absorbent material beneath.
Repair damaged FRP wall so that surfaces are smooth and easily cleanable.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture.
Observed missing ceiling tiles above the water heater at the dry storage room.
Replace missing ceiling tiles so that ceilings are smooth and easily cleanable, and to prevent possible entrance of pests.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

rows.violation_text:
8 - Hands clean & properly washed: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P
Observed one food employee wash his hands multiple times throughout the time of inspection, and not using a protective barrier to turn faucets off after every handwash. All other food prep employees were observed following proper handwashing procedures.
CDI: PIC went over handwashing procedures with food employee and he re-washed his hands to prevent contamination.

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink.
Observed no handwashing signs posted at any of the handsinks inside the kitchen.
CDI: EHS provided PIC with handwashing signs and PIC posted at each handsink.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pan of raw shrimp stored directly behind a pan of prepared deviled eggs, inside the cooler drawers.
CDI: PIC corrected storage order to prevent contamination.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed pan of beyond meat, pan of pizza sauce, pan of mozzarella cheese, pan of pre-cooked sausage, and pan of roasted garlic all cold holding above 41F inside the flip top portion of make table 1. PIC stated they did not have the lid open prior to start of inspection. Ambient air temp of unit was at 41F. PIC stated the fan inside of the unit will sometimes get blocked by the pans inside, affecting food temps. 
CDI: PIC moved all above mentioned TCS food items into the WIF to rapidly cool down. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

25 - Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods: 3-603.11 (A) Provide consumer advisory for animal foods served raw or under-cooked. Consumer advisory must include disclosure and reminder.-Pf
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed no consumer advisory present on facility's online menu. Facility's physical menu has consumer advisory.
Observed no asterisks on any menu items for facility's physical menu correlating with the footnote. PIC stated caesar dressing is made in-house and includes raw eggs. PIC also stated steak is cooked to temp per order. The following menu items will need asterisks included to be in compliance for facility's consumer advisory: Surf & Turf, Caesar Salad, Side Caesar Salad, Chicken Caesar Wrap, Huevos Rancheros (on brunch column) any option to have caesar dressing as the dressing or sauce for menu item, any option to sub chicken for steak on bowls, and any option to add steak as a protein for salads. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

Observed one unlabeled spray bottle stored at the dish area. PIC stated that the liquid inside the spray bottle was degreaser.
CDI: PIC labeled spray bottle as degreaser for easy identification.

40 - Personal cleanliness: 2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
Observed food employee actively engaging in food prep with bracelets on.
Ensure food employees remove all jewelry from hands when engaging in food prep to prevent contamination.

Observed stacks of single-serve boats and single-serve bowls stored out of it's original protective packaging, right-side up, along the make line.
Keep all single-serve articles stored inverted when out of it's original protective packaging to prevent contamination.

48 - Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips: 4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf
Observed no chlorine test strips to measure the sanitizer concentrations for the chlorine dish machine.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed damaged FRP wall on the left corner of can wash, exposing absorbent material beneath.
Repair damaged FRP wall so that surfaces are smooth and easily cleanable.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture.

Replace missing ceiling tiles so that ceilings are smooth and easily cleanable, and to prevent possible entrance of pests.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
10-DAY VERIFICATION REUQUIRED
3-603.11 (A) Provide consumer advisory for animal foods served raw or under-cooked. Consumer advisory must include disclosure and reminder. -Pf
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: *Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness. -Pf
Observed missing disclosure, consumer advisory and asterisk for ceviche on the bar menu. Observed missing advisory on the menu board and paper menu. 

The correct, full statement should read: 
*This item is served using raw or undercooked ingredients. Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.” Asterisks should be placed before the statement and before ceviche on the menu.

REPEAT
2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
Observed one employee preparing chips and salsa while wearing an ornate ring.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue.
Observed a utensil for cleaning out salsa containers inside of 70 degree water. EHS recommends storing utensil in an empty cup and washed, rinsed, sanitized every 4 hours.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed increased paint chipping on the floor throughout the kitchen.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
REPEAT
6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity. 10ft candles of light are required for walk-in coolers. Observed 4-6ft candles of light in the walk-in coolers. Improvements made from previous inspection due to brighter bulbs being installed.

violations.comments:
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P 
EHS observed several items in prep units that were not date marked. EHS observed tofu, mushrooms, vegetable dumplings, cut cabbage, and cooked beef with no date marks. 
EHS and PIC observed that items are being dated marked on the plastic wrap used to cover food so when the plastic is removed so is the date mark. 
CDI- EHS allowed PIC and employee to date mark the items as they were present during prep and/or the items could be traced back to larger portions in the walk in/lowboy 
**REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor.
EHS obsevred food stored on the floor of walk in freezer. 
CDI- PIC instructed food employee to move the food off the floor. 
-0-

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
EHS Observed wet wiping cloth stored on counter. 
-0-

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. 
EHS observed scoops for rice stored in water held at room temperature. EHS advised to only hold utensils in water above 135F. 
CDI - PIC removed water from container.
-0.5-

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf 
Observed PIC who was not monitoring to ensure dishes are cleaned and sanitized through the dish machine. PIC was unaware that sanitizer container for dish machine was filled with Clorox instead of chlorine sanitizer. PIC stated they are unsure if new employee was trained on how to check and monitor dish machine. Facility has repeated the same occurence and failed to demonstrate control over maintaining cotaminated equipment and utensils. Verification required. 

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P 
Observed chlorine dish machine with empty sanitizer bottle. PIC state they ran out a few days ago and are manually adding bleach to the machine to wash dishes. Facility unable to use 3 comp sink since stopper for sanitizer bin is inoperational. Facility has voluntarily closed until dish machine is fully repairs. Verification required. Repeat. 

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed heavy cream, mozzerella, eggplant parmigiana, milk, and all different types of pastas in prep top not dated. CDI items dated when opened or prepared. Observed diced tomatoes from 6/16. CDI item discarded. Repeat. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
4-703.11(B) After being cleaned in a mechanical dish machine, equipment food-contact surfaces and utensils shall be sanitized in the dish machine by achieving a utensil surface temperature of 160F as measured by a max/min thermometer.-P. 
Observed high temp dish machine unable to reach 160F after multiple cycles were run. PIC stated last time ECOLAB was on-site for routine maintenance was early this month. PIC contacted ECOLAB rep for repairs. ECOLAB rep stated they will make a visit tomorrow morning. Facility has properly concentrated QUAT sanitizer used at the 3-compartment sink. PIC stated they will only utilize the 3-compartment sink to wash/rinse/sanitize all multi-use equipment and utensils until repairs have been made.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following weblink: Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed no asterisks for consumer advisory on new menu, which includes 5 menu items that are served raw or undercooked. Under "shareable plates" section, spicy tuna crispy rice (for raw tuna chunks). Under "signature entrees" section, Korean power bowl (for sunny side egg). Under "Brunch" section, 1) avocado toast 2) breakfast smashburger 3) fried chicken biscuit sandwich (all for undercooked eggs).
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed two pans of sliced tomatoes cooling at a rate of 0.05 deg/min, inside the blast chiller, unable to meet the cooling rate. Tomatoes were cooling inside the blast chiller in plastic plans that were tightly covered with seran wrap. Date labels showed tomatoes had been just prepared and placed inside to cool.
CDI: PIC removed seran wrap to allow proper cold air flow.
Observed cast iron dishes of portioned out spinach dip cooling at a rate of 0.09 deg/min, inside the drawers of make table 1, unable to meet the cooling rate. Cast iron dishes were covered with seran wrap. Spinach dip was prepared less than 4 hours prior to EHS's observation. 
CDI: PIC removed seran wrap from spinach dip and moved dishes into the blast chiller to rapidly cool down.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf
Observed a couple plastic spatulas with pitting and chipping food-contact surfaces, stored at the clean utensil hooks. 
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded plastic spatulas.

4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed bottom shelf of clean dish rack damaged and storing metal pans at a distance less than 6 inches from the floor.
Repair shelf so that cleaned/sanitized pans can be stored at least 6 inches above the floor to prevent contamination.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed bottled shrimp sauce on prep top without time or temp control, at 50F.
CDI- EHS discusses with staff that shrimp sauce is TCS and requires time and temp control. Shrimp sauces were rapidly cooled in WIC and re-started on 4 hour TPHC once 41F. Shrimp sauces added to TPHC procedure sheet. (-0)

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C)

rows.general_comments:
NOTE: EHS spoke with PIC about air conditioning issue. Air conditioning has not been working at the facility. All units have been affected due to the lack of A/C. EHS inspected Maroon market 6/24/26. During inspection EHS found that the prep top was not functioning as stated my the PIC at Maroon Market. The ambient air temp was 65F. Facility currently has very limited volume and is able to serve out of the cold drawers for now. EHS will verify that the unit is fixed within 3 days. Kitchen area was very hot during inspection. If facilities are not able to safely serve food due to increased temperatures, facilities will have to close. EHS asked to speak with owner of the operation, however PIC did not have a contact.

EHS spoke with several vendors at Monarch market about the air conditioning issue. Several vendors have had issues with keeping up with food temperatures since the air conditioning stopped working. Food safety needs to be maintained at all times. If food safety cannot be maintained, push for compliance will follow. 





For questions please contact EHS at 980.402.4689

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-102.11 (A) - (C )(1), (4)-(16) The Person in charge shall demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this code by (A) Complying with the Food Code by having no violations of Priority Items during the current inspection, (B) Being a certified food protection manager, or (C) by responding correctly to the inspector's questions related to the operation of the food establishment. -Pf. Observed coldholding , storage order, cooling, and date marking out of compliance. EHS will arrive back on site within 10 days to verify active managerial control in items listed above. 




Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed food buildup in pans stored as clean. CDI - pans moved back to dish pit for proper washing

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed pho broth cooling from night before above 41. Ambient temperature of walk-in cooler 44°F. CDI - food was voluntarily discarded. 


For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P observed several TCS food in prep unit above 41. Observed all TCS foods in walk-in cooler above 41. Ambient temperature of unit is 44°F. PIC stated they will call maintenance to assess unit. PIC stated they will voluntarily close facility until walk-in cooler is repaired. All TCS items in walk-in cooler were voluntarily discarded. Establishment shall not open until WIC is cold holding 41F and below. EHS will arrive back on site to verify unit is holding 41 and below.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed, cooked, broccoli, chicken, sausage, sausages, meatballs, cream cheese and noodle with out date marking. CDI - PIC added appropriate dates on all items.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed several items cooling above fill line in prep unit. Observed cooked chicken cooling in 51F water on countertop. Observed broth cooling in large pot in WIC. CDI - broth voluntarily discarded, items in prep unit will be discarded at 9pm



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf observed fish eggs, thawing on counter. CDI - PIC moved to LB for thawing.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed working containers of spices, seasonings, oil, without label labeling.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed sanitizer bucket with 0ppm quat sani. CDI - PIC remade sanitizer bucket.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed avocados with stickers on them. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed containers stored inside product with handle inside food. 
3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed utensils stored in water below 135. CDI - water was dumped out of container. 
3-304.12 (C ) Store in-use utensils on a clean portion of the food preparation table or cooking equipment. The utensils and storage surface must be cleaned and sanitized every 4 hours. Observed knives stored in between prep units.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet nesting throughout dishes, stored as clean. CDI - food employee moved back to dish pit for rewash.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed walk-in cooler door, unable to latch due to handle on the inside missing. Repair.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed cooking equipment and unit units with food, buildup, and debris. Increase cleaning frequency.

1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-102.11 (A) - (C )(1), (4)-(16) The Person in charge shall demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this code by (A) Complying with the Food Code by having no violations of Priority Items during the current inspection, (B) Being a certified food protection manager, or (C) by responding correctly to the inspector's questions related to the operation of the food establishment. -Pf. Observed coldholding , storage order, cooling, and date marking out of compliance. EHS will arrive back on site within 10 days to verify active managerial control in items listed above. 




Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw fish stored above RTE items in sushi LB. CDI - PIC rearrange storage order to prevent contamination.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed some dead pests in closet with air handler and closets in the bathrooms. Ensure thorough cleaning of these areas and monitoring. Facility has a pest control company who makes routine visits in place. No live pests noted today.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving in areas throughout facility including in the walk in cooler, in dry storage, and on the cart between stove and flat top grill. Observed shelving lined with cardboard throughout facility. Ensure proper shelf liners that can be washed, rinsed, and sanitized, are used as necessary. Observed previously repainted shelves at the dish area peeling. 
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed broken gasket on walk in cooler door.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Multiple non food-contact (NFC) surfaces in need of cleaning; gaskets, top of ovens, side of grills, shelves, insides of coolers, microwave on the line, small crevices of dish racks etc. REPEAT VIOLATION
4-601.11 (B) Food contact surfaces of cooking equipment and pans shall be kept free of encrusted grease deposits and other soil accumulations. Observed heavy carbonization and build up on sheet pans and muffin tins stored as clean.

5-202.14 Prevent backflow or back siphonage prevention devices installed shall meet American Society of Sanitary Engineering standards. -P Observed spray arm at pre-wash below flood rim level. CDI: Arm placed back. Ensure hose is never left below flood rim. EHS recommended installing a longer spring to bring the hose up further. Observed pistol grip sprayer attached to hose with no high pressure back flow at mop sink. CDI: Water turned off and pressure released. PIC stated they will look into a high pressure back flow for the hose connection.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed absorbent ceiling tile near 3-comp sink.
