For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed pre portioned packages of rice to be over stacked in the cookline opentop refrigerator with the top layers being at temperatures of 45-48F. Bottom layers observed at 41F or below. REPEAT.

CDI - Out of temperature bags voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. - Observed no written procedures on site at the time of the inspection for the 4 hour TPHC procedure that the facility uses for the sushi rice as per the kitchen manager. Facility was not open so EHS was unable to observe the TPHC procedure in action but PIC stated they do use 4 hour time holds on sushi rice in bins after cooking.

CDI - EHS filled out TPHC written procedures with the PIC at the time of the inspection.

TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. - Observed packages of raw frozen tuna thawing in its ROP packages on the countertop in the back kitchen area. Thawing instructions state “remove from package while thawing” and no cuts were observed in the packaging.

CDI - PIC placed packages under running water (<70F) and removed from ROP after thawing was completed.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. - Observed the WIC to be maintaining a temperature of 53F during the entirety of the inspection. EHS attempted inspection the previous night but discovered the facility closed for the night due to them discovering the WIC was down on the day the business is closed (Mondays). All items were discarded prior to the EHS arriving (see 6/23 visit sheet). Repair company was on site this morning at 8:30am and had repaired the electrical issue in the compressor that caused the breaker to trip. The unit had continued to drop for the first hour after the repair company left but stopped at 53F and will not go below that. WIC only has non-TCS vegetables in it and facility has contacted the repair company to come out again and take another look at it while the EHS was on site. EHS to follow up on the repair later this week. Facilities other refrigeration was observed functional and plans to operate for the day with the items they have and will not use the WIC for TCS products.

violations.comments:
2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. 
EHS observed food employees not utilizing beard restraints. 
**REPEAT** 
-0.5-

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. 
EHS observed build-up on gaskets in reach in coolers and build up accumulating on the outside of hot holding cabinets. 
**REPEAT** -1-

rows.general_comments:
Kitchen occupies multiple floors. Establishment no longer offers salmon cooked to order based on SOP's and current menu. Supplier freezes salmon at -4F for seven days with parasite destruction information is posted on wall. Hanger steak is fully cooked, cooled,
and reheated on grill for service. Establishment does not par-cook or use noncontinuous cooking methods.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P Observed cut lemons in reach in cooler with abundance of mold growth. CDI - Lemons were discarded. -0 points-

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw beef steaks and raw lobster tail stored above cooked onions, cooked chicken breast, and mashed potatoes in walk in cooler. CDI - Storage order was corrected. -0 points-

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed hardboiled eggs with a prep date of 6/16, cooked shrimp with a prep date of 6/17, risotto 6/17, cous cous 6/16, riced cauliflower 6/16, butter sauce 6/15, and horseradish sauce 5/15 stored in in top floor kitchen and bottom floor pizza kitchen. CDI - Items were discarded. PIC has been advised to conduct frequent inventory checks of TCS foods and to discard food that has exceeded the 7 day time/temperature window. -REPEAT- -3 points-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed covered containers of baked potato cooling in reach in cooler, covered sheet pan of chicken breast cooling in walk in cooler, pasta with a temperature of 68F, cooling at ambient room temperature on speed rack, and a deep Cambro of cooked oatmeal cooling in a beverage cooler. CDI - All items were unwrapped, uncovered, and taken to walk in cooler to rapidly cool to 41F or less. PIC has been advised to review cooling methods of TCS foods. Instructions on proper cooling methods have been given to PIC -0.5 points-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. Observed completely thawed tuna still in ROP environment. Employee was cutting open thawed tuna and transferring to metal pan to store at prep station. -0 points-

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed large bins of unlabeled dry ingredients (flours, sugar, etc.) in upstairs kitchen. -0 points-

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed box of food stored on floor of walk in freezer. -0 points-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-901.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils may not be cloth dried. Observed employee at the dish machine using cloth to hand dry utensils and equipment. CDI - Employee was instructed to allow utensils and equipment to air dry only. -0 points-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-205.10 Food equipment shall be used in accordance to the manufacturers intended use. Observed beverage cooler " intended for the storage and display of packaged or bottled products only" being used to store and cool TCS foods. Cooling TCS foods were moved to walk in cooler on second floor. PIC has been advised to store TCS foods in equipment intended for food storage. -0.5 points-

violations.comments:
4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P 
Observed container of sanitizer with in-use rags exceeding the ppm limit for sink & surface sanitizer (1130+ppm) CDI - PIC emptied and re-filled bucket with appropriately concentrated sanitizer. 

4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure. Observed cavity of microwave on cook line with debris and buildup. More frequent cleaning required.


4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed inside of ice chute for drink dispenser on server line with black buildup. CDI - PIC instructed server to clean chute.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed cooked wings cooked day prior to inspection in a large tub at 42-43F, indicating the cooling window was missed. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded wings.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed egg white halves, egg wholes, housemade pimento cheese, cooked cut chicken, cooked wings, liquid egg, jumbalaya, shredded pork, and velote cold holding above 41F (see temp observation chart). CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded affected items. REPEAT. VR REQUIRED, ITS IMPLEMENTED TODAY.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed 1 milk jug bearing no date mark, PIC indicated milk would last over 24hrs. Observed housemade pimento cheese bearing no date mark. CDI - PIC labeled or instructed food handlers to place date marks on items.
Observed pork tamales with no date mark. CDI - PIC verified date made and instructed food handler to place date marks on item. 
Observed out of date items: boiled eggs (6/13), roasted garlic (6/15). CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded items.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed one bottle of degreaser with illegible labeling to identify. Observed one bottle bearing no label to identify chemical. CDI - PIC placed labels to identify.

Observed avocados with stickers still in tact stored in prep unit. Observed food handler actively cutting avocados with stickers still in tact. PIC asked food handler if they were washed prior to use. Food handler indicated that they were. REPEAT.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed container of cooked chicken in make unit reach in compartment, tightly wrapped, and stored under other containers of chicken. EHS observed chicken over 41F. PIC stated the temperature was not verified after the cooling process and placed into unit prior to finishing cooling. EHS observed cooked chicken in WIC tightly covered in plastic wrap and stored in larger container. PIC the cooling method was incorrectly done and temperatures should be verified prior to wrapping. PIC stated facility is supposed to cool cooked items in ice bath and removed once at 41F for storage. PIC stated items were made 1.5 hours prior to beginning of inspection. CDI - PIC removed chicken from containers and placed on new ice bath, EHS observed chicken under 41F prior to end of inspection(see temp chart) REPEAT. (1)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. No employee present with active ANSI food protection manager certification. At least 1 employee shall be present during all hours of operation with active ANSI food protection manager certification. REPEAT

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-501.16(B) Maintain raw shell eggs in refrigeration with an ambient air temperature of 45F or below.- P Raw eggs in walk-in cooler measured 49F. Do not use walk-in cooler to store TCS foods unless it is repaired and holds below 41F. CDI - Employee moved eggs to functioning upright cooler. Improvement from last inspection so kept at half credit. REPEAT

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Some items in upright cooler were missing prep date labels. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days under refrigeration. Establishment needs to put cook/freeze date on food, and then put thaw date on food also to keep track of the 7 days allowed under refrigeration. CDI - PIC educated on date marking and she placed thaw dates on items. Improvement from last inspection so kept at half credit. REPEAT

violations.comments:
4-703.11(B) After being cleaned in a mechanical dish machine, equipment food-contact surfaces and utensils shall be sanitized in the dish machine by achieving a utensil surface temperature of 160F as measured by a max/min thermometer.-P. Observed high temperature dish machine not reaching 160F for sanitizing according to EHS plate thermometer. PIC called maintenance to address the issue and EHS observed technician arrive on site during inspection. CDI: Dish machine now reaching 160F for sanitizing. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following weblink: Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-401.11 Cook raw animal foods to required temperatures. Do not interrupt the cooking process without approved procedures. -P Observed sliced roast beef in RIC drawer at sandwich prep line. PIC stated that the roast beef is cooked on a spit at wood fired oven to 110F, sliced, cooled, cold held, then cooked to 165F for service per order. PIC does not have approved procedures for non-continuous cooking in facility. PIC stated that they will fill out an application for procedures to continue this procedure once approved. PIC stated that until procedures are approved, roast will be cooked to 130F for 110 minutes to be in compliance with the food code. CDI: non-continuous cooked roast beef voluntarily discarded.

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed large plastic bin with lid holding raw whole chickens in a cold brine solution in WIC cooling from yesterday.chickens were measuring 43F-45F. CDI: PIC added ice to brine solution to quickly cool raw chickens to 41F or below. EHS observed chickens at 38F by end of inspection. PIC stated that room temperature water is put into the brine solution and placed in the WIC to cool. PIC stated that they are now going to start adding ice to the solution to ensure chickens are cooled quicker. 




For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed raw whole chickens in large, tightly covered plastic bings in a cold brine solution that was cooling over night with temperatures above 41F. CDI: PIC added ice to bins to quickly cool chickens to below 41F and will use ice for their procedure going forward to ensure chickens are cooled quickly. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed raw ROP chicken tenders thawing in Cambro submerged in running water at prep sink. Water was 80F. CDI: PIC instructed employee to move product to WIC to continue thawing on a speed rack. EHS discussed proper thawing parameters with PIC.

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed stack of metal dishes on rack above 3 comp sink still sticky to touch.
CDI- Dishes re-w/r/s. 

4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code, P. 
Observed tongs and spoons at cook line with oil and food debris from yesterday left at flat top overnight. EHS advises PIC on proper frequency of cleaning in-use utensils.
CDI- All cook line utensils w/r/s. (-1.5)

REPEAT.
3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed RTE chicken, egg rolls, and cooked veggies without date mark. EHS provided education to PIC and head chef about what items require a date mark and confirmed prep date of items.
CDI- Education provided, items date marked. (-3)

Reminder- the following need date marks if prepped and kept onsite more than 24 hours:
1. All cooked meats 
2. Cooked eggs and egg rolls 
3. Cooked plant food- rice, vegetables
4. Opened containers of milk 
5. Opened containers of soft cheeses 
6. Shrimp and hoisin sauce 
7. Slices lettuce, cabbage, and tomatoes 
8. Fresh garlic and oil mixtures 
9. Cooked noodles, lo mein 
Note: this list is not all inclusive, and is only a starting guide for relevant foods onsite.

4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent.
Observed non ANSI approved crockpots left on overnight with food inside. EHS advises PIC this equipment is not graded for continuous use without monitoring. EHS advises PIC to not leave equipment on overnight, or replace with commercial models. (-0.5)

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed grease/oil residue accumulated on dry storage racks throughout main kitchen. Increase cleaning frequency. (-0.5)

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure.
Observed yellowing of walls and grease build up behind equipment in main kitchen and 3 comp sink corridor. Increase cleaning frequency. (-0)

rows.violation_text:
16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed stack of metal dishes on rack above 3 comp sink still sticky to touch.
CDI- Dishes re-w/r/s. 

4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code, P. 
Observed tongs and spoons at cook line with oil and food debris from yesterday left at flat top overnight. EHS advises PIC on proper frequency of cleaning in-use utensils.
CDI- All cook line utensils w/r/s. (-1.5)

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: REPEAT.
3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed RTE chicken, egg rolls, and cooked veggies without date mark. EHS provided education to PIC and head chef about what items require a date mark and confirmed prep date of items.
CDI- Education provided, items date marked. (-3)

Reminder- the following need date marks if prepped and kept onsite more than 24 hours:
1. All cooked meats 
2. Cooked eggs and egg rolls 
3. Cooked plant food- rice, vegetables
4. Opened containers of milk 
5. Opened containers of soft cheeses 
6. Shrimp and hoisin sauce 
7. Slices lettuce, cabbage, and tomatoes 
8. Fresh garlic and oil mixtures 
9. Cooked noodles, lo mein 
Note: this list is not all inclusive, and is only a starting guide for relevant foods onsite.

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry.
Observed back door cracked open at start of inspection and several fruit flies inside. No contamination observed and regular pest control services verified. 
CDI- Door closed. (-0)

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed soiled wiping cloths on prep tops and surfaces throughout. EHS discusses with PIC and advises them to create sani-buckets for wiping cloths storage in front and back of kitchen. (-0.5)

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: REPEAT.
4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent.
Observed non ANSI approved crockpots left on overnight with food inside. EHS advises PIC this equipment is not graded for continuous use without monitoring. EHS advises PIC to not leave equipment on overnight, or replace with commercial models. (-0.5)

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed grease/oil residue accumulated on dry storage racks throughout main kitchen. Increase cleaning frequency. (-0.5)

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure.
Observed yellowing of walls and grease build up behind equipment in main kitchen and 3 comp sink corridor. Increase cleaning frequency. (-0)

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. No employee present with active ANSI food protection manager certification. At least 1 employee shall be present during all hours of operation that has active ANSI food protection manager certification. REPEAT

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Several bottles of water that were from employees were stored on prep table. Do not store personal drinks on prep tables. 0 pts.

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf No paper towels at hand washing sink. Hand washing sinks must be equipped with paper towels. CDI - PIC put roll of paper towels at hand sink.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Raw beef stored above dried shrimp in upright cooler. Do not store raw animal foods above ready-to-eat foods. CDI - PIC corrected food storage order. Improvement from last inspection so kept at half credit. REPEAT

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Steamed buns, boiled eggs, and sausage were missing prep date labels. Cooked pork had 5-24 prep date on it. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days of preparation. CDI - PIC placed prep date labels on eggs, buns and sausage. PIC discarded pork. REPEAT

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf Steamed buns held using TPHC were not labeled with start time. CDI - PIC stated they were cooked and placed there at 10AM so she placed a 10AM-2PM TPHC sticker on basket of steamed buns.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Several dead insects observed throughout kitchen. Remove dead pests and ensure no live ones are present and continue using monthly pest control. REPEAT

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf Establishment is using cardboard lids for bun containers. Cardboard is not approved as food-contact surface. Buy lids that are smooth, safe for food, and are cleanable. REPEAT // 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Components of walk-in freezer door and handle are rusted and taped. Shelves in walk-in cooler are rusted. REPEAT

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Cleaning needed inside refrigeration, shelves, outsides of cooking equipment, etc. Clean department more thoroughly and more frequently. REPEAT

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Floor in walk-in cooler is in poor repair. REPEAT // 6-501.114 Discard items and litter stored on the premises as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance. Large amount of equipment that is broken and not used in kitchen. Remove old and broken equipment to aid in cleaning and to prevent locations that can harbor pests. REPEAT

violations.comments:
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink.
Observed no handwashing sign at handsink in dish room. Ensure all handsinks have a handwash sign posted.
0 pts.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:
3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P
Observed moldy strawberries in WIC during inspection.
CDI - Strawberries voluntarily discarded.
Repeat. 1 pt.

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. 
Observed raw ground beef being stored above raw whole in-tact muscle beef in WIC during inspection.
CDI - PIC corrected storage order.
Repeat. 1.5 pts.
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed all TCS foods in sushi prep holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart. Ambient in unit 43F. Maintenance contacted during inspection. Facility removed all sushi items from menu until repair. All out of temperature foods voluntarily discarded.
72 hour VR required.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed tomato jam from 6/11/26 in WIC and dessert prep during inspection.
CDI - Tomato jam voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (B)(2) Consumer Advisory Disclosures that are asterisked to a footnote shall state, * Items are served raw or undercooked, or contain (or may contain) raw or undercooked ingredients. -Pf
Observed various items on menu that are allowed to be ordered undercooked or raw without an asterisk leading to consumer advisory during inspection.
CDI - Employee added asterisks to all items on all menus during inspection.
0 pts.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-204.11 Provide sanitizer at correct concentrations, diluting as required.-P
Observed sanitizer at three compartment sink dispensing above 400ppm quat during inspection.
CDI - Facility to dilute and test sanitizer until repair. Maintenance contacted during inspection.
1 pt.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed raw spicy tuna mix cooling from ambient while in deep, sealed plastic containers in Robata unit during inspection. Tuna was not cooling rapidly. See temperature chart.
CDI - Tuna moved to WIC, ice bath, and eventually shallow, metal pan in WIF to hasten cooling. Tuna achieved 37F during inspection.
Repeat. 1 pt.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
Observed various working containers void of label identifying their contents. Ensure all working containers are properly labeled to identify their contents.
0 pts.

rows.general_comments:
Jammy Egg is not reheated
Ham and Cheese Croissants are now kept in the walk-in cooler
Establishment ferments sour dough for Bagels
Approved additives used for drinks
TPHC - Coffee Creamer, Fried Rice, Cream Cheese Frosting (house made), Coffee Creamers

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed metal insert containers of raw beef to be stored in the top refrigerated grill drawers directly above containers of cooked and diced chicken.

CDI - PIC rearranged refrigerated grill drawers so that all cooked/RTE items are in the top drawers.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. - Observed pairs of tongs, plastic insert containers, and mandolin slicers to be stored as clean in the back kitchen are but were found with visible food debris/tape residue on their food contact surfaces.

CDI - All dirty dishes moved to the dirty dish pile to be W/R/S.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed 2 trays of cooked ribs with date marks of 6/16, making today day 8. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed a metal container of carpaccio to be at a temperature of 45F and was found to be cooling in the 2 door lowboy adjacent the upright RIF at the cookline. PIC stated that they had cut it at around 4pm to be used tonight. The lowboy refrigerator is not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.

CDI - PIC moved item to WIC to rapidly cool.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed uncovered employee beverage .

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed a few pickles overstocked in prep unit in and soiled area wedged between prep unit door and upper shelf. CDI, pickles voluntarily discarded .
4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure. Observed heavy dry soil in microwave.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. -P - Observed bleach stored on vegetable prep sink. CDI, bleach relocated for proper storage.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed containers of single-service cups stored on ground from morning delivery.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments: 4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf - Observed quat test kit hooked up to 3 compartment sink but facility did not have quat test strips on site. Vr required.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed soil and debris accumulating on shelving and inside prep units throughout kitchen.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. No employee present with active ANSI food protection manager certification. At least 1 employee with active ANSI food protection manager certification shall be present during all hours of operation. REPEAT

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Raw chicken stored above cooked chicken, raw pork stored above cooked food, and raw beef stored above tamarindo in walk-in cooler. Do not store raw animal foods above ready-to-eat foods. CDI - Employee corrected food storage order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Chicharrones and chicken in hot holding measured 95-120F. TCS foods in hot holding shall measure at least 135F. CDI - Both items reheated to above 165F. REPEAT

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Several TCS RTE foods in walk-in cooler were missing prep date labels. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days of preparation. CDI - Employee placed prep date labels on all items. REPEAT

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Several cooks were not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear a hair restraint, such as a hat or hair net. REPEAT

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- * *Observed - PIC cleaning out 2 Door Standing RIC upon arrival. EHS temped food within unit and took an ambient temperature of the unit. 3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. *Observed - Thawed ROPd Salmon still within ROP environment. Release Frozen Salmon from ROP environment before thawing under refrigeration or after thawing under running cold water. *CDI - PIC removed Salmon from packaging.

CDI - PIC labeled “do not use” and moved to another shelf, to be returned for credit.

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P - Observed the chemical dishwasher to be testing at 0ppm of chlorine at the time of the inspection. The sanitizer level in the sanitizer bottle was observed to be low and the hose was not in the solution, resulting in no sanitizer being pumped through the line every use/cycle. PIC stated that they often move the chemical bottles around to clean beneath the machine and that they were not aware that the hose did not always reach the bottom of the bottle.

CDI - Hose adjusted to reach the solution in the bottle. Machine primed and observed testing at 100ppm.

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. - Observed the deli slicer to be stored as clean as per the PIC and was observed to have a notebook stored directly on top of the slicer portion, contaminating the slicer. REPEAT.

CDI - Notebook removed and deli slicer W/R/S.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed a plastic container of prepared yogurt parfaits and a large container of garlic confit in the 3 door RIC that were not provided with date marks of any kind. PIC stated that the yogurt parfaits were prepared yesterday (6/22) and the garlic confit was prepared on Sunday (6/21). REPEAT.

CDI - PIC date marked items.

- Observed a container of house made pesto to be stored in the cookline refrigerated drawer with a date mark of 5/20. PIC stated that after being prepared and portioned into smaller plastic containers, they freeze the batch of pesto and pull out one container to be used at a time. Facility does not currently have a procedure of putting “pulled from freezer” dates onto items. EHS explained the date marking procedure when items are frozen and why they require to have pulled from freezer dates. Prep employee stated pulling the container of pesto in question out of the freezer on Sunday (6/21).

CDI - Pulled from freezer date placed onto container.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed a plastic container of egg patties to be at a temperature of 85F in the cookline refrigerated drawers. Egg patties were part of the batch that were actively cooling upon the EHS arrival and were stated to have been made at approx. 10am. An employee had portioned it out into 2 containers after the EHS arrival placing one into the RIC to cool and one into the refrigerated drawers, both of which are not designed nor approved to rapidly cool down items. Facilities permit only approves them to cooling egg patties in the RIF.

CDI - Egg patties transferred to an sheet pan and placed onto an ice bath to rapidly cool the patties down as the time constraints of the first 2 hour window of cooling may not have been met in the RIF. EHS observed the egg patties at 56F by 11:45am.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P

3-306.11 Protect food on display using shields, packaging, or other effective means. -P
Observed an unlocked ice machine at the coffee bar. During transitional permitting, a condition stated that: Ice machine must be inaccessible to guests, must dispense ice so that bulk ice is not exposed, or must be removed.

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils.
Observed two employees preparing food while wearing bracelets and no hair restraints.

rows.violation_text:
8 - Hands clean & properly washed: 2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P 
Observed an employee wash hands for 5-6 seconds before drying. CDI - Employee was educated and they rewashed hands.

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: REPEAT
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed 47-50 degree TCS food products in the reach-in cooler due to the thermostat not being turned down low enough. CDI - Digital thermostat was adjusted and cooler came down to 35 degrees. All TCS food products were voluntarily discarded.

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-306.11 Protect food on display using shields, packaging, or other effective means. -P

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed pan of onion sauce, pan of pico, and a pan of cooked potatoes cold holding above 41F inside the flip top portion of make table 1. PIC stated the lid was kept open prior to EHS's arrival to fulfill orders. All other TCS food items inside this unit were at proper temps.
CDI: PIC moved all above mentioned TCS food items into the WIF to rapidly cool down. 
Observed pan of spinach sauce and a pan of marinated raw salmon cold holding above 41F inside the lowboy portion of make table 1. PIC stated both of these pans were taken out of the unit recently for an order.
CDI: PIC moved both pans of TCS food items into the WIF to rapidly cool down.
Observed large bustub full of cut romaine lettuce cold holding above 41F inside the lowboy portion of make table 2. PIC stated they had this bustub out to make a couple salads for an order.
CDI: PIC removed lid off of the bustub and moved into the WIC to rapidly cool down. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed soiled gaskets inside cold holding units. Observed debris buildup on clean dish racks.
Increase cleaning frequency to prevent contamination.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
-REPEAT-
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf-
Observed chickpeas in deep, densely packed plastic container cooling at a rate below .12F/min in WIC. CDI- PIC transferred chickpeas to shallow metal pans to cool uncovered in WIC.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(H)(I) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf.’
Observed PIC fail to ensure TCS foods properly cooled or were being held to required temperature parameters by operating with walk-in in disrepair. VERIFICATION REQUIRED to assess monitoring and active managerial controls on or before 7/3. 



A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed establishment unable to maintain TCS foods at 41F or below per the 2017 food code. TCS items maintained out of temp in unit were voluntarily discarded. Ambient temp of unit read 48F. Items delivered frozen on yesterday were relocated to separate store to be maintained until WIC is repaired. VERIFICATION REQUIRED on or before 6/26 to assess walk-in unit. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. -Observed no Certified Food Protection Manager on site during inspection. Ensure CFPM is on site during all hours of food preparation and handling.
-REPEAT-

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf -Observed no paper towels at front handsink at start of inspection. -CDI- PIC provided paper towels at sink.

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. -Observed pizza sauce miss cooling parameters after being prepped and cooled overnight in WIC. Observed two containers of pizza sauce, one from 6/22 and another from 6/20, above 41F after not being removed from WIC since cooling started. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded both containers of pizza sauce.
-REPEAT-

For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed anchovies held past their 7-day timeframe (prep date of 6/15). -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the anchovies. 
-Observed lasagna without a date mark. -CDI- PIC verified the original cook date of the earliest-prepared ingredient by speaking with the chef and properly added a date mark (6/21). 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf -Observed two containers of pizza sauce miss cooling parameters after cooling too slowly in WIC overnight. Observed pizza sauce stored in large, deep plastic containers, covered tightly with lids, in the WIC. EHS stated facility must improve cooling methods, and recommends using ice wands to cool large batches of pizza sauce. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the pizza sauce.
-REPEAT-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed a pan of chicken shawarma hot holding inside the hotbox below 135F. PIC stated they had recently cut the chicken shawarma and placed into the hotbox.
CDI: PIC had food employee reheat the pan of chicken shawarma on the stovetop and EHS observed shawarma was reheated above 165F.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed pan of hard-boiled eggs, pan of tzatziki, pan of hummus, and pan of cucumber and tomato salad all cold holding above 41F in the CH well at the serving line. PIC stated these pans were placed into the cold holding unit over an hour prior. All other TCS food items in this unit were at proper temps.
CDI: PIC moved all above mentioned TCS food items into the blast chiller and WIF to rapidly cool down. 
Observed pans of yogurt and pans of cheese sauce cold holding above 41F inside make table 2. PICs stated that the food employee working this station had left the lid open to fulfill orders for large catering order upon EHS's arrival. 
CDI: PICs moved above mentioned TCS food items into the blast chiller and WIF to rapidly cool down. 
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed cut salmon and cut chicken actively cooling in grill make unit. PIC stated salmon was placed into unit and cut an hour prior to inspection. after thirty minutes, EHS observed inadequate cooling rate (see temp chart). EHS observed cooked chicken bagged into sets for harissa chicken. EHS observed chicken above 41F (see temp chart). PIC stated chicken was placed into unit at noon but was left out unintentionally for longer than needed prior to being placed into unit so item needed to cool after. No temperature was checked after being placed into unit. EHS observed diced tomatoes being stored in WIC in numerous pans tightly covered with lid. PIC stated items should not be covered, and removed lid. EHS observed inadequate cooling rate after 25 minutes (see temp chart). PIC stated tomatoes were cut at 1:30pm. CDI - Due to having 15 minutes left to cool, PIC removed harissa chicken and placed into blast chiller to quickly cool. PIC removed salmon from make unit and placed into blast chiller. EHS observed chicken at 41 within 15 minutes. EHS observed salmon at 41F prior to end of inspection. PIC moved diced tomatoes to blast chiller. EHS observed adequate cooling rate by end of inspection. EHS advised to ensure temperatures are taken by staff throughout the day to ensure when items are moved they are cooled prior to storage, and to ensure proper cooling methods are followed. REPEAT. (1)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. EHS observed numerous pans stored clean stacked on each other with water still present on interior surfaces of pans. EHS advised to stagger pans prior to stacking to allow for adequate air drying prior to storage. (0)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf EHS observed numerous rubber spatulas with burnt/melted handles and flat part of spatula beginning to break. EHS advised to replace spatulas once handles melt, burnt, or when cracks are present on flat part of spatula due to food contact surface no longer being smooth and free of cracks. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded spatulas. REPEAT. 

4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. EHS observed two cutting boards with melted circles on cutting surface of cutting boards. PIC stated this is due to preparing Mac and cheese; hot pan of pasta is placed on cutting board to allow for sauce to be mixed in with pasta. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded cutting boards. (0.5)

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed two ceiling tiles soiled with brown residue, possibly due to splash or water damage. EHS observed AC vent mentioned during last inspection soiled with dust and debris. AC vent is located above food preparation area. REPEAT. (0.5)

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no certified PIC on site during inspection. CDI by instruction. 





For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P - Observed a food employee not vigorously rub hands for required time when washing and use bare hands to turn faucet off. CDI, employee rewashed hands. 



For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed raw shell egg stored over RTE items in tall reach in refrigerator . CDI, foods relocated for proper storage. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P - Observed employee pick up soiled item from ground but not discard gloves before proceeding to handle clean single-service articles and food preparation.

4-703.11 After cleaning, equipment, food contact surfaces, and utensils shall be sanitized using an approved method.-P 
Observed employees not rinsing prior to putting clean equipment in sanitizer solution. CDI to rewash and sanitize. Observed quat sanitizer testing between 0-100 ppm and quat sanitizer bottle empty below 3 compartment sink. CDI, santnizer refilled with new sanitizer bottle. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .O

3-401.11 Cook raw animal foods to required temperatures. Do not interrupt the cooking process without approved procedures. -P - Observed ribeye par-cooked and not cooked fully prior to placing in tall 2 door reach in to cool. CDI, ribeye transferred to finish cooking process on flat top and observed ribeye cooked to 152 degrees F. Facility to discontinue non-continuous cooking unless procedures approved .

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments:
2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur.
EHS observed two employee beverages stored with clean dishes. 
CDI- Drinks were relocated to an approved location. 
-0-

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P 
EHS observed cut melon with no date mark present in RIC and a Demi glaze (contains cooked tomatoes) in prep unit with a prep date of 06/09. 
CDI- EHS allowed PIC to date mark the watermelon. PIC stated that the demi glaze was prepped on 06/09 and frozen, then thawed today for use. EHS advised PIC to label items when they were removed from the freezer to denote the number of days the product has left for use. 
**Points not escalated due to overall improvements with date marking.** **REPEAT** -1.5- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
EHS observed two containers of dry seasonings with no label present. 
CDI- PIC labeled the items. 
-0-

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw chicken stored above cheese in LB. CDI - PIC rearrange to prevent contamination.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed slicer stored as cleaned with visible food debris. CDI - slicer move to three comp sink for proper cleaning. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P observed unsalted butter stored on grill shelf and counter HH below 135. CDI - PIC heated to 165 and above and placed on stove flame. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed wiping cloths in 0ppm sanitizer. CDI- Sanitizer remade to proper concentration.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed utensil stored in sauces with handle inside food. CDI - PIC removed utensil

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non
food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed significant
build up on pieces of equipment throughout facility and on cooking equipment (stove, burners, and shelving.) Increase cleaning frequency.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed utensil stored in sauces with handle inside food. CDI - PIC removed utensil

rows.general_comments: EHS observed Typhoidal Salmonella missing from the cooperate Health Policy. Establishment has a State Employee Poster posted with all 6 reportable illnesses.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments:
2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur.
Observed an employee drink stored on a shelf above a prep surface.

REPEAT
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P. 
Observed food employee fail to stop task to wash hands after picking up utensil that had fallen on the floor. CDI - Employee was educated and wash hands properly before returning to task.

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed two pairs of dish gloves draped over the side of the back kitchen hand sink. CDI - Items were removed.

REPEAT
3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P. 
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. 
Observed an employee fail to discard gloves after picking up utensil that fell on floor. CDI - Employee was educated and they discarded gloves, washed hands. Observed unwashed vegetables stored above ready-to-eat vegetables.

4-703.11 After cleaning, equipment, food contact surfaces, and utensils shall be sanitized using an approved method.-P 
Observed an employee sanitize a utensil that had fallen on the floor for 1-2 seconds. CDI - Employee was educated and they rewashed, rinsed and sanitized utensil with the required sanitizer contact time.

3-DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed 43 degree spring rolls in the walk-in freezer due to it having an ambient air temperature of 54 degrees. Items were voluntarily discarded. Manager stated that a work order for the walk-in freezer was submitted on 6/19/26. All foods below 41 degrees were moved to the regular walk-in cooler. Walk-in freezer will not be used until it is repaired.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
10-DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED
4-301.11 Provide equipment in number and capacity so that cooling, heating, and holding temperatures are achieved. -Pf
3-501.11 Stored frozen foods shall be maintained frozen. 
Observed a 54 degree walk-in freezer. Observed all frozen boxes of food thawing in the walk-in freezer.

3-501.12 Food that is being slacked shall be held in refrigeration at 41F and below or at any temperature if the food remains frozen.
Observed all frozen foods thawing in a 54 degree walk-in freezer.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry.
Observed the back kitchen door cracked open during the entire inspection.

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed.
Observed two employees preparing food while wearing watches and bracelets.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted.
4-901.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils may not be cloth dried.
Observed an employee cloth dry a utensil. Observed wet-stacked dishes.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor.
4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used.
Observed boxes of single-service items stored on a wet floor. Observed single-service cups stacked high at the check-out counter without a protective sleeve.

5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair.
5-202.14 Prevent backflow or back siphonage prevention devices installed shall meet American Society of Sanitary Engineering standards. -P
Observed a water hose being cut off by a splitter valve with an ASSE-1001 backflow prevention device that is not rated for continuous pressure. CDI - Water was turned off at the faucet. Observed a leak coming from the 3-compartment sink faucet and the drain pipes underneath.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments:
5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors.
5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place.
Observed open dumpster doors and both drain plugs missing.

violations.comments:
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf
Observed handsink at dish pit out of paper towels during inspeciton.
CDI - Paper towels provided.
0 pts.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed raw steak being stored above cooked, RTE steak in prep WIC during inspection.
CDI - PIC corrected order.
0 pts.
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. 
Observed shredded cheese and cut lettuce miss cooling window during inspection. Observed salsa pitchers in server lowboy and large batch of prepped salsa in prep WIC almost miss cooling window during inspection. See temperature chart. Cooling methods insufficient to support proper cooling. See violation 33 for further details. 
CDI - Items that missed window voluntarily discarded. Large batch of salsa voluntarily discarded. Salsa pitchers in server lowboy moved to blast chiller to rapidly cool.
1.5 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed various items throughout kitchen holding above 41F during inspection. See temperature chart. PIC verified items bad been in units for less than four hours, and that all temperatures were compliant when checked at 10:30am.
CDI - Ice packs placed on all out of temperature items to rapidly cool back down to 41F.
0 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed cut lettuce and shredded cheese cooling in meat WIC while in deep, covered plastic containers during inspection. Both items had missed cooling window. Observed large batch of salsa cooling in deep, covered plastic container in prep WIC. Salsa was about to miss cooling window. Observed salsa pitchers cooling in server lowboy during inspection. Salsa was about to miss cooling window.
CDI - Salsa pitchers moved to blast chiller to complete cooling process in time. All other items voluntarily discarded.
Repeat. 1 pt.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

REPEAT

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. Observed sliced cheese being held on TPHC without written procedures. EHS emailed TPHC procedures. VERIFICATION REQUIRED. 

TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (A) Provide consumer advisory for animal foods served raw or under-cooked. Consumer advisory must include disclosure and reminder.-Pf. Observed no consumer advisory for items served undercooked on chalk board menu. VERIFICATION REQUIRED. 

3-603.11 (B)(1) Consumer Advisory Disclosures shall indicate that the animal-derived food is or can be served raw or undercooked by using words and phrases such as raw, undercooked, cooked to order, etc., or by asterisking to a footnote per 3-603.11 (B)(2). -Pf. Observed no asterisks beside menu items containing lox on paper menu. VERIFICATION REQUIRED.

violations.comments:

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no CFPM onsite during inspection. CDI- CFPM arrived during type up portion. 






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed handsink being used to stored container used to trap drain flies. CDI- PIC removed container to separate area and bean process of cleaning sink.

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings.
Observed open bag of flour stored uncovered on lower shelf of prep table near walk-in. Cover or wrap bag.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed can opener blade heavily soiled during inspection. CDI- PIC removed item to be w/r/s. PIC stated new blades had been ordered.
