violations.comments:
4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf. 
Observed establishment without quat test strips. CDI- EHS provided. PIC to order strips and send proof to EHS via business card.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed hole in wall near office setup. Observed column damaged needing repair. 
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed vent cover with heavy dust accumulation.

rows.violation_text:
2 - Certified Food Protection Manager: 2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no CFPM onsite during inspection. CDI- CFPM arrived during type up portion. 






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed handsink being used to stored container used to trap drain flies. CDI- PIC removed container to separate area and bean process of cleaning sink.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings.
Observed open bag of flour stored uncovered on lower shelf of prep table near walk-in. Cover or wrap bag.

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed can opener blade heavily soiled during inspection. CDI- PIC removed item to be w/r/s. PIC stated new blades had been ordered. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed canned roasted red peppers being held out of temp for extended period for draining in prep sink. CDI- EHS discussed with PIC, PIC removed item to cool in low reach-in cooler on prep line. *REPEAT*



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-202.13 Properly locate mechanical insect control devices.
Fly glue trap stored directly over handsink and close to single-use pizza boxes. EHS discussed relocating. 
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry.
Observed front door maintained open without fly fan. Large windows adjacent had 1/2 fly fans on at start of inspection. *REPEAT*
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observd heavy presence of small flying insects in dish pit and can wash area. PIC stated pest control has been onsite weekly, in addition to establishment hiring floor and drain cleaners.

48 - Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips: 4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf. 
Observed establishment without quat test strips. CDI- EHS provided. PIC to order strips and send proof to EHS via business card.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed hole in wall near office setup. Observed column damaged needing repair. 
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed vent cover with heavy dust accumulation.

violations.comments:
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed hand sinks that were noticeably soiled with food debris/residue in the basins. Do not use hand sinks for any other purposes other than hand washing. (1 pt)

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed Raw shell eggs being stored above tubs of yogurt. -CDI- PIC relocated the raw shell eggs to the bottom shelf. (0 pts) 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed TCS food items that were being held below 135F. -CDI- PIC decided to add those items to the TPHC list and will start using proper TPHC procedures. REPEAT*** (1.5 pts) 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed TCS food items throughout the facility holding above 41F while said to be cold holding. The units had ambient temperatures recorded at 38-40F. EHS noticed that the doors for the units were opened for prolonged periods of time likely raising the temperature of the items inside. -CDI- EHS coached PIC on keeping the doors closed as much as possible in order to maintain TCS food products at 41F and below. PIC closed the doors to allow the items to cool. (1.5 pts) 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed soiled cloths being stored throughout the facility on surfaces such as shelves and prep top units. Hold soiled cloths in a properly concentrated sanitizer solution. (0 pts)

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed base tiles coming off of the wall or completely missing throughout the facility. Repair necessary tiles on FRP walling. (0.5 pts)

violations.comments: 5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. -Observed handsink by soda station blocked by a pitcher, stored inside sink. Ensure handsinks remain accessible for use. CDI- Pitcher was removed from handsink.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed diced chicken and spring mix held past its 7-day timeframe (6/13 for both items). -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the chicken and spring mix.
-Observed opened package of hot dogs and cooked steak in WIC without date marks. -CDI- PIC verified open/cook date of both items by referencing additional bag of hot dogs and checking the freezer thaw-date of steak. PIC properly added date marks.
-REPEAT-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf-
Observed sticker residue on metal containers being stored as clean. CDI - PIC moved items to dish pit for w/r/s.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

-REPEAT-
3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. -Pf- 
Observed raw beef thawing under running water. Water temp was 78F. CDI - Item moved to walk-in cooler to thaw. EHS advised PIC that summer cold tap water temperatures are generally too warm to thaw items in the prep sink.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed scoop for semolina stored with handle in food. PIC moved scoop to dish pit for w/r/s and replaced with a clean scoop. All other scoops properly stored.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
-REPEAT-
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. 
Observed non-functioning grill drawers. Repair or replace grill drawers.

-REPEAT-
4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris. 4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. 
Observed build up of food debris on front and sides of coolers, fryers and other cooking equipment. PIC to address cleaning frequency.

-REPEAT-
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed cracked floor tiles near reach-in freezer unit on cook line. Repair or replace cracked tiles.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-202.11 Shield the lights or provide shatter-resistant bulbs in areas of exposed food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-use articles.
Observed two light bulb shields missing over the pizza oven. Observed one light bulb shield missing over gas burners. Replace bulb covers.

6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity.
Observed two non-functional lights over the fryer. Observed one non-functional light over the dish machine. Repair or replace non-functioning bulbs.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF 
Observed PIC not able to explain how new employees are trained in the employee health policy, and uncertain if there is a written policy onsite. 
CDI- County EHP and education provided. (-1)

REPEAT.
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pooled egg yolks above RTE foods and sliced produce in cook line lowboy. No contamination observed.
CDI- Eggs and RTE foods change positions so raw eggs are on bottom rack. (-1.5)

4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code, P. 
Observed meat slicer not yet used toward with food debris from last use behind blade. EHS advises staff to clean and sanitize equipment at required frequency.
CDI- Slicer w/r/s. (-1.5)

4-602.11 (A) (4) Food contact surface of a thermometer shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, before using or storing. P.
Observed probe thermometer in storage with food residue on probe. Increased cleaning frequency advised.
CDI- Probe sanitized.

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. 
Observed no written procedures for TPHC onsite.
CDI- EHS provided county form. 

FORM HERE: https://mecknc.widen.net/s/pvtc855zds/tphc-template-english

3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf
Observed fried chicken on TPHC without time label.
CDI- EHS verifies correct start temp >165F and allows PIC to mark for time. (-1.5)

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf
Observed raw chicken thawing on prep top at 45F.
CDI- EHS advises PIC on proper thawing methods. Chicken returned to WIC. (-0)

REPEAT.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed numerous used wiping cloths on prep tops throughout front and back of kitchen.
CDI- Sani-buckets made up for wiping cloth storage, sanitizer concentration verified. (-1)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf
Observed one fryer basket with metal wire coming apart from frame, no longer safe to use.
CDI- Basket voluntarily disposed and replaced with new. (-0)

REPEAT.
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized.
Observed funerals cutting boards with deep scoring, no longer cleanable. Replace at earliest opportunity. (-0.5) 

4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance.
Observed rusted metal racks inside WIC, no longer cleanable. Replace racks which are corroded; not smooth and cleanable.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-202.11 Shield the lights or provide shatter-resistant bulbs in areas of exposed food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-use articles.
Observed far left cook line hood bulb shattered. Replace urgently with shatterproof bulb. (-0)

6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf. Observed hand sink beside large ice machine without soap. CDI: PIC refilled soap. 
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf. Observed hand sink behind registers without paper towels. CDI: Paper towels were placed at hand sink.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed various milks above 41F inside lowboy under espresso machine closest to registers. Ambient air of unit was 48F. PIC voluntarily discarded all milks. VERIFICATION REQUIRED.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed a a couple of open bags bag raw breaded chicken chunks stored above french fries in the single door reach-in freezer near the fryer station. CDI- PIC corrected storage.
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed grease and food residue on several cleaned metal and plastic food pans and containers on the clean equipment storage shelf. CDI- PIC voluntarily removed the items to the dishwasher area to have them washed again. REPEAT.


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed containers of cooked diced onions, cooked cut peppers, and diced tomato in the walk-in cooler all holding at 45F. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the food items during inspection. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed a box of mini apple pies and a box of brownies stored directly on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Ensure food boxes are stored at least 6 inches above the floor.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed several cleaned metal and plastic pans and containers stored on the clean equipment shelf while still wet. Ensure clean equipment is properly air dried before stacking. CDI- PIC removed equipment to the dish washer to be re-washed. REPEAT.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf. Observed no available chlorine sanitizer test kit to measure the chlorine sanitizer concentration in the dishwasher. CDI- EHS provided chlorine sanitizer test kit to PIC. Ensure sanitizer test kit is used and remains available.

4-501.14 Warewashing machines and the compartments/drainboards of sinks used for washing and rinsing equipment, utensils, raw food, etc. shall be cleaned before use, throughout the day to prevent recontamination of equipment and utensils, and at least every 24 hours if used. Observed food debris and lime scale build-up on the interior of the dish washer machine. The dish washer machine is not cleaned every 24 hours. PIC must educate staff on cleaning the dish washer machine at required frequency.

REPEAT
4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed food debris and grease residue build-up on wire rack shelving in the clean equipment room and by the cut table. Also, the wire rack shelving above the 3-comp sink has grease residue build-up. Increase frequency of detailed cleaning shelving. Observed excessive residue build-up on the pizza oven tracks and on top of the pizza oven and needs cleaned.
Observed food residue build-up on the exterior handles and the interior sections of the make-line cooler, the standing 2 door reach-in cooler, and the standing 2 door reach-in freezer. Ensure all equipment is cleaned and cleaned frequently enough to prevent build-up.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed food debris and residue build-up throughout kitchen primarily the walls and floor area around the pizza oven. Walls and floors need to be maintained clean. REPEAT.

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P- *Observed - Box of raw Chicken stored above plastic lidded container of marinated Raw Beef. *CDI - PIC corrected storage order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P- *Observed - Dish Machine chlorine sanitizer below proper concentration 10ppm. *CDI - EHS showed PIC how to prime dish machine to assist unit in achieving proper sanitizing concentration. Unit tested at 50-100ppm after priming.

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- *Observed - most foods in right flip top unit above 41F, see temp chart for details. PIC started all items were prepped this morning around 10-10:30AM. *CDI - PIC placed entire unit on TPHC and will only use make table of unit on TPHC moving forward until unit is repaired and can maintain proper cold holding temperatures. EHS will follow up with establishment to ensure food safety.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
*REPEAT* 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- *Observed - TCS items in Right Flip Top Unit above 41F, see temp chart for details. PIC stated items were cooling in flip top unit from prep this morning around 10:30AM. PIC stated unit was repaired a week or 2 ago and unit was fine this morning upon their arrival this morning when they checked it. Establishment utilizing unit thats in disrepair for cooling. *CDI - PIC moved all items in units lowboy to other refrigeration units to improve cooling rate. Items in Make Table were placed onto 4 hour TPHC Time hold. Items will be discarded at 2:30PM. PIC already possesses written procedures from a previous inspection.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. *Observed - Soy Sauce, Fish Oil and 2 other squeeze bottles without labels to properly identify substances within. *CDI - PIC labeled items.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. *Observed - Screen Back Door unable to fully close, allowing the possibility of pest entry. Close outer openings to avoid possible pest habitants.

*REPEAT* 2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. *Observed - PIC engaged in food prep without the use of a proper hair restraint.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. *Observed - Right Flip Top Unit in disrepair unable to maintain proper cold holding temperature of 41F or less and Left Flip Top Unit Lowboy Door broken and falling off of unit. PIC is able to maintain door shut but cannot use door until repairs are made. PIC stated delivery person ran into unit with cart and broke it.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. *Observed - Baseboards broken, cracked and missing throughout prep area.

violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed a bucket of sauerkraut and a bucket of pickle chips being stored on floor in walk in cooler. CDI: PIC moved containers to wooden pallets.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed a metal bowl being stored in a bus tub of wings which was being used as a scoop. CDI: PIC removed metal bowl from bus tub.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed several boxes of single use containers being stored on stairs leading to second floor. Store single use articles on shelving.

4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used. Observed a stack of single use to go containers being stored outside of their original packaging right side up on prep counter. Store single use containers inverted to protect against contamination.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed build up on floor underneath shelving in walk in cooler and on floor underneath 3 comp sink. Clean as needed.

rows.violation_text:
16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 . Observed several metal containers being stacked within each other with date mark sticker residue on outside of container which was contacting food contact surface of next container. CDI: PIC returned items to be W/R/S. REPEAT.

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ . Observed a container of raw burger patties being held at 43F in grill drawer cooler. CDI: PIC moved burger patties to walk in cooler to rapidly cool.

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed a bucket of sauerkraut and a bucket of pickle chips being stored on floor in walk in cooler. CDI: PIC moved containers to wooden pallets.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed a metal bowl being stored in a bus tub of wings which was being used as a scoop. CDI: PIC removed metal bowl from bus tub.

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed several boxes of single use containers being stored on stairs leading to second floor. Store single use articles on shelving.

4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used. Observed a stack of single use to go containers being stored outside of their original packaging right side up on prep counter. Store single use containers inverted to protect against contamination.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed build up on floor underneath shelving in walk in cooler and on floor underneath 3 comp sink. Clean as needed.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed eggs in a plastic container stored over top of a box of sliced cheese. Ensure that proper storage order procedures are followed. -CDI- Eggs were moved so that they were no longer above RTE foods. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-401.11 Cook raw animal foods to required temperatures. Do not interrupt the cooking process without approved procedures. -P- Observed raw hamburgers cooked on the grill with a final cook temp of 149F-165F depending on the patty and part of the patty cooked. PIC had food employee drop another batch of burgers, burgers were cooked to 149F-165F again. -CDI- All burgers were voluntarily discarded. PIC adjusted the automatic clamshell grill to cook for longer. 
Burgers were properly cooked after adjustment.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed lidded prepped foods such as lettuce and tomato’s that were from prep approximately 1 hour prior in the lowboy cooler -CDI- Once PIC observed the foods from prep cooling in the lowboy he directed a food employee to go and cool the foods in the WIC. Foods were cooling properly by the end of the inspection. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed food employee working with food with no head covering present ensure that all food employees working with food or food contact surfaces are wearing proper hair restraints.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed dish machine with an out of order sign. PIC stated that the dish machine was not working properly. EHS notes that the 3-comp sink is what is being used to W/R/S. PIC stated that a work order is in for the dish machine. All sanitizer was functioning and reading properly at the 3-Comp sink. Ensure that equipment is maintained in good repair.

rows.general_comments: Ensure we are completing all cooling processes, even sliced meats and cheeses, in the WIC, without any plastic wrap or cover.

violations.comments: 5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed one hand sink blcoked with rolling cart. CDI rolling cart moved.
3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed a few items in WIC and WIF that had completed cooling phase with no covers.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed one line of TCS items hot holding (soups), holding below 135F for approximately 1 hour. CDI Employee quickly reheated times to 165F and ensured equipment was turned on. 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed several items holding on the salad/sandwich prep line above 41F for an unknown amount of time. CDI PIC voluntarily discarded and will have unit looked at by repair person. Repeat If facility has cold holding violation next inspection, RCP is recommended. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments: 8-103.12 Comply with the procedures in the HACCP plan as submitted and approved as part of a variance or local approval. Maintain approval and records as required.-P/Pf Observed multiple days in log books both on the computer and written that were missing the pH for the batches of rice. VR required.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed build up and residue on teh outside and handles of RICS, hot boxes, cold drawers. Clean with more frequency. Repeat

violations.comments: 4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. Buildup observed inside ice machine. Clean ice machine more frequently. 0 pts.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Pan of barbacoa in walk-in cooler measured 46F. Employee did not know what day it was from or why it was warm. CDI - Employee discarded barbacoa.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Several TCS RTE foods in walk-in cooler were missing prep date labels. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days of preparation. CDI - Employee discarded items that she didn't know prep date and most of the other foods were prepared yesterday so she put yesterday's date on items. REPEAT

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed one container of shredded lettuce and 3 containers of cut lettuce above 41F after cooling more than 4 hours. CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded items. 

For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed plastic containers wet stacked.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed minor food debris in bottom of reach in freezer and on sides of freezer. Observed greasy residue on refrigeration door handles. Clean as needed.

REPEAT
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed low grout lines in flooring throughout kitchen.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed PIC on duty not certified. 

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed employee beverage stored on shelf above dry good items. CDI- beverage relocated.
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf. Observed no paper towels at hand sink in men’s restroom. CDI- paper towels provided.

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed raw chicken skewers stored above raw salmon in grill drawers. CDI- foods rearranged. Observed individually wrapped packages of raw burgers and raw salmon stored together in reach in freezer. CDI- products separated. 

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed box of unwashed green peppers stored above washed ready to eat tomatoes, onions and cucumbers in walk in cooler. 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P. Observed employee place raw lamb on grill and then place pitas on grill to warm with same pair of gloves. CDI- employee discarded gloves, washed hands and donned clean gloves. Pitas were heated on grill above 165F.

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf. Observed bus tub of French fries on counter and time label not completed. When label was completed, PIC labeled for 11:00am but actual time was 10:45. CDI- time label corrected and education provided to PIC.

Remove and only use approved food contact surfaces.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. *Observed - Build up behind and under cooking equipment in hard to reach areas.

rows.violation_text:
10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: *REPEAT* 6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf- *Observed - Handsink without paper towels or alternative drying device available. *CDI - PIC provided paper towels.

rows.general_comments: Observed the walk-in freezer door is sticking and not opening properly at times. PIC is aware and is working on having it repaired.

violations.comments: 6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf Observed two hand washing sinks in the deli area and one in the bakery area without a hand drying device. Hand washing sinks need to have a hand drying device available. CDI- PIC had papertowels placed at the hand washing sinks during the inspection. REPEAT.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed the TCS buttermilk hand batter for chicken holding at 72F in the flip top unit. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the batter and replaced it with a new batter that was cooled in the walk-in freezer before placing in the flip top unit.

rows.general_comments:
Observed raw meat stored over onions on Don coyotl. Observed raw shelled eggs commingled with shredded cheese in WIC #2. Observed raw shrimp over potatoes at ace of spuds in WIC #2. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. PIC was in process of daily walk through and had already placed “parking tickets” at some of the shared spaces, which indicates to the individual PICs that these items need to be corrected.

Observed one PIC in WIC #2 cooling rice with protective plastic coverings and stacked on top of one another. EHS educated PIC about proper cooling methods and items unstacked and protective coverings removed.

violations.comments:
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. Observed 4 handwashing sinks without handwashing signs. -CDI- PIC posted handsink signage. -0-
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf- Observed one handwashing sink with no soap. -CDI- PIC replenished soap supply. 
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf- Observed one handsink with no paper towels. -CDI- PIC replenished paper towel supply.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris on shared slicer stored as clean. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. -1.5-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. Observed dark buildup on inside of ice machine at ice chute. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). In WIC observed no date mark on housemade broth in at Mazali, rice and vegetable mix with no dates at sabor limon, sliced watermelon date marked 6/13 (discard 6/20), and bacon wrapped watermelon 6/15 (discard 6/21) at Solo, macaroni noodles 6/6 (discard 6/12), rice 6/10 (discard 6/16) at crown and tide, and seafood rice 6/7 (discard 6/13), noodles with no date mark at Mem’s Thai RIC, and heavy cream date marked 5/13 (discard 5/20) at Grey’s RIC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed flying pests throughout. Per PIC, pest control frequency has increased. **REPEAT** -1-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-402.11 Fixed equipment shall be spaced to allow access for cleaning around the equipment or sealed to adjoining equipment or walls. Observed some sinks in need of recaulking/resealing. PIC stated that she had removed some of the caulking because it is set to be repaired this week. -0-
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed gaskets lifting from door of WIC. Repair/replace. Per PIC, gaskets have been ordered. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed some rusted shelving.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tops of dish machine and some equipment beneath hoods in need of cleaning. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. -0-

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed dust buildup on some ceilings and vent covers throughout. Observed top of paper towel dispenser by handsink at BOH/near dishpit/3-comp sink in need of cleaning. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed detached baseboard throughout shared kitchen in need of repair. Per PIC, work order has been placed for baseboards and set to be repaired this week. **REPEAT** -0.5-

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf Observed sesame chicken held on time without time stamp. PIC added correct time stamp.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf observed several items cooling in dense plastic containers not moving temperature. CDI - PIC separated into smaller portions and moved to WIF for rapid cooling. PIC stated they will cool things in WIF for all products to rapidly cooling to ensure temp requirements. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments: 3-304.11 (A) Food shall only contact surfaces of properly cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils. -P Observed two (2) portions of food (grilled chicken and diced tomatoes) in relatively clean containers in prep unit 3 nesting within spinach bin. CDI - PIC removed containers from food and rotated to prevent potential contamination.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed three (3) out of date, non date mark exempt TCS food items (burrata prepared 6/13, sliced tomatoes prepared 6/14, and undated meatballs) in prep line 1 (closest to bar.) Per conversation with PIC, items without dates in prep units were prepared on day inspection and are to be held for less than 24 hours. CDI - PIC voluntarily disposed of food held for greater than 7 days.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Observed one (1) employee preparing food (lasagna trays, etc.) while wearing wrist band. REPEAT.

Note: No escalation in point deduction due to some improvement; only one (1) food employee was observed with hand, arm, or wrist jewelry.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed one (1) in use wiping cloth held at prep line, above in use wiping cloth bucket. CDI - Bucket was remade and calibrated with at 3 compartment sink and wiping cloths was returned to bucket.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. - Observed one (1) container of water used to hold in use utensils at the cookline in an insert being held 124F water on the prep counter at the time of the inspection. All other water containers used to hold in use utensils were observed at 135F or above. PIC has been informed that they must keep the insert container of water at 135F or to not use water to store in use utensils.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed approximately six (6) clean containers stored on drying rack in a manner that did not facilitate air drying (wet stacking). CDI - Containers were separated out to allow for air drying. REPEAT.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed three (3) multi use children’s drinking cups at ice machine shelving with pits and discoloration. Per conversation with PIC, cups had not been used since switch to single service cups for children. CDI - PIC voluntarily disposed of distressed multi use utensils. 

Note: EHS initially observed high temperature dish machine failing to hit 160F on high temperature dish plates. After rerunning machine, final temperatures above 160F were observed. PIC has stated that repair personnel have been contacted.

5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed one (1) of two faucets at 3 compartment sink not dispensing water. Observed water flowing from hot water heater in outdoor riser room. Repair and maintain water systems as required

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed three (3) broken tiles at walk in cooler entrance. Per conversation with PIC, work order exists to replace broken titles. REPEAT. 

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed minor soils accumulation on floors near clean in place equipment (I.E. bread slicer) and in WIC. Clean floors as needed to prevent soils accumulation on.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-403.11 (A)Areas designated for employees to eat and drink, shall be located so that food, equipment, linens, single service/use items are protected from contamination. Observed one (1) employee drink stored above unused food prep table. CDI - Drink was disposed of prior to EHS correction.

rows.violation_text:
15 - Food separated & protected: 3-304.11 (A) Food shall only contact surfaces of properly cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils. -P Observed two (2) portions of food (grilled chicken and diced tomatoes) in relatively clean containers in prep unit 3 nesting within spinach bin. CDI - PIC removed containers from food and rotated to prevent potential contamination.

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed three (3) out of date, non date mark exempt TCS food items (burrata prepared 6/13, sliced tomatoes prepared 6/14, and undated meatballs) in prep line 1 (closest to bar.) Per conversation with PIC, items without dates in prep units were prepared on day inspection and are to be held for less than 24 hours. CDI - PIC voluntarily disposed of food held for greater than 7 days.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

40 - Personal cleanliness: 2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Observed one (1) employee preparing food (lasagna trays, etc.) while wearing wrist band. REPEAT.

Note: No escalation in point deduction due to some improvement; only one (1) food employee was observed with hand, arm, or wrist jewelry.

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed one (1) in use wiping cloth held at prep line, above in use wiping cloth bucket. CDI - Bucket was remade and calibrated with at 3 compartment sink and wiping cloths was returned to bucket.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. - Observed one (1) container of water used to hold in use utensils at the cookline in an insert being held 124F water on the prep counter at the time of the inspection. All other water containers used to hold in use utensils were observed at 135F or above. PIC has been informed that they must keep the insert container of water at 135F or to not use water to store in use utensils.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed approximately six (6) clean containers stored on drying rack in a manner that did not facilitate air drying (wet stacking). CDI - Containers were separated out to allow for air drying. REPEAT.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed three (3) multi use children’s drinking cups at ice machine shelving with pits and discoloration. Per conversation with PIC, cups had not been used since switch to single service cups for children. CDI - PIC voluntarily disposed of distressed multi use utensils. 

Note: EHS initially observed high temperature dish machine failing to hit 160F on high temperature dish plates. After rerunning machine, final temperatures above 160F were observed. PIC has stated that repair personnel have been contacted.

51 - Plumbing installed; proper backflow devices: 5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed one (1) of two faucets at 3 compartment sink not dispensing water. Observed water flowing from hot water heater in outdoor riser room. Repair and maintain water systems as required

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed three (3) broken tiles at walk in cooler entrance. Per conversation with PIC, work order exists to replace broken titles. REPEAT. 

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed minor soils accumulation on floors near clean in place equipment (I.E. bread slicer) and in WIC. Clean floors as needed to prevent soils accumulation on.

56 - Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used: 6-403.11 (A)Areas designated for employees to eat and drink, shall be located so that food, equipment, linens, single service/use items are protected from contamination. Observed one (1) employee drink stored above unused food prep table. CDI - Drink was disposed of prior to EHS correction.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments:
2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur.
EHS observed two employee beverages stored on shelves above prep area and expo area. 
CDI- PIC relocated the drinks to an approved location. 
-0-

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
EHS observed a pan of cheese whiz in hot holding cabinet at 125F. 
CDI- PIC reheated the cheese to 165F and placed it back into the hot box. 
-0- 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
EHS observed several items in prep unit #3 that were holding above 41F. PIC stated that a temperature check was conducted at 11AM and all food was at 41F. PIC instructed food employee to place items on ice to help rapidly cool them. Ambient air temperature was 49F in the lower portion of the unit and 47F in the upper portion. After approximately 45 minutes items in the cooler had began to cool, however the ambient air temperature of the unit is still at 44F. 
CDI- PIC is placing entire unit on ice - Emergency TPHC if needed. 3- day VR Required. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed a burrito in the hot holding drawer at 127F. PIC stated that the burrito would be discarded after a 30 minute time hold. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the burrito. Ensure that all foods in hot holding are maintained at 135F and above. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed ranch cups that were prepped approximately an hour prior to the EHS arrival. Ranch cups were present in the front of house lowboy cooler with lids on and stacked together. The ranch’s were recorded at 44F, 30 minutes later a second temperature was recorded at 44F. -CDI- PIC moved the ranch cups to cool in the WIC, ranch cups were properly cooling by the end of the inspection. Ensure proper cooling methods such as venting cooling foods and using adequate cooling equipment. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed several food employees with no hair restraints present upon EHSs arrival to the facility. -CDI- Food employees placed a hair restraint on after inspection began. 
Ensure all food employees who are working with food or food contact surfaces are wearing some form of hair restraint.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed a build up of food debris and residue on the batter station NFCSs, on the sides of cooking equipment, and on the sides of holding equipment. Ensure that all non-food contact surfaces are cleaned and maintained as clean.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed a floor tile missing upon entry into WIC 1, observed small chips in floor tiles throughout the facility. Observed low grout present on the floors throughout the facility. 
Ensure that floors are designed to be smooth and easily cleanable.

rows.facility_name: BAD DADDY'S BURGER BAR OF BALLANTYNE
rows.address:
15105 JOHN J DELANEY DR SUITE A
 
CHARLOTTE, NC 28277

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P -Observed raw bacon stored over ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the prep unit cooler. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the raw bacon.
-REPEAT-


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. -Observed food residue among multiple dishes, stored as clean. -CDI- Dishes were moved back to dish pit to be re-washed/rinsed/sanitized. 
-REPEAT- No point escalation due improvement in sticker residue since last inspection. EHS observed no sticker residue present on clean dishes. PIC stated facility switched to dissolvable stickers.-


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P -Observed queso holding below 135F in hot holding steam well. -CDI- Because queso was originally cooked less than one hour ago, PIC reheated queso to 165F within 30 minutes.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 

-Observed hard boiled eggs above 41F in prep top cooler. Observed eggs above fill-line of container. Top portion of hard boiled eggs were above 41F, while bottom portion of eggs (below fill-line) was properly cold holding below 41F. PIC stated eggs were filled this morning, less than one hour ago. -CDI- PIC removed top half of hard boiled eggs and placed in WIC for rapid cooling. Going forward, facility will not fill hard boiled eggs above fill line to ensure proper cold holding.

-Observed portioned cups of buttermilk ranch and chipotle ranch left at room temperature on server expo line. PIC stated they hold the portioned cups at room temperature all day. Both ranches contain sour cream and buttermilk. -CDI- PIC placed the chipotle ranch and buttermilk ranch on 4-hour TPHC, and added both food items to 4-hour TPHC procedures. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed multiple foods throughout facility without a date mark (grilled mushrooms, corn, sliced tomatoes, diced tomatoes, queso, jalapeno cheddar sauce, cooked noodles, hot-brined pickled red onions, hot dogs, mozzarella cheese, pimento cheese, black bean burgers, beyond burgers, deviled egg mix, spring mix, romaine). 
-CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the cooked noodles and pickled onions, as PIC could not verify prep dates for those.
-CDI- PIC verified prep/cook dates for all other food items by discussing with chef and referencing large batches in WIC and properly date marked all foods listed above, except noodles and pickled onions.
REPEAT


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. -Observed flour/breader containers without labels. Label all containers with the common name of the food inside.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. -Observed multiple flies throughout kitchen during inspection. EHS recommends increasing frequency of pest control services during summer months to reduce insect presence.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. -Observed container of deviled eggs directly on floor in WIC.

6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf -Observed two heavily dented cans stored among cans intended for customer use. -CDI- PIC removed cans from shelves and placed in office for discard or credit.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. -Observed in-use wiping cloth stored directly on prep top surface, not within sanitizer solution. -CDI- PIC placed wiping cloth is soiled laundry bin.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. -Observed sticker intact on avocado that has been cut and placed in prep cooler. Remove stickers during the wash process.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. -Observed bowls used for scooping breader mixture submerged within the food. -CDI- PIC removed bowls from breader.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. -Observed numerous stacks of clean dishing being stored on top of each other wet. EHS advised to stagger dishes to allow for adequate air drying prior to storage.
REPEAT

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. -Observed interior surfaces of reach-in coolers, freezers, and cooler gaskets with visible accumulation of food residue. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation.
REPEAT

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. -Observed floors in WIC with buildup of food debris. Increase frequency of cleaning.

REPEAT

6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf -Observed two heavily dented cans stored among cans intended for customer use. -CDI- PIC removed cans from shelves and placed in office for discard or credit.

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. -Observed in-use wiping cloth stored directly on prep top surface, not within sanitizer solution. -CDI- PIC placed wiping cloth is soiled laundry bin.

42 - Washing fruits & vegetables: 3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. -Observed sticker intact on avocado that has been cut and placed in prep cooler. Remove stickers during the wash process.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. -Observed bowls used for scooping breader mixture submerged within the food. -CDI- PIC removed bowls from breader.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. -Observed numerous stacks of clean dishing being stored on top of each other wet. EHS advised to stagger dishes to allow for adequate air drying prior to storage.
REPEAT

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. -Observed interior surfaces of reach-in coolers, freezers, and cooler gaskets with visible accumulation of food residue. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation.
REPEAT

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. -Observed floors in WIC with buildup of food debris. Increase frequency of cleaning.

56 - Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. -Observed employee food stored above customer food in prep unit cooler. Observed employee bag stored above onion bags in dry storage. -CDI- PIC relocated employee belongings to prevent contamination. 
REPEAT

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed sliced roast beef in hot tray between 126F-137F other hot tray was holding at 137F throughout. For the tray below 135F PIC stated that the tray had been in HH for 1 Hour. -CDI- PIC moved the tray into the oven and reheated the roast beef to above 165F before placing back onto HH. Ensure that all foods on HH are maintained at 135F or above. REPEAT



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed tomatoes, caramelized onions, and gyro above 41F in the make station unit. PIC stated that the foods went into the unit this morning at approximately 10:00 PM. -CDI- PIC moved foods above 41F to WIC to cool down, foods cooled by end of the inspection. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P

Observed employee handle raw salmon, change gloves with no handwash, and then handle single service items. CDI: Employee was educated on when to properly Washburn hands. Employee washed hands and donned new gloves.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. Pf Observed 2 dented cans of salsa in basement storage area on the can rack. CDI: Cans removed to designated area for return.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed cold smoked salmon stored over ready to eat dressings in salad walk in. CDI: Items reorganized. 
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over ready to eat items at juice station. Observed unwashed berries stored over pickled peppers in reach in part of cooler at nourish station. CDI: Items reorganized. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

violations.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
violations.comments: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed pan of meatballs reheated to 130F. CDI: Meatballs placed back in steamer and reheated to over 165F.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed sea bass, short rib, and mashed potatoes in hotbox for a catering held below 135F. Hot box was at 134F per digital face on unit. CDI: Items rapidly reheated to over 165F. Hot box moved to 170F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed sliced and shredded cheese in cooler drawers, and pasteurized eggs and pasteurized egg whites in reach in cooler above 41F. CDI: Items moved to walk in cooler to cool. Ambient air temps and temps of other items in different areas of the unit were at or below 41F. Ensure units are not overloaded with items preventing air flow and that cooler drawers are closed completely between use. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P Observed calzones pulled to TPHC prior to reaching 41F. CDI: Product returned to walk in to continue cooling. 
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf Observed no time marked for lettuce or quinoa at nourish station.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.

rows.general_comments:
Cold holding on the makeline has been out constantly during inspections, likely due to the broken lids on the equipment as it is not able to maintain a tight seal. If cold holding is found to be out of compliance during next routine inspection facility will be placed on a risk control plan for cold holding. 

EHS advised that permit states “This FOOD ESTABLISHMENT, when providing an open air atmosphere by use of garage doors, open wall or other means to a dining area must in operation effective measures in to prevent the presence of insects, rodents or other public health pests. The roll-up doors in the dining area are to be secured at all times until an air curtain or screens are installed.” EHS told employees they are not permitted to open garage door until air curtain is installed.
