violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments: 2-102.11 (C ) (2), (3), (17) The Person in charge shall demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this code by (A) Complying with the Food Code by having no violations of Priority Items during the current inspection, (B) Being a certified food protection manager, or (C) by responding correctly to the inspector's questions related to employee health process and procedures. -Pf observed PIC fail to demonstrate sufficient knowledge of foodborne disease, prevention, facility had multiple priority and priority foundation violations, and no certification for certified food protection manager was provided. CDI coached.

violations.description: Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events
violations.comments:
2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf observed no Fecal vomitus plan provided. CDI handout red distributed.
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed dishes stored in hand sink. CDI. PIC removed. Repeat
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. OBSERVED one of the sink without hand sign. Cdi. Hand sign was provided.

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed multiple containers of uncovered food. Repeat. 
**** improvement from last time.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed sauces, Cold holding in prep area at temperatures above 41F. CDI. PIC voluntarily discarded. Repeat


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 

Observed items in the RI and prep unit without dates. CDI. PIC dated. 
Facility has improved dates in WI coolers but items in prep and RI unit are still missing dates. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed wet stacking of multiple dishes in the kitchen area. Repeat

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed cleaning needed on non food contact parts of equipments, micro waves. Repeat

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee items stored in same place as customer items. REPEAT. 
*** improvement from last time.

rows.violation_text:
2 - Certified Food Protection Manager: 2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no certified food protection manager on duty during inspection. Repeat 






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3 - Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting: 2-102.11 (C ) (2), (3), (17) The Person in charge shall demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this code by (A) Complying with the Food Code by having no violations of Priority Items during the current inspection, (B) Being a certified food protection manager, or (C) by responding correctly to the inspector's questions related to employee health process and procedures. -Pf observed PIC fail to demonstrate sufficient knowledge of foodborne disease, prevention, facility had multiple priority and priority foundation violations, and no certification for certified food protection manager was provided. CDI coached.

5 - Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events: 2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf observed no Fecal vomitus plan provided. CDI handout red distributed.
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A,D,E,K) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf. Food should be from approved sources, handwashing should be monitored, Food establishment operations should not be conducted in a private home.
 



A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A) Employees shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and other items needing protection can not result. EHS observed an employee meal on a prep top unit. CDI. Food employee moved their meal to a designated area.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P. EHS observed boxes of unwashed produce on a shelf over washed and peeled onions. Also observed raw shell eggs resting on top of a box with employee beverages inside.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. REHS observed several dishes with food debris and residue on the inside.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). PIC stated that they had stickers for date marking but did not have datemarks on any TCS foods throughout the establishment.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf. Establishment had TPHC procedures but did not have any stickers or documentation of the discard time.

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf. Facility was thawing under running water under 70F as instructed, however when EHS took a temperature of the shrimp it had reached 66 F. CDI. PIC moved the thawed shrimp to the WIC to get it down to 41F. * REPEAT *
3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Bins of flour and seasonings were missing labels.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf. Two cans were dented and not in a designated area.


3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. EHS observed a large container of seasoning on a shelf without a lid.
2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Food employee not utilizing a hair covering.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. EHS observed a scoop for flour completely submerged in the food.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Rice spatula was sitting in a container of water not at 135F. CDI. PIC relocated the spatula to a clean prep table instead.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Wet stacking was observed throughout the clean dish storage area.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent. Equipment throughout the establishment that is not food safe or ANSI certified, including a rice cooker, and two deep freezers.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments: 4-301.12(B) Sink compartments shall be large enough to accommodate immersion of the largest equipment and utensils.-Pf Facility possesses several large bowls that are not able to be immersed in the three compartment sink.

5-203.13 Provide at least one service sink or curbed cleaning facility for cleaning of mops and disposing of mop water. Mop Sink is unable to be utilized due to the amount of items being stored inside. Items should be relocated so the mop sink can be used for it’s intended purpose.

violations.description: Toilet facilities: properly constructed, supplied & cleaned
violations.comments: 5-501.17 Provide a covered waste bin in female restrooms. The women’s restroom was out of order during the time of inspection so all customers were utilizing the ‘mens’ restroom. The trash can did not have a lid.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments:
5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place. Shared dumpster was missing drain plugs.
5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Dumpster doors were open at time of inspection.

6-202.112 Living or sleeping quarters shall be separated from rooms and areas used for food establishment operations by complete partitioning and solid self-closing doors. A cot was observed in the dry storage area of the establishment. A separate living space requires a separation of space achieved through partitioning.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Half of a ceiling tile was missing in the dry storage area.


6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Significant amount of food debris and residue were observed throughout the establishment. Increase cleaning frequency.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity. No light is available in the dry storage area near the canned goods. A light should be added to discourage pests and avoid injury.



6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. EHS observed employee items throughout the establishment (phones, coffee).

6-403.11 (A)Areas designated for employees to eat and drink, shall be located so that food, equipment, linens, single service/use items are protected from contamination. Keep employee food and drinks in designated spaces. * REPEAT *

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety including handwashing, unadulterated foods, properly cooling foods, maintaining cold foods at 41F or below, consumer advisory on the menu for undercooked meats, properly sanitizing multi-use equipment and utensils. -Pf 




A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P Observed food employee wash hands and then re contaminate hands my turning facet off with bare hands. CDI - re washed hands and turned off facet with paper towel.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P Observed several bulging cans in dry storage over ready to eat food.CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded all cans

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed several raw animal proteins (chicken, beef, shrimp) with improper storage order (chicken over shrimp, beef over shrimp, chicken over beef). CDI - PIC rearrange storage order.


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed several dishes (10+) with the debris, stored as clean. The slicer also had dried food debris from a previous use. CDI - PIC moved to dish machine for re-washing. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized.


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Mixture of vegetables including cut cabbage were cut and placed into Eight large plastic containers and then stacked two days ago. All eight measured from 44F-47F, CDI-all eight containers were voluntarily discarded





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed several items cold holding on countertop with ice above 41. CDI - PIC move to walk-in freezer for rapid cooling.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed several items held longer than 24 hours with no date mark. CDI - PIC added correct dates. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments: 3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf observed menu serving raw undercooked items without a reminder. EHS will return in 10 days to verify reminder is added to menu.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf Observed sanitizer spray bottle without labeling. CDI - PIC added correct labels.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf observed several items being cooled from multiple days ago missing cooling window in plastic tubs, dense portions, and in close containers. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded items.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed several squirt bottles and seasoning shakers with out labeling.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed several live roaches in facility. Increase frequency of pest control.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-303.12(B)(C)(D) Unpackaged food may not be stored in direct contact with undrained ice except: whole raw fruits or vegetables; cut raw vegetables such as celery, carrots, or potatoes; tofu; and raw poultry and raw fish received immersed in ice. Observed cabbage, lettuce, tofu stored in direct ice. 

3-304.13 Linens and napkins may not be used in contact with food unless used to line a container for the service of foods and are replaced each time the container is refilled for a new customer. Observed linen napkins stored in direct contact with the cabbage mixture and cut onions in the walk in cooler.

3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed ready to eat items and raw items stored on floor and walk-in freezer.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed unwashed cut, broccoli and cabbage being used for service and stored for use.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. observed and use utensil with handle inside food container. CDI - PIC removed in use utensil.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed and use utensil for rice being stored in room temperature water. CDI - PIC move to three pump sink for proper cleaning.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed several dishes, stored as clean witnessing. CDI - moved back to dish machine for re washing.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed wooden steam baskets used for cooking vegetables in serving to customers. EHS will return within 72 hours to verify an alternate means to steam vegetables is being used.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. observed dumpster with door opened. CDI - PIC closed lid.

rows.violation_text:
1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety including handwashing, unadulterated foods, properly cooling foods, maintaining cold foods at 41F or below, consumer advisory on the menu for undercooked meats, properly sanitizing multi-use equipment and utensils. -Pf 




A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

8 - Hands clean & properly washed: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P Observed food employee wash hands and then re contaminate hands my turning facet off with bare hands. CDI - re washed hands and turned off facet with paper towel.

13 - Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P Observed several bulging cans in dry storage over ready to eat food.CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded all cans

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed several raw animal proteins (chicken, beef, shrimp) with improper storage order (chicken over shrimp, beef over shrimp, chicken over beef). CDI - PIC rearrange storage order.


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed several dishes (10+) with the debris, stored as clean. The slicer also had dried food debris from a previous use. CDI - PIC moved to dish machine for re-washing. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized.


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

20 - Proper cooling time & temperatures: 3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Mixture of vegetables including cut cabbage were cut and placed into Eight large plastic containers and then stacked two days ago. All eight measured from 44F-47F, CDI-all eight containers were voluntarily discarded





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed several items cold holding on countertop with ice above 41. CDI - PIC move to walk-in freezer for rapid cooling.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed several items held longer than 24 hours with no date mark. CDI - PIC added correct dates. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

25 - Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods: 3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf observed menu serving raw undercooked items without a reminder. EHS will return in 10 days to verify reminder is added to menu.

28 - Toxic substances properly identified stored & used: 7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf Observed sanitizer spray bottle without labeling. CDI - PIC added correct labels.

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf observed several items being cooled from multiple days ago missing cooling window in plastic tubs, dense portions, and in close containers. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded items.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

37 - Food properly labeled: original container: 3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed several squirt bottles and seasoning shakers with out labeling.

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed several live roaches in facility. Increase frequency of pest control.

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-303.12(B)(C)(D) Unpackaged food may not be stored in direct contact with undrained ice except: whole raw fruits or vegetables; cut raw vegetables such as celery, carrots, or potatoes; tofu; and raw poultry and raw fish received immersed in ice. Observed cabbage, lettuce, tofu stored in direct ice. 

3-304.13 Linens and napkins may not be used in contact with food unless used to line a container for the service of foods and are replaced each time the container is refilled for a new customer. Observed linen napkins stored in direct contact with the cabbage mixture and cut onions in the walk in cooler.

3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed ready to eat items and raw items stored on floor and walk-in freezer.

42 - Washing fruits & vegetables: 3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed unwashed cut, broccoli and cabbage being used for service and stored for use.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. observed and use utensil with handle inside food container. CDI - PIC removed in use utensil.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed and use utensil for rice being stored in room temperature water. CDI - PIC move to three pump sink for proper cleaning.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed several dishes, stored as clean witnessing. CDI - moved back to dish machine for re washing.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed wooden steam baskets used for cooking vegetables in serving to customers. EHS will return within 72 hours to verify an alternate means to steam vegetables is being used.

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. observed dumpster with door opened. CDI - PIC closed lid.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no CFPM present at the facility.
Ensure there is a CFPM present during all hours of operation.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf
Observed no paper towels at the handsink.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed pan of grilled steak on the stovetop with some pieces temping below 135F. Observed pan of fried whole tilapia and pan of grilled chicken leg quarters hot holding below 135F in steam table 1. Observed large pan of fried, meaty chicharrones hot holding below 135F in steam table 2. 
CDI: Grilled steak, fried whole tilapia, and grilled chicken leg quarters were are reheated to above 165F in the oven, and fried meaty chicharrones were voluntarily discarded by PIC since they had been fried last night and not reheated today.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed a few TCS food items cold holding inside the CH case above 41F. PIC stated these items were moved from the WIC into the case moments before start of the inspection. 
CDI: Items were moved into the WIC to rapidly cool down to 41F.
Observed portioned 2-oz cups of green salsa cold holding above 41F inside make table 1. All other TCS food items were temping at proper temps in this cold holding unit.
CDI: Salsa was moved to WIC to cool down. 
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed container of roasted poblanos and container of refried beans both stored inside make table 1 that bore no date labels. PIC stated these were made yesterday.
CDI: PIC created date labels for both TCS food items. 
Observed opened jug of milk stored inside make table 1 that bore no date label.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded milk.
Observed two pans of cooked carnitas, two pans of cooked barbacoa, and a bowl of boiled pork all stored inside the WIC that bore no date labels. PIC stated all three TCS food items were cooked yesterday. 
CDI: PIC created date labels for above mentioned TCS food items. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed an unlabeled spray bottle stored on the chemical storage rack.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded spray bottle.

4-302.12 Provide an accessible thermometer for use. Provide a thin probe thermometer for accurate measure of thin foods.-Pf .
Observed thin probe thermometer was out of battery. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor.
Observed 2 tubs of strawberry filling stored on the floor inside the WIC. Observed 4 tubs of yellow cherries in syrup stored on the floor inside the WIC.
Keep all food stored at least 6 inches off of the floor to prevent contamination.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils.
Observed two food employees actively engaging in food prep without any sort of hair restraints.
Ensure food employees have some sort of hair restraint when actively engaging in food prep to prevent contamination.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used.
Observed multiple stacks of single-serve containers and plates stored right-side up, out of it's protective packaging, throughout the kitchen.
Ensure all single-serve articles are stored inverted when out of it's original protective packaging to prevent contamination.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed rusted shelves in cold holding units throughout the facility.
Replace rusted shelves so that surfaces are smooth and easily cleanable.
REPEAT VIOLATION.
*Note: Although a repeat violation, EHS not deducting full points today since different equipment from previous report was observed not in good repair.

violations.description: Hot & cold water available; adequate pressure
violations.comments:
5-103.11(B) Provide sufficient hot water to meet the peak hot water demands.-Pf
Observed hot water at the 3-compartment sink temping at 76F, unable to meet minimum hot water temp of 110F. Detergent used at the 3-compartment sink did not state any temperature requirement for low temp use. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance. PICs were told to boil large pots of hot water to wash their multi-use equipment and utensils at the 3-compartment sink temporarily until repairman checks on the plumbing at the 3-compartment sink.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P. Observed raw shrimp being stored above rte foods inside lowboy cooler. CDI: Storage order was rearranged. **REPEAT** -1.5- 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed multiple plastic containers, squeeze bottles, and utensils soiled with food debris while being stored as clean. Observed multiple dishes with visible sticker residue present, dishes were stacked so that sticker residue is touching the food contact surface. CDI: Items were placed to be rewashed. **REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed back prep unit holding ambient temperatures at 48F. Items inside the unit were found to be above 41F (chipotle peppers, crab Rangoon mix(x2)). See temperature chart. PIC stated that they do not regular check the temperature of this unit. Chipotle peppers were voluntarily discarded. Crab Rangoon mix was placed into WIC as PIC stated it was removed from the walk in and placed in the prep unit approximately 30 minutes before. **REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed bean sprouts, BK sauce (fresh garlic), Au ju, cut cabbage and cooked chicken without dates marks present. Observed cut cabbage with a prep date of 1/28, shredded chicken with a prep date of 1/23, and cooked chipotle peppers with a prep date of 12/5. CDI: Items beyond the 7 days were voluntarily discarded. EHS allowed PIC to date items as they could be traced got larger portions in the walk in cooler. **REPEAT** -1.5- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed crab Rangoon mix (x2) with a cooling rate of 0F/min over 110minutes. EHS observed that items were in large portions in sealed zip lock bags. CDI- EHS provided education and PIC had employee flatten bags and place in walk in freezer. **REPEAT** -1- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed a large amount of small flying insects throughout the kitchen area. **REPEAT** -1-

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed staff preparing/handling food without the use of a beard guard. -0-

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed plastic container being stored down inside container in lowboy freezer on the cookline. CDI- PIC removed the container. **REPEAT** -0.5-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed multiple dishes stored as clean and stacked wet. -0-

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed multiple boxes of single service items stored on the floor of outdoor storage shed. -0-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed shelving in dry storage area rusted. Replace. **REPEAT** -0.5-

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed food debris build up in bottom of reach in freezers. **REPEAT** -1-

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed damaged flooring throughout facility. Observed floor lifting in the Walk in freezer. Repair. **REPEAT** -0.5-

Observed multiple plastic containers, squeeze bottles, and utensils soiled with food debris while being stored as clean. Observed multiple dishes with visible sticker residue present, dishes were stacked so that sticker residue is touching the food contact surface. CDI: Items were placed to be rewashed. **REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed back prep unit holding ambient temperatures at 48F. Items inside the unit were found to be above 41F (chipotle peppers, crab Rangoon mix(x2)). See temperature chart. PIC stated that they do not regular check the temperature of this unit. Chipotle peppers were voluntarily discarded. Crab Rangoon mix was placed into WIC as PIC stated it was removed from the walk in and placed in the prep unit approximately 30 minutes before. **REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed bean sprouts, BK sauce (fresh garlic), Au ju, cut cabbage and cooked chicken without dates marks present. Observed cut cabbage with a prep date of 1/28, shredded chicken with a prep date of 1/23, and cooked chipotle peppers with a prep date of 12/5. CDI: Items beyond the 7 days were voluntarily discarded. EHS allowed PIC to date items as they could be traced got larger portions in the walk in cooler. **REPEAT** -1.5- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed crab Rangoon mix (x2) with a cooling rate of 0F/min over 110minutes. EHS observed that items were in large portions in sealed zip lock bags. CDI- EHS provided education and PIC had employee flatten bags and place in walk in freezer. **REPEAT** -1- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed a large amount of small flying insects throughout the kitchen area. **REPEAT** -1-

40 - Personal cleanliness: 2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed staff preparing/handling food without the use of a beard guard. -0-

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed plastic container being stored down inside container in lowboy freezer on the cookline. CDI- PIC removed the container. **REPEAT** -0.5-

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed multiple dishes stored as clean and stacked wet. -0-

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed multiple boxes of single service items stored on the floor of outdoor storage shed. -0-

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed shelving in dry storage area rusted. Replace. **REPEAT** -0.5-

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed food debris build up in bottom of reach in freezers. **REPEAT** -1-

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed damaged flooring throughout facility. Observed floor lifting in the Walk in freezer. Repair. **REPEAT** -0.5-

rows.general_comments:
NOTE: Maintenance came on site to fix hot water for 3 comp sink. 

EHS assessed cooling and capacity during inspection. Facility does not have the capacity to cook and cool all of their items without either a dedicated cooler or walk in cooler. Items cooked and cooled listed below. Facility does not have a dedicated cooler for cooling. Cooling procedure is to leave them out on the prep table to get to room temp then place into the tall reach in units. Temps are taken next day around 7pm. Employees get to the restaurant at 11/11:30am to check temps. Facility closes at 1:30am. EHS advised them to take temperatures before leaving the facility to ensure food is at 41F or below prior to leaving. EHS is requesting SOPs for cooling for review. 

rice (cooked in large batches), onion sauce, toamto sauce, lentils, paneer, cauliflower (fried), baby corn (fried), eggs, chicken, lamb, goat, chick peas

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments: 2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed violations with Cold holding, date marking, bare hand contact, proper washing of dishes, contamination of equipment/ utensils, storage order among other things (see violations below). VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed employee wash dishes in handwashing sink. CDI- Removed and relocated to 3 comp sink to sit until sink is fixed.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw fish, eggs, chicken, lamb and ground meats stored above ready to eat food items throughout. CDI- ALL items placed on the bottom. Observed raw ground meats stored above unpackaged prepped items in the tall reach in freezer. Items could not be rearranged at the time. Keep raw meats on the bottom. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN10 DAYS. 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed slicer and knives stored clean with lots of old food debris. CDI- Taken to the sink to be cleaned. Facility could not clean dishes because there was no hot water. Dish wash solution did not state anywhere that it was low temp. EHS asked for sink to be set up for washing. Bleach water was ran in the middle basin. Soiled dishes were placed in the 1st and 3rd basin. Manager took slicer from first basin, wiped it, sprayed it with the spray arm and dipped it into the sanitizer. EHS explained proper way to clean dishes. Facility will close due to incapability to wash dishes. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS. Wash, rinse and sanitize dishes for cleaning. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed 5 containers of onion sauce holding above 41F in tall reach in cooler 1. Observed goat, 5 containers of biryani holding above 41F in tall RI cooler 2. Observed 8 containers of tomato/ onion sauce mix holding above 41F stored on prep table shelf. Items must be held under refrigeration. Observed fried onions held out on speed rack under the bread above 41F. EHS asked how long they had been there and if they were in use. PIC stated that they use them for customers and they had been out on the speed rack over night. Oninos connt be held out overnight. CDI- All items were discarded. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed several containers of onion sauce, tomato sauce, rice, milk without dates. CDI- Items dated with the day they were prepared. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. -P Observed household raid stored beside onions. CDI- Removed. 
7-202.12(A) Use poisonous or toxic materials according to manufacturer's label. Use pesticides approved for use in commercial food service only.-PObserved household raid used at facility. Use commercial pest control only.

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed chicken thawing without water. CDI- water ran on thawing chicken.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed roach smashed on wall behind knives. EHS went to place knives in 3 comp sink for cleaning and noticed that the roach had been removed. Observed gnats in kitchen

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed cut onions stored in a container on the floor. CDI- Onions removed.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed onions and herbs being prepped for cutting without being washed. CDI- Items taken to be washed.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed handles of scoops in flour.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean dishes stored near soiled dishes at 3 comp sink. Observed clean dishes stored at prep sink with raw meat. Dishes taken to be cleaned.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed gasket heavily damaged at tall reach in freezer.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed flour bins and sauce bottles in need of cleaning.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floors and walls in need of cleaning throughout.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee coat stored on dry rack with customer items. Keep employee items in designated area.

rows.general_comments: OSV- Cold drawers under grill were repaired. Unit holding at 34F.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A) Employees shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and other items needing protection can not result. Observed employee on grill dip hand into expired pico and eat it on the line above prep area. CDI- Discarded.

2-301.14 Wash hands before engaging in food preparation including working with exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and unwrapped single service and single use articles. -P Observed employee handle soiled wishes, then go to get clean ones without hand wash. Observed Employee handle soiled equipment then go to handle food without hand wash. CDI- Hands washed. 
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P Observed employee handling soiled dishes, touch mouth and complete other tasks inside and outside of the kitchen, Then put gloves on to prepare food without washing hands.

3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P Observed employee wipe gloved hands on pants, then go to prepare food. CDI- Gloves changed and hands washed.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed piles of plates with old food debris. CDI- Dishes taken to be cleaned. Observed slicer stored clean with old food debris. CDI- Slicer taken to be cleaned. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed food in prep top unit holding above 41F. All items were in the 50Fs. CDI- All items discarded. Unit is not holding above 41F. This is the 6th consecutive time facility has been out on cold holding. EHS highly recommends completing a risk control plan. EHS left copy of the risk control plan. ITS issued. Facility has not keep checking temps and getting corrective actions. PIC knew that the unit was not holding above 41F and had called to get the unit serviced. Unit has been used for day while non-functional. 3 DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed sliced turkey and marinara without date. Observed pico dated 1/27 (in prep top unit and low reach in) and egg rolls dated 1/17. CDI- All items discarded



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed grill employee without hair net. CDI- Employee put hair net on.

violations.comments: 4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed cleaned dishes stored at 3 comp sink where soiled dishes area sprayed. Area in exposed to splatter. Relocate to new area or clean dishes in another area.

violations.comments: 4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed spatula bent. Observed several lids heavily cracked/ broken throughout. Observed container with burn damage.

violations.comments: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P Observed employee relocate nail clippers when go back to food preparation without a handwashing. CDI: PIC instructed employee to wash hands.

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed handling blocked by garbage can. CDI: PIC relocated garbage can to make sink accessible.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw lobster tail being stored over unwashed potatoes in WIC. CDI: PIC rearranged storage order.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed multiple garlic sauces below 135F in hot holding equipment. PIC stated that the equipment was not turned on and items were placed in HH ann hour prior. REPEAT CDI: Items reheated to 165F and equipment turned on.

rows.general_comments:
NOTE: EHS observed cooked items cooling in tall reach in units (Paneer, eggs). Observed several other foods cooked and cooled in the facility that were not agreed upon by EHS and PIC. Facility agreed to only cook and cool onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb. EHS and PIC agreed to adding rice to the cooling list. EHS observed eggs, rice, chicken, paneer, goat soup, dal, potatoes, veg mix among other items cooked and cooled. EHS found several issues with cooling today. Cooked items were cooled in the tall reach in unit. Several other items were cooling with lids on and wrapped in the tall reach in. NO temperatures were taken throughout the cooling period. The facility places the items in the tall reach ins to cool, then leaves them until needed. This process is not approved. Facility will be restricted to cooking and cooling (rice, onion sauce, tomato sauce, lamb and goat). 
If EHS finds other items cooked and cooled without prior approval an INTENT TO SUSPEND WILL BE ISSUED. 

NOTE: Disinfectant wipes used for prep tables do not specify that they are food safe. Directed use does not include food contact surfaces. Use bleach sanitizer or food safe sanitizer for cleaning. Kitchen.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed violations pertaining to reheating, hot holding, date marking, storage order, clean dishes, cross contamination among other things.

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed employee drinks stored throughout the kitchen area.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw shrimp stored over ready to eat items in the low reach in unit. Observed Opened raw chicken stored above opened beef in tall reach in freezer. Freezer was at 44F. Items were thawed. Raw chicken/ meat juice covered over the bottom of the cooler. CDI- Items relocated to proper order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed cleaned dishes splattered with raw chicken juice. Observed robo coupe stored clean, soiled with old food debris. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
violations.comments: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed pre-cooked rice reheated to 120F. CDI- Reheated to 165F.

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed food cooling above 41F after 4 hours of cooling. CDI- Discarded


For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed eggs, chick peas, potato soup and onion/ tomato sauce mix holding below 135F in hot well. CDI- All items reheated to 165F.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed coconut milk and unsalted butter in prep top unit holding above 41F. CDI- Coconut milk discarded. Butter placed in low reach in. Pts de-escalated based on improvement. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed goat soup, dal and beans without date marking. CDI- Items dated. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed several food items cooling with lids on. Cdi- Removed. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

4-301.11 Provide equipment in number and capacity so that cooling, heating, and holding temperatures are achieved. -Pf Observed facility without enough equipment to sufficiently and safely cool items. EHS reiterated to PIC during the last visit that facility is limited to cooling to 4 items (onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb). EHS observed cooked and cooled vegetables, dal, goat soup, cauliflower, paneer, rice, biryani, marinated chicken, chicken biryani among other things. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed chicken thawing in prep sink without running water. CDI- Water ran on chicken.
3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed several spice containers without labels.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed live roaches in kitchen area. EHS reviewed most recent pest control log. Pest control came out 2/24. Continue use pest control services, clean frequently and keep food covered.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-304.13 Linens and napkins may not be used in contact with food unless used to line a container for the service of foods and are replaced each time the container is refilled for a new customer. Observed napkins stored in container of food in tall reach in.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean dishes stored at prep sink with raw chicken. Observed clean knives stored on soiled magnet strip. CDI- All items taken to be cleaned. 

4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed several dishes wet stacked in clean dish storage area.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed tall reach in freezer gasket heavily damaged. Observed broken handle on rice cooker. Repair/ replace

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tall reach in units, squeeze bottles in need of cleaning. Pts not escalated due to improvement

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floors in need of cleaning, under and around equipment. Pts de-escalated based on improvement. Observed wall behind 3 comp in need of cleaning.
