rows.general_comments:
PIC stated that oysters are no longer offered. 

No consumer advisory provided on breakfast menu but is supplied on regular menu which is always given in conjunction with breakfast menu. 

RCP will be implemented for date marking. 

For re-inspection, please call 980-314-1620.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf- Observed multiple risk factor violations during inspection. **10 DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED BY 2/1/2026** -1-
A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure. -Pf- Observed no signed attestation of EHP. -CDI- EHS provided copy and it was signed by all employees at time of visit. -0-
For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events
violations.comments:
2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf- Observed no V&D cleanup procedures. -CDI- EHS provided copy. -0-
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. Observed no handwashing sign at handsink at BOH near dishpit. -CDI- EHS provided signage. -0-

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P- Observed white and black microbial growth on jalapenos, ketchup and boiled eggs in WIC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items. -1-

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed bag of raw chorizo stored in container on top of bag of cooked chicken. Observed raw chorizo stored in container with ranch and queso in prep unit. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed multiple items in RIFs, RIC, and WIC with no protective covering. **REPEAT** -3-
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over washed RTE tomatoes in WIC. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris on dishes stored as clean. Per PIC, some items not in use. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. Items not in use must be stored clean or removed from facility. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code. -P- Observed in-use utensils that per PIC, had been in-use since facility opened at 9am (time of observation was at 2p). -CDI- EHS educated PIC on cleaning frequency and items were placed at dishpit to be W/R/S. 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure. Observed microwave in need of cleaning due to food splash. Per PIC, microwave is cleaned every 2 days. Ensure microwave is being cleaned daily.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed crema in prep unit #1 above 41F. Observed items in prep unit #2 on right side of unit above 41F. Observed items in WIC above 41F. Ambient air of WIC at 40-41F. However, most foods are not maintaining temperatures at 41F or below. Refer to temperature chart. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items above 41F. **VERIFICATION REQUIRED BY 1/23/26**
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed items missing date marks: cooked frozen beef, chicken, hot pickled onions, shredded mozzarella cheese, queso fresco, sauce containing mayo and whole milk, sliced tomatoes, cut cabbage, salad mix, red sauce, salsa verde, chip salsa, flautas, housemade drink containing milk, and carnitas. Observed items past date mark: cut cabbage 12/29 (discard 1/4), and queso 12/25 & 12/28 (discard 12/31 & 1/3). Observed items improperly date marked: corn, carrots and cut cabbage 1/18-1/26 (discard should be 1/24), and beans and seafood soup 1/13-1/23 (discard should be 1/19). Per PIC, items with no date mark were prepared 1/19. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. **REPEAT** -3- **RCP BEING IMPLEMENTED FOR DATE MARKING.**
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed multiple squeeze bottles with no labels. **REPEAT** -2-

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. Observed back door not fully closed. Ensure door is closed to protect establishment from insect or rodent entry. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed dead pest in cold pan in prep unit. Observed flying pest in RIC being used for storage.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed cokes and waters stored on floor in dry storage area. -0-

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed in-use utensils being held in water at 80F. -0-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacking with dishes stored as clean. -0-
4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean tongs stored on hook where spray nozzle to rinse dirty dishes is stored.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed to go plates stored on floor in dry storage area. -0-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed cutting boards throughout no longer smooth and easily cleanable. Resurface or replace. 
4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed grocery bags being used on raw fish and other items. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rust buildup on bottom shelves of 2 prep tables. Observed rusted shelving throughout in need of replacement. Observed shelving used to store clean dishes that had been spray painted. **REPEAT** -1-
4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed utensils with damaged/warped handles. 
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed broken door handle gasket at RIF near WIC. Observed door on prep unit #1 not properly closing. 
4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent. Observed nutri bullet that read “for household use only”.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tops and sides of equipment in need of cleaning throughout, especially cooking equipment and shelving in need of cleaning throughout. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. -0-

violations.description: Hot & cold water available; adequate pressure
violations.comments: 5-103.12 Provide water under pressure to all fixtures. Observed no water coming out at handsink by cook line. -CDI- PIC repaired. -0-

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed cracked and lifting floor tiles near the back door. Also observed multiple missing baseboards throughout the facility (Kitchen, back storage room, and WIC). Observed ceiling tile above RIC in need of replacement due to hole.
6-501.114 (A) The premises shall be free of items that are unnecessary to the operation or maintenance of the establishment such as equipment that is nonfunctional or no longer used. Observed broken RI unit and lowboy being used for storage. Observed plates and some cold pans that per PIC were no longer in use. Remove any items no longer in use. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed debris buildup on tops of paper towel dispensers. Observed food debris and water on floors. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed most lights with no light shields and one light shield hanging down, not fully intact. Replace.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee items stored over retail items in RIC. Observed employee coffee machine stored on top of table next to small cold holding unit where sauces are held. Observed bible stored on shelving above prep unit with other retail items. **REPEAT** -0.5-
6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity. Observed little to no lighting at prep units and at cook/grill line.

rows.violation_text:
1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf- Observed multiple risk factor violations during inspection. **10 DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED BY 2/1/2026** -1-
A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety

3 - Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting: 2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure. -Pf- Observed no signed attestation of EHP. -CDI- EHS provided copy and it was signed by all employees at time of visit. -0-
For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5 - Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events: 2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf- Observed no V&D cleanup procedures. -CDI- EHS provided copy. -0-
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. Observed no handwashing sign at handsink at BOH near dishpit. -CDI- EHS provided signage. -0-

13 - Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P- Observed white and black microbial growth on jalapenos, ketchup and boiled eggs in WIC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items. -1-

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed bag of raw chorizo stored in container on top of bag of cooked chicken. Observed raw chorizo stored in container with ranch and queso in prep unit. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed multiple items in RIFs, RIC, and WIC with no protective covering. **REPEAT** -3-
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over washed RTE tomatoes in WIC. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris on dishes stored as clean. Per PIC, some items not in use. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. Items not in use must be stored clean or removed from facility. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code. -P- Observed in-use utensils that per PIC, had been in-use since facility opened at 9am (time of observation was at 2p). -CDI- EHS educated PIC on cleaning frequency and items were placed at dishpit to be W/R/S. 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure. Observed microwave in need of cleaning due to food splash. Per PIC, microwave is cleaned every 2 days. Ensure microwave is being cleaned daily.

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed crema in prep unit #1 above 41F. Observed items in prep unit #2 on right side of unit above 41F. Observed items in WIC above 41F. Ambient air of WIC at 40-41F. However, most foods are not maintaining temperatures at 41F or below. Refer to temperature chart. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items above 41F. **VERIFICATION REQUIRED BY 1/23/26**
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed items missing date marks: cooked frozen beef, chicken, hot pickled onions, shredded mozzarella cheese, queso fresco, sauce containing mayo and whole milk, sliced tomatoes, cut cabbage, salad mix, red sauce, salsa verde, chip salsa, flautas, housemade drink containing milk, and carnitas. Observed items past date mark: cut cabbage 12/29 (discard 1/4), and queso 12/25 & 12/28 (discard 12/31 & 1/3). Observed items improperly date marked: corn, carrots and cut cabbage 1/18-1/26 (discard should be 1/24), and beans and seafood soup 1/13-1/23 (discard should be 1/19). Per PIC, items with no date mark were prepared 1/19. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. **REPEAT** -3- **RCP BEING IMPLEMENTED FOR DATE MARKING.**
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

37 - Food properly labeled: original container: 3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed multiple squeeze bottles with no labels. **REPEAT** -2-

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. Observed back door not fully closed. Ensure door is closed to protect establishment from insect or rodent entry. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed dead pest in cold pan in prep unit. Observed flying pest in RIC being used for storage.

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed cokes and waters stored on floor in dry storage area. -0-

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed in-use utensils being held in water at 80F. -0-

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacking with dishes stored as clean. -0-
4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean tongs stored on hook where spray nozzle to rinse dirty dishes is stored.

45 - Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed to go plates stored on floor in dry storage area. -0-

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed cutting boards throughout no longer smooth and easily cleanable. Resurface or replace. 
4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed grocery bags being used on raw fish and other items. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rust buildup on bottom shelves of 2 prep tables. Observed rusted shelving throughout in need of replacement. Observed shelving used to store clean dishes that had been spray painted. **REPEAT** -1-
4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed utensils with damaged/warped handles. 
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed broken door handle gasket at RIF near WIC. Observed door on prep unit #1 not properly closing. 
4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent. Observed nutri bullet that read “for household use only”.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tops and sides of equipment in need of cleaning throughout, especially cooking equipment and shelving in need of cleaning throughout. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. -0-

50 - Hot & cold water available; adequate pressure: 5-103.12 Provide water under pressure to all fixtures. Observed no water coming out at handsink by cook line. -CDI- PIC repaired. -0-

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed cracked and lifting floor tiles near the back door. Also observed multiple missing baseboards throughout the facility (Kitchen, back storage room, and WIC). Observed ceiling tile above RIC in need of replacement due to hole.
6-501.114 (A) The premises shall be free of items that are unnecessary to the operation or maintenance of the establishment such as equipment that is nonfunctional or no longer used. Observed broken RI unit and lowboy being used for storage. Observed plates and some cold pans that per PIC were no longer in use. Remove any items no longer in use. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed debris buildup on tops of paper towel dispensers. Observed food debris and water on floors. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed most lights with no light shields and one light shield hanging down, not fully intact. Replace.

56 - Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee items stored over retail items in RIC. Observed employee coffee machine stored on top of table next to small cold holding unit where sauces are held. Observed bible stored on shelving above prep unit with other retail items. **REPEAT** -0.5-
6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity. Observed little to no lighting at prep units and at cook/grill line.

rows.general_comments:
Buffet is no longer operating as of November 2025. 


Facility may contact the office at 980-314-1620 for re-inspection.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. - Pf. Observed the following priority and priority foundation items out of compliance during the inspection: Improper hand washing practices, inconsistent date marking procedures, improper holding temperatures, improper food storage order, malfunctioning sanitary sewage system and toxic chemical storage. Several of the above mentioned were repeat risk factors from previous inspection(s). CDI - Employees present were educated on critical items; please see item numbers below for more details and corrective actions. -1-

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC
Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. No CFPM present during today’s inspection. CFPM arrived at end of inspection. -1- 

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety.

2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. - P. Observed employee enter kitchen, attempt to repair kitchen hand sink drainage by submerging hand inside the waste water using a screw driver (trying to clear out debris). Employee then transitioned in to food prep without hand washing. EHS observed another employee who also engaged with assessing the hand sink. The second employee’s hands made contact with waste water and debris as he was forced to drain the sink by detaching and cleaning the P-trap. Employee re-assembled plumbing and immediately transitioned into cleaning the food prep table. CDI - Employees were coached on when to wash and washed hands at bar hand sink.

2-301.15 Only wash hands in handwashing sink. - Pf. Discussion with employees present revealed that they utilize the prep sink adjacent to the hand sink to wash hands. CDI - Employees educated on where to wash hands. -2-

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. - P. Observed raw shelled eggs stored over cooked biryani masala in prep reach-in unit and raw chicken stored over cooked chicken in the walk-in cooler. No physical evidence of contamination observed. CDI - Storage order corrected to prevent cross-contamination. -1.5-

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation.

REPEAT: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41 F or less. - P. Observed multiple TCS applicable foods inside large prep top and held out in ambient temperatures temping above 41 F. Refer to temperature observation chart. From discussion with employee food were placed into cold holding 1.5-2 hours prior to EHS arrival. CDI - All foods transferred to the walk-in cooler/reach-in freezer for rapid cooling towards < 41 F. -3-

***Facility has had two consecutive repeat violations with full points for non-compliance. Next repeat will implement a risk control plan.***

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ.

REPEAT: 3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. - P. The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed paneer prepared on 12/28/25 with incorrect date label of 12/21/25. Observed cooked chicken tikka and cooked noodles without date label which had been made on 12/30/25 & 12/31/25, respectively. CDI - EHS allowed employee to correct and add labels. -1.5-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. - P. Observed air freshener spray stored above food service equipment on shelving at server galley, 2 pack heavy duty degreaser stored above single service spoons and containers on shelving in basement, liquid lighter stored on packaged containers of salt, soap and hand sanitizer gel stored on shelf above prep table in basement. CDI - All toxic chemicals were removed to prevent contamination. -1-

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. Observed back door being propped open with a stanchion. Keep closed or install curtain, mesh screen door or fly fans. -0-

REPEAT: 2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed all food handlers preparing foods without a hair restraint. -1-

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed employee use soiled wiping cloth to remove debris from prep table with no means to sanitize food contact surfaces. -0.5-

REPEAT: 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed small containers used as scooper submerged inside raita sauces in prep top and upright reach-in unit. -0.5-

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed several boxes of single service to-go containers, cups and deli soup cups stored on the floor in the basement. -0-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: REPEAT: 4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed large prep unit with a heavily scored, deep cuts and stained cutting board. Resurface or replace the cutting board. -1-
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Water leak observed at both upstairs prep sink and 3-compartment sink fixture. Repair.

5-402.13 Maintain sanitary sewage system according to Law. - P. Observed waste water from hand sink in upstairs kitchen unable to drain. Employees present attempted to repair drainage but were unsuccessful. PIC notified by employee and arrived towards end of inspection. PIC advised that emergency request for repairs will be made. Additional hand washing sink is available at bar station outside of kitchen and hand washing sink available in basement. Facility shall only operate in small kitchen upstairs using foods in ready to eat form. 1- 

***Verification visit is required by no later than 1/5/25.

rows.general_comments:
Facility has been marking for hot holding procedures on previous inspection reports. Today, facility was marked for reheating for hot holding procedures. Facility shall establish an SOP for reheating and hot holding foods. 

For inspection call the main office (980) 314-1620 
EHS has 10 business days to respond to the request for a reinspection after facility as called main office.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments:
2-401.11 (A) Employees shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and other items needing protection can not result. Observed plate with a half eaten piece of pizza on prep unit while employees was prepping tomatoes. CDI- Item was discarded. 
2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed employee beverage stored on prep unit while active prep was occurring. -0-

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P Observed employee wash hands for less than 20 seconds. Employee rinsed hands in 3-compartment sink and did not use soap and dried hands on apron. CDI- Employee was instructed on proper handwashing methods and completed a correct handwash. -2-
2-301.15 Only wash hands in handwashing sink. -PF Observed employee wash hands in 3-compartment sink. CDI- Employee was instructed on proper handwashing methods and completed a correct handwash. -2-
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P Employee washed hands and used bare hands to turn off faucet. CDI- Employee was instructed on proper handwashing methods and completed a correct handwash.-2-

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P Observed employee prepping tomatoes (will not go through a cooking process) with barehands. CDI- Tomates were voluntarily discarded and uncut tomatoes were rewashed. -2-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:
3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P Observed a bag containing several visible spoiled heads of lettuce sitting in the spoiled vegetable juice in walk in cooler. CDI- Heads of lettuce were voluntarily discarded. 
3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. Pf Observed a few cans in dry storage area that were visibly dented along the seams. Cans were not segregated and stored with other dry goods. CDI- Items were segregated in dry storage area for reimbursement. -1-

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed a very large amount of dishes in clean dish storage with sticker residue present. Dishes were stacked so that sticker residue is touching the food contact surface. CDI- Items were taken to dish area to be washed/rinsed/sanitized. **REPEAT** 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
violations.comments: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed marinara sauce in steam well at 115F. When asked about product PIC stated that it was hot holding. PIC stated that they reheat items inside the steam well - marinara sauce was actually reheating and had been reheating for the past 1.5 hours. PIC was unaware of what temperature product should be at and that they product was reheating. CDI- EHS advised that facility should work to rapidly reheat the food and suggested heating it on the stove. PIC instructed food employee to reheat the sauce on the stove. Marinara sauce reached 175F and was placed back into steam well. -1.5- **See comments**

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed facility holding garlic butter (fresh garlic and margarine) on counter at 70F. CDI- Product was voluntarily discarded and facility is going to hold butter on TPHC moving forward. -0- 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed cooked chicken in lowboy with a prep date of 12/10, observed cooked wings with no date mark in lowboy unit, observed cooked wings in walk in cooler with date mark of 1/2 when PIC stated items were prepped the day prior, pasta salad with a prep date of 12/17, bag of sliced deli meat with 12/24 prep date, half and half with date mark, and cut lettuce (salads) with no date marks. CDI- All items past the 7-day allotment for date marking were voluntarily discarded. EHS allowed PIC to date half and half and it was opened the day prior and EHS allowed PIC to correct date mark on cooked chicken wings. **REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-207.11 (B) Store labeled, employee medications to prevent contamination of food, equipment, and other food service related items. -P Observed medication (Insulin) stored above food in the walk in cooler. CDI- Item was relocated to a lower shelf away from facility food. -1-

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed multiple containers throughout facility without labels. Anytime a product is removed from its original container it should be labeled. -0-

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed all employees in the kitchen prepping and handling food without the use of a hair restraint. All employees with beards should wear a bear guard regardless of length. -0.5-

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. Observed scoop with handle stored inside sugar and a scoop without a handle stored in sugar. Scoops shall be stored to keep handles out of product

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed rust accumulating on bottom prep surface of prep table and on dry storage racks throughout the kitchen. Repair/Replace. **REPEAT**

rows.general_comments: For a reinspection call 704-432-1620. Reinspections occur within 10 business days of request. Verification visit will occur next week for all items checked VR.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed multiple handwashing and cooling violations. PIC did not seem to know the parameters or corrections. Staff need retraining in food safety and employee health. 


A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P Observed employees washing hands for far less than 20 seconds and without using a barrier to turn off the faucet. Also observed an employee not washing between dirty and clean dishes at the dish area. Another employee changed gloves but did not wash hands when switching from raw to ready to eat food. Also front of the house employees would come in and put on gloves to grab chips and other items without washing their hands. CDI- handwashing was demonstrated to several employees and more hand washing started occurring. 



For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed one of the kitchen hand sinks with towels and the knife sharpener in it. CDI - removed.



6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf. No soap at the bar hand sinks. Provided. CDI. No paper towels at the bar hand sink. CDI. Provided. 
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf
3-201.11(A) Use food from approved sources only.-P. Observed there were no invoices on site and fish had not boxes or other ways to determine if it was from an approved source. VR>.

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments: 3-402.11 and 3-402.12 Provide records from supplier to guarantee fish served raw or undercooked is free of live parasites. -Pf. Observed no parasite destruction letters for Ceviche on the menu. Manager says it is cooked in lemon not with heat. Return visit required. Facility must stop serving Ceviche until letters are provided.

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. - 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 

Observed raw beef over sauce, raw chicken over beef, raw chicken over vegetables, and other instances of improper storage of raw meat with other meats and vegetables. VR


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-602.11 (A) (1) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, before each use with a different type of raw animal food. P Observed the same utensils were being used for different types of raw animal foods as well as cooked foods and all being put back in the same water container. VR


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
violations.comments: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P. Observed 20 pans of TCS food in the two hot holding units of items such as pork, beef, cooked beans, sauces, cooked vegetables which had been there since 7:30 per PIC and at 11:15 had still not reached 165F. Items were between 90-140F. CDI - all were voluntarily discarded.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed beans, roja sauce, and pork did not make it to 70F within 2 hours. At the 2 hour mark they were reheated to 165F to be recooked. CDI. 



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed soup in the walk in dated 12/30/2025. Observed items on the line such as pico which had been prepared on previous days were not date marked. CDI - discarded or dated depending on items. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P. Observed raw vegetables held on time were not makes in any way. Procedures were posted on the board but not being followed. Only one item was on time at time of inspection. VR.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-101.11 Containers of poisonous or toxic materials and personal care items shall bear a legible manufacturer's label. - Pf. Observed spray bottles of chemicals had labels added after the inspection started. CDI.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed ice baths on the counters of large pans and containers of beans (3), pork (2) and sauce (1) were all not cooling at a sufficient rate. With intervention of stirring half of the containers made it to 70f at the 2 hour mark, but the other 3 had to be reheated to start over in the cooling process. CDI. - intervention, reheating and education. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed containers of rice on the floor of the dry storage room. Observed the walk in cooler had food stored under the shelves.

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Observed kitchen staff with bracelets on. Observed employees working with food without hair restraints.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed wet wiping clothes not held in sanitizer between uses. They were laying on prep surfaces.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Rice scoop was under 135F.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-901.11(A) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils shall not be used until air dried or adequately drained before contact with food. Observed wet stacks pans.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed rusty shelves throughout the facility. Observed one was spray painted with silver paint. This is not a proper way to refinish shelving.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed shelving in need of cleaning throughout.
5-402.13. Observed wastewater had been dumped around the back near the can wash but not in the can wash. VR.

violations.description: Toilet facilities: properly constructed, supplied & cleaned
violations.comments: 5-501.17 Provide a covered waste bin in female restrooms. Observed no covered waste bin in female restroom. Provide covered waste bin as needed. Repeat violation.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments:
5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed dumpster doors open. 
5-501.16(A) Provide receptacles of sufficient capacity to hold refuse, recyclables, and returnables that accumulate. Observed trash around the dumpster. Ensure sufficient capacity.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed general cleaning needed of floors and walls.

20 - Proper cooling time & temperatures: 3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed beans, roja sauce, and pork did not make it to 70F within 2 hours. At the 2 hour mark they were reheated to 165F to be recooked. CDI. 



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed soup in the walk in dated 12/30/2025. Observed items on the line such as pico which had been prepared on previous days were not date marked. CDI - discarded or dated depending on items. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

24 - Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P. Observed raw vegetables held on time were not makes in any way. Procedures were posted on the board but not being followed. Only one item was on time at time of inspection. VR.

28 - Toxic substances properly identified stored & used: 7-101.11 Containers of poisonous or toxic materials and personal care items shall bear a legible manufacturer's label. - Pf. Observed spray bottles of chemicals had labels added after the inspection started. CDI.

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed ice baths on the counters of large pans and containers of beans (3), pork (2) and sauce (1) were all not cooling at a sufficient rate. With intervention of stirring half of the containers made it to 70f at the 2 hour mark, but the other 3 had to be reheated to start over in the cooling process. CDI. - intervention, reheating and education. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed containers of rice on the floor of the dry storage room. Observed the walk in cooler had food stored under the shelves.

40 - Personal cleanliness: 2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Observed kitchen staff with bracelets on. Observed employees working with food without hair restraints.

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed wet wiping clothes not held in sanitizer between uses. They were laying on prep surfaces.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Rice scoop was under 135F.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-901.11(A) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils shall not be used until air dried or adequately drained before contact with food. Observed wet stacks pans.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed rusty shelves throughout the facility. Observed one was spray painted with silver paint. This is not a proper way to refinish shelving.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed shelving in need of cleaning throughout.

52 - Sewage & wastewater properly disposed: 5-402.13. Observed wastewater had been dumped around the back near the can wash but not in the can wash. VR.

53 - Toilet facilities: properly constructed, supplied & cleaned: 5-501.17 Provide a covered waste bin in female restrooms. Observed no covered waste bin in female restroom. Provide covered waste bin as needed. Repeat violation.

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed dumpster doors open. 
5-501.16(A) Provide receptacles of sufficient capacity to hold refuse, recyclables, and returnables that accumulate. Observed trash around the dumpster. Ensure sufficient capacity.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed general cleaning needed of floors and walls.
